entry.title: [BE] Indigenous Friends of Israel

entry.title: [DDP] Is Israel a Jewish Supremacist State? Rabbi Haim, Atzmon & Marouf Debate Zionism
entry.summary:
Is Israel a Jewish supremacist state—or is the central issue Zionism, identity and power? Rabbi Hayem Sohra, Gilad Atzmon and Laith Marouf debate Palestine, Jewish anti-Zionism and the future of the region.
In this episode of Deep Dive Perspective, three guests examine the relationship between Zionism, Judaism, Jewish identity and the Israeli state.
Rabbi Hayem Sohra discusses his personal history, Palestinian nationalism, the Nakba, the right of return and his opposition to Zionism.
Gilad Atzmon argues that Benjamin Netanyahu is a symptom of a deeper crisis in the construction of modern Jewish identity. He also questions whether Jewish anti-Zionism, without material sacrifice or direct political action, can meaningfully assist Palestinian liberation.
Laith Marouf challenges what he sees as the limits of symbolic solidarity. He argues that Palestinians and the wider region must ultimately determine their own path to liberation, while questioning whether anti-Zionist Jewish activism is sufficiently committed to that goal.
The discussion also examines the historical origins of the conflict, the role of British power, the meaning of Jewish supremacy, the future of Israel, the Palestinian right of return and competing visions of solidarity and liberation.
In This Episode:
Is Israel a Jewish supremacist state or a Zionist state? The difference between Zionism, Judaism and Jewish identity The historical roots of the Palestinian struggle Rabbi Hayem Sohra’s personal opposition to Zionism The Nakba, land confiscation and the Palestinian right of return Whether Jewish anti-Zionism is helping or hindering Palestine Gilad Atzmon’s critique of symbolic solidarity Laith Marouf’s argument for Palestinian self-liberation The future of Israel and the wider regional conflict The role of Britain and Western power in supporting Israel
Guest Information: Rabbi Hayem Sohra Jewish anti-Zionist activist discussing Palestinian nationalism, the right of return, Zionism and historical memory.
Gilad Atzmon Artist, musician and philosopher offering a critique of Zionism, Jewish identity and the limits of contemporary anti-Zionist activism.
Laith Marouf Journalist and war correspondent discussing Israel, Palestine, regional resistance and the principle of Palestinian self-liberation.
⏱️ Timestamps: 00:00 Introduction: Is Israel a Jewish supremacist or Zionist state? 00:02 The conflict as an existential struggle 00:06 Rabbi Hayem Sohra on history, exile and Palestine 00:13 The debate over Jewish anti-Zionist action 00:20 Rabbi Hayem responds: solidarity, protection and activism 00:29 The case for more direct political action 00:31 Is Jewish anti-Zionism helping or hindering liberation? 00:36 Israel’s future and competing visions of liberation 00:43 Gilad Atzmon’s closing argument 00:44 Laith Marouf on sacrifice and Palestinian self-liberation 00:48 The host’s conclusion: strategy, solidarity and Zionism’s future 00:54 Final remarks and guest thanks
#Zionism #IsraelPalestine #AntiZionism #Palestine #JewishIdentity #MiddleEastPolitics #Geopolitics #DeepDivePerspective

The views and opinions expressed in this video are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of Deep Dive Perspective. Deep Dive Perspective assumes no responsibility or liability for any errors or omissions in the content. All information is provided on an as-is basis. Viewer discretion is advised.
entry.content:
Is Israel a Jewish supremacist state—or is the central issue Zionism, identity and power? Rabbi Hayem Sohra, Gilad Atzmon and Laith Marouf debate Palestine, Jewish anti-Zionism and the future of the region.

In this episode of Deep Dive Perspective, three guests examine the relationship between Zionism, Judaism, Jewish identity and the Israeli state.

Rabbi Hayem Sohra discusses his personal history, Palestinian nationalism, the Nakba, the right of return and his opposition to Zionism.

Gilad Atzmon argues that Benjamin Netanyahu is a symptom of a deeper crisis in the construction of modern Jewish identity. He also questions whether Jewish anti-Zionism, without material sacrifice or direct political action, can meaningfully assist Palestinian liberation.

Laith Marouf challenges what he sees as the limits of symbolic solidarity. He argues that Palestinians and the wider region must ultimately determine their own path to liberation, while questioning whether anti-Zionist Jewish activism is sufficiently committed to that goal.

The discussion also examines the historical origins of the conflict, the role of British power, the meaning of Jewish supremacy, the future of Israel, the Palestinian right of return and competing visions of solidarity and liberation.


In This Episode:

Is Israel a Jewish supremacist state or a Zionist state?
The difference between Zionism, Judaism and Jewish identity
The historical roots of the Palestinian struggle
Rabbi Hayem Sohra’s personal opposition to Zionism
The Nakba, land confiscation and the Palestinian right of return
Whether Jewish anti-Zionism is helping or hindering Palestine
Gilad Atzmon’s critique of symbolic solidarity
Laith Marouf’s argument for Palestinian self-liberation
The future of Israel and the wider regional conflict
The role of Britain and Western power in supporting Israel

Guest Information:
Rabbi Hayem Sohra
Jewish anti-Zionist activist discussing Palestinian nationalism, the right of return, Zionism and historical memory.

Gilad Atzmon
Artist, musician and philosopher offering a critique of Zionism, Jewish identity and the limits of contemporary anti-Zionist activism.

Laith Marouf
Journalist and war correspondent discussing Israel, Palestine, regional resistance and the principle of Palestinian self-liberation.

entry.title: [MR] INSIDE TEHRAN: CIVILIAN SITES HIT IN U.S. & ISRAELI STRIKES | ON THE GROUND
entry.summary:
This report documents visits to several locations in Tehran that the video identifies as having been bombed during the recent U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran. The footage includes a damaged bridge, the Azadi Sports Complex, the Iranian Space Center, Gandhi Hospital, and a Jewish synagogue. At each location, the video examines the visible damage, presents the available evidence discussed in the report, and explores why these sites became part of the conflict.
Rather than relying solely on news coverage, this report takes viewers directly to the locations, documenting the aftermath on the ground and examining the impact on public infrastructure and civilian sites across Tehran. The video also discusses the evidence presented for each location and the broader implications of the strikes. Subscribe for more exclusive reporting, interviews, and on-the-ground coverage from across Iran, including Tehran, Mashhad, and Qom.
#Iran #Tehran #IranNews #WorldNews #BreakingNews #US #Israel #MiddleEast #Geopolitics #OnTheGround #FieldReport #IndependentJournalism #CurrentEvents #Tehran #IranConflict

entry.title: [GZ] The US and Iran "fighting for final, ultimate victory" - top Iranian analyst
entry.summary:
The Grayzone's Max Blumenthal meets Mostafa Khoshcheshm, one of Iran's most connected and prolific analysts and journalists, to discuss the next phase of the US-Israeli war on the country, and why he thinks Iran will emerge as a regional power.
Khoshcheshm explains how the current phase of conflict will likely escalate into a full blown regional war, and why he believes that Israel is already on its way toward an internal political implosion.
This is part two of a two part interview. Watch part one here: https://youtu.be/k6mhX6QVoCM?si=aBXWjixhmrR5Yulj

entry.title: [RTHINK] Iranian Special Forces cross into Iraq, and prepare to take over US bases in the Gulf! #iran #war
entry.summary:
During the recent escalation with the United States, Iran deployed new missiles and hybrid warheads, striking American bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan. CENTCOM has confirmed four U.S. soldiers dead, one missing, and fifteen wounded in the latest exchange of fire.
Iran also hit King Hussein International Airport in Aqaba, Jordan, destroying several U.S. aircraft. In Kuwait, Iranian forces struck desalination and electricity facilities in retaliation for U.S. strikes on Iranian territory.
Meanwhile, Iran's 65th NOHED Airborne Special Forces Brigade crossed the border into Iraqi Kurdistan, targeting leaders of U.S.- and Israeli-backed Kurdish groups. According to an Iranian journalist, Tehran is now considering using ground troops to seize U.S. bases in Kuwait.
Find out more about the latest developments in the Middle East by watching this episode of Radical Thinker!
If you enjoy my content and want to support me, you can do it here: patreon.com/RadicalThinkerRT
You can also follow me on X: https://x.com/RadicalThinker5
#MiddleEast #Geopolitics #RadicalThinker #iran #iranwar #trump #usa #kuwait #jordan #iraq #persiangulf #strait of hormuz #aqaba #kurdistan #iranisraelwar #israel
entry.content:
During the recent escalation with the United States, Iran deployed new missiles and hybrid warheads, striking American bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan. CENTCOM has confirmed four U.S. soldiers dead, one missing, and fifteen wounded in the latest exchange of fire.

Iran also hit King Hussein International Airport in Aqaba, Jordan, destroying several U.S. aircraft. In Kuwait, Iranian forces struck desalination and electricity facilities in retaliation for U.S. strikes on Iranian territory.

Meanwhile, Iran's 65th NOHED Airborne Special Forces Brigade crossed the border into Iraqi Kurdistan, targeting leaders of U.S.- and Israeli-backed Kurdish groups. According to an Iranian journalist, Tehran is now considering using ground troops to seize U.S. bases in Kuwait.

Find out more about the latest developments in the Middle East by watching this episode of Radical Thinker!

If you enjoy my content and want to support me, you can do it here: patreon.com/RadicalThinkerRT

You can also follow me on X: https://x.com/RadicalThinker5

entry.title: [EI] How Israel targets the small bodies of Palestinian kids, with Dr. Mimi Syed and Wes Bryant
entry.summary:
In Gaza, Israel killed 13 Palestinians and wounded at least 18 others over a 24-hour period on Tuesday and Wednesday, in a series of drone strikes, shelling and gunfire.
In the pre-dawn hours on Wednesday, Israeli helicopter gunships targeted a home in Deir al-Balah, killing three members of the Abu Qassem family: Omar, 33, his wife Asmaa, 32, and their 6-year-old daughter Habiba.
The killing of children has been a key feature of the ongoing genocide, and the evidence shows it is systematic.
Recently, an independent United Nations Commission of Inquiry concluded that Israel’s deliberate targeting of children in Gaza establishes, in part, its genocidal intent to destroy Palestinian society.
We are joined by Dr. Mimi Syed and Wes Bryant. They are co-authors of a new report, “Lethal Precision Without Accountability: Israeli ‘Quadcopter’ Use in Gaza,” which documents Israel’s patterns of targeting children.
Dr. Syed is an emergency medicine physician who worked in Gaza during Israel’s genocide. Bryant is a Pentagon whistleblower and a war crimes analyst, and served as a special advisor to the United Nations International Commission of Inquiry on Palestine.
This is a segment from The Electronic Intifada's livestream on day 1014 of the Gaza genocide. Ali Abunimah, Nora Barrows-Friedman and Jon Elmer were joined by Dr. Mimi Syed and Wes Bryant. You can watch the full show here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fwS9YbgaPUg
Theme Music by Greg Wilson
Your gift supports The Electronic Intifada's independent journalism on Palestine: • Donate by credit card, ApplePay, PayPal, Venmo or US bank account via Kindful: https://bit.ly/EIKindful
Gifts are welcome from anywhere and are tax-deductible for US taxpayers as allowed by law.

#TheElectronicIntifada #TheElectronicIntifadaPodcast
entry.content:
In Gaza, Israel killed 13 Palestinians and wounded at least 18 others over a 24-hour period on Tuesday and Wednesday, in a series of drone strikes, shelling and gunfire.

In the pre-dawn hours on Wednesday, Israeli helicopter gunships targeted a home in Deir al-Balah, killing three members of the Abu Qassem family: Omar, 33, his wife Asmaa, 32, and their 6-year-old daughter Habiba.

The killing of children has been a key feature of the ongoing genocide, and the evidence shows it is systematic.

Recently, an independent United Nations Commission of Inquiry concluded that Israel’s deliberate targeting of children in Gaza establishes, in part, its genocidal intent to destroy Palestinian society.

We are joined by Dr. Mimi Syed and Wes Bryant. They are co-authors of a new report, “Lethal Precision Without Accountability: Israeli ‘Quadcopter’ Use in Gaza,” which documents Israel’s patterns of targeting children.

Dr. Syed is an emergency medicine physician who worked in Gaza during Israel’s genocide. Bryant is a Pentagon whistleblower and a war crimes analyst, and served as a special advisor to the United Nations International Commission of Inquiry on Palestine.

This is a segment from The Electronic Intifada's livestream on day 1014 of the Gaza genocide. Ali Abunimah, Nora Barrows-Friedman and Jon Elmer were joined by Dr. Mimi Syed and Wes Bryant. You can watch the full show here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fwS9YbgaPUg

entry.title: [BE] Internet Decides Paraguay is Racist - But It Actually Did Reverse Yakub
entry.summary: Everyone decided Paraguay, a country they never heard of, was racist due to the world cup. Here I talk about how stupid the logic of this is, and use the opportunity to tell you about an interesting part of Paraguayan history that might paint a different picture. entry.title: [MR] EXCLUSIVE: THE TEAM BEHIND THE VIRAL AI LEGO VIDEOS SPEAKS
entry.summary:
In this exclusive interview, Sulaiman Ahmed sits down with the founders of Explosive Media, the Tehran-based creative studio behind the AI-generated Lego-style videos that have captured millions of views across social media. Their distinctive animations have become some of the most recognizable AI-generated content online, blending storytelling, satire, and current events into a unique visual style.
During the conversation, the team discusses how Explosive Media was founded, the creative process behind their viral productions, the AI tools and workflows they use, the challenges of producing large-scale animated content, and their vision for the future of AI-powered filmmaking. They also share insights into the rapid evolution of generative AI, the impact of social media on digital storytelling, and what it takes to create videos that resonate with audiences around the world.
If you're interested in artificial intelligence, filmmaking, animation, content creation, or the future of digital media, this interview offers a rare behind-the-scenes look at one of the most talked-about AI creative teams.
#ArtificialIntelligence #AI #GenerativeAI #ExplosiveMedia #LegoAnimation #AIAnimation #Filmmaking #ContentCreation #DigitalMedia #SulaimanAhmed #ExclusiveInterview #ViralVideos #Animation #CreativeAI #AIArt #Tehran #Iran #Technology #FutureOfAI #BehindTheScenes

entry.title: [SYR] CIA’s Secret Armies: The Truth About Proxy Warfare
entry.summary:
Does the CIA use secret armies to shape global politics? In this documentary, Syriana Analysis explores the hidden history of proxy warfare and the controversial strategies used to influence conflicts from Afghanistan to the modern-day crisis in Ukraine. We examine the evolution of "contract armies" and the long-term consequences of covert operations.
In this video, we uncover:
- The Origins of Proxy Warfare: How the 1970s conflict in Afghanistan set the stage for decades of covert intervention. - Operation Timber Sycamore: An in-depth look at one of the costliest operations in history and its impact on the Syrian conflict. - The "Contract Army" Theory: Analyzing expert insights from Professor Michael Hudson and Scott Ritter on the use of non-conventional forces. - Geopolitical Consequences: Why these strategies are often shrouded in secrecy and what they mean for the future of international relations.
Featuring archival footage and expert testimony, we piece together a narrative that challenges the official story of global "terror" and examines the geopolitical tools used by major powers.
Timestamps: 0:00 – The Official Story vs. The Hidden History 1:05 – Afghanistan: The Birth of the Proxy Model 3:20 – Hillary Clinton’s Confessions on Funding Groups 5:15 – Are Certain Groups "Contract Armies"? 7:30 – Timber Sycamore: The War for Syria 10:00 – Scott Ritter on the Criminality of Secret Aid 12:15 – Jeffrey Sachs: The Destruction of Syria 15:35 – The Ukraine Connection: A New Front? 18:00 – Final Analysis: Are These Geopolitical Tools?
📌Support Syriana Analysis: If you value independent geopolitical analysis, please consider supporting our work so we can continue bringing you these deep dives:
🛡️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join
⚠️ This documentary is presented for educational, journalistic, and historical analysis. It examines publicly available historical sources, official statements, and archival material. The inclusion of historical footage or quotations does not constitute endorsement of any organization or individual discussed.
📢 Syriana Analysis is an independent platform providing well-researched geopolitical insights, founded by Kevork Almassian. This program provides independent journalistic analysis for educational and documentary purposes, focusing on complex geopolitical shifts in West Asia and globally.
#CIA #ProxyWar #Documentary #SyrianaAnalysis #ForeignPolicy #Geopolitics #TimberSycamore #HistoryExposed

entry.title: [MR] EXCLUSIVE: IRAN'S FOREIGN MINISTRY SPOKESPERSON ESMAEIL BAGHAEI SPEAKS
entry.summary:
In this exclusive interview, Sulaiman Ahmed speaks with Esmaeil Baghaei, spokesperson for the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Islamic Republic of Iran, for an in-depth discussion on Iran's foreign policy and the major geopolitical developments shaping the Middle East. The conversation explores Iran's diplomatic position, regional security, relations with neighboring countries, and Tehran's perspective on recent international events.
The interview also covers Iran's views on the ongoing regional conflicts, the future of diplomacy, relations with Western governments, and the country's role in an increasingly complex geopolitical landscape. Esmaeil Baghaei responds to key questions regarding Iran's foreign policy priorities and shares the official perspective of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs on issues that continue to shape international affairs.
If you enjoy exclusive interviews, geopolitical analysis, and conversations with senior government officials, subscribe for more in-depth interviews and on-the-ground reporting from Iran and across the Middle East.

entry.title: [SYR] Cold War 2.0 #77: Why the Iran War Is Far From Over | Tarik Cyril Amar
entry.summary:
Is the U.S. sleepwalking into a permanent conflict with Iran, or is there a deeper strategic "siege" at play? In episode #77 of Cold War 2.0, Kevork Almassian and historian Tarik Cyril Amar break down the escalating tensions in the Persian Gulf, the leverage of the Strait of Hormuz, and why diplomacy like the JCPOA and recent MOUs may have been destined to fail.
We also dive into Trump’s latest claims of election interference, the role of intelligence services in foreign elections, and a critical look back at Angela Merkel’s legacy—could the war in Ukraine have been avoided with different leadership in 2008?
Join us live every Friday for Cold War 2.0, where we examine the week's biggest geopolitical developments with historical context and strategic analysis.
📅 Every Friday 🕚 11:00 ET | 17:00 CET
📌 If you enjoy independent investigative journalism, consider supporting Kit Klarenberg's work and subscribe to Syriana Analysis for more in-depth geopolitical analysis.

entry.title: [TLorenz] The Govt Is Building Files On All Of Us
entry.summary:
The government is using powerful AI tools to build massive digital dossiers on everyday citizens, and it’s happening with zero oversight.
SUPPORT MY WORK: Buy a paid subscription to my newsletter at https://www.usermag.co Support my work on Patreon: http://patreon.com/taylorlorenz
In this episode of Free Speech Friday, I sat with Matthew Guariglia, Senior Policy Analyst at the Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF), to expose how generative AI has democratized mass surveillance.
From the FBI’s massive physical paper filing hangars of the 20th century to modern AI models that scrape your social media, track your geolocation, and predict your political affiliation in seconds—the barrier to entry for government surveillance has officially collapsed.
We dive deep into Guariglia's recent congressional testimony on the AI security landscape, revealing how the Pentagon is blacklisting tech companies like Anthropic for trying to restrict AI from being used in autonomous weapons and mass surveillance.
In this video, we cover: How AI is automating mass surveillance at an unprecedented scale. The secret government contracts and the battle for unrestricted AI use. Why your social media history, geolocation, and purchases create a permanent record. The historical lessons from Ferguson and the Japanese-American internment that prove data is vulnerability. What you can do to protect your digital rights (hint: it starts with turning off your geolocation).

entry.title: [DDP] Iran Closed the Strait of Hormuz — Marandi Explains Why the US Is Losing
entry.summary:
Iran says the United States violated its agreement. Now the Strait of Hormuz is closed — and Dr. Said Mohammed Marandi explains what happens next. Dr. Said Mohammed Marandi joins Deep Dive Perspective to examine the Iran–US war, the collapse of the memorandum of understanding, and the strategic consequences of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. In Marandi’s account, the United States violated the agreement through renewed sanctions, threats, attacks, and attempts to undermine Iran’s authority in the Strait. He argues that Iran anticipated a renewed confrontation and used the ceasefire period to export oil, import essential supplies, expand underground facilities, and prepare its missile and drone capabilities.
The discussion also examines the depletion of US missile stocks, the difficulty of sustaining a prolonged war, the possibility of a ground offensive, the vulnerability of American bases and Gulf infrastructure, and the potential effect on global energy markets.
Marandi further argues that the conflict has strengthened Iran’s regional position and accelerated wider geopolitical changes, including closer cooperation between Iran, Russia, China, Yemen, Lebanon, and Iraq. He also discusses de-dollarisation, the future of the Middle East, and what he describes as the weakening of US regional hegemony.
These are the views and assessments expressed by the guest during the interview. They are presented for political analysis, public debate, and critical scrutiny.
🕒 Timestamps00:00 Introduction and the central question 01:10 Trump, ego, hubris, and the influence of political interests 02:29 The alleged violations of the Iran memorandum of understanding 03:02 Sanctions, threats, and pressure on Iran 03:20 The Strait of Hormuz clause 03:59 Ships, Iranian naval orders, and the escalation 05:23 The Strait of Hormuz is declared closed 05:37 Trump’s threats and Western political silence 07:07 What is the US trying to achieve? 07:44 US missile depletion and the limits of a prolonged war 08:54 Iran’s underground bases, missiles, and drones 10:09 The strategic consequences of the Oman mistake 11:20 Why Hormuz is central to the conflict 11:55 The possibility of a US ground offensive 12:46 Trump’s threats against Iranian infrastructure 14:02 Iran’s stated response to attacks on critical infrastructure 16:42 Threats against Gulf infrastructure and the risk of wider escalation 19:16 Could the US launch a ground invasion? 22:00 What has the US achieved, according to Marandi? 30:56 Yemen, Saudi Arabia, and Iran’s regional allies 33:25 Iran as a fourth centre of regional power 35:54 De-dollarisation and Turkey’s role 37:51 Closing remarks and the future of the conflict
👤 About Dr. Said Mohammed Marandi Dr. Said Mohammed Marandi is a professor and geopolitical commentator who provides analysis on Iran, US foreign policy, the Middle East, regional security, and the changing international order. In this interview, Marandi discusses the Iran–US conflict, the Strait of Hormuz, military strategy, economic pressure, the Axis of Resistance, and the wider consequences of the war. The views expressed are his own.

entry.content:
Iran says the United States violated its agreement. Now the Strait of Hormuz is closed — and Dr. Said Mohammed Marandi explains what happens next. Dr. Said Mohammed Marandi joins Deep Dive Perspective to examine the Iran–US war, the collapse of the memorandum of understanding, and the strategic consequences of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. In Marandi’s account, the United States violated the agreement through renewed sanctions, threats, attacks, and attempts to undermine Iran’s authority in the Strait. He argues that Iran anticipated a renewed confrontation and used the ceasefire period to export oil, import essential supplies, expand underground facilities, and prepare its missile and drone capabilities.

The discussion also examines the depletion of US missile stocks, the difficulty of sustaining a prolonged war, the possibility of a ground offensive, the vulnerability of American bases and Gulf infrastructure, and the potential effect on global energy markets.

Marandi further argues that the conflict has strengthened Iran’s regional position and accelerated wider geopolitical changes, including closer cooperation between Iran, Russia, China, Yemen, Lebanon, and Iraq. He also discusses de-dollarisation, the future of the Middle East, and what he describes as the weakening of US regional hegemony.

These are the views and assessments expressed by the guest during the interview. They are presented for political analysis, public debate, and critical scrutiny.

02:29 The alleged violations of the Iran memorandum of understanding
03:02 Sanctions, threats, and pressure on Iran

03:59 Ships, Iranian naval orders, and the escalation
05:23 The Strait of Hormuz is declared closed

07:44 US missile depletion and the limits of a prolonged war
08:54 Iran’s underground bases, missiles, and drones

11:55 The possibility of a US ground offensive

16:42 Threats against Gulf infrastructure and the risk of wider escalation
19:16 Could the US launch a ground invasion?
22:00 What has the US achieved, according to Marandi?
30:56 Yemen, Saudi Arabia, and Iran’s regional allies
33:25 Iran as a fourth centre of regional power
35:54 De-dollarisation and Turkey’s role
37:51 Closing remarks and the future of the conflict


👤 About Dr. Said Mohammed Marandi
Dr. Said Mohammed Marandi is a professor and geopolitical commentator who provides analysis on Iran, US foreign policy, the Middle East, regional security, and the changing international order. In this interview, Marandi discusses the Iran–US conflict, the Strait of Hormuz, military strategy, economic pressure, the Axis of Resistance, and the wider consequences of the war. The views expressed are his own.

entry.title: [GZ] 'Strategic entrapment': Dennis Kucinich on disastrous US-Israeli military merger
entry.summary:
Former US Rep. Dennis Kucinich joins The Grayzone's Max Blumenthal to discuss Section 219, a measure insidiously inserted into the NDAA bill which will effectively merge the US and Israeli militaries across areas including AI, cyberwarfare, biotech, missile defense, and defense industrial production.
Kucinch explains why the bill will shatter what's left of American sovereignty and congressional oversight, contaminating the US military with Israeli spy technology, encouraging new levels of domestic repression while essentially forbidding any disentanglement from the strategically calamitous special relationship with Israel.

entry.title: [MR] I WALKED THE STREETS OF IRAN. THIS IS WHAT PEOPLE TOLD ME
entry.summary:
What is everyday life really like in Iran? Beyond the headlines and political debates, this video explores the streets, markets, neighborhoods, and public spaces across several Iranian cities to capture a firsthand look at daily life. Along the way, I speak with ordinary people about the recent war, how it has affected them, and their perspectives on the events shaping the region.
From historic landmarks and busy streets to spontaneous conversations with local residents, this journey offers a closer look at modern Iran through the eyes of the people who live there. Whether discussing current events, daily life, or the future, these candid conversations provide a unique glimpse into a country that is often viewed only through the lens of international news. If you enjoy independent travel, geopolitics, street interviews, and on-the-ground reporting, subscribe for more exclusive coverage from Iran and across the Middle East.

entry.title: [DDP] America Has No Military Option: Ex-CIA Analyst on the Iran Crisis
entry.summary:
The United States provoked a war with Iran it cannot win. That is not a prediction — says former CIA analyst Larry C. Johnson. It is the current reality.
Former CIA analyst and State Department counter-terrorism official Larry C. Johnson returns to Deep Dive Perspective for what may be his most direct assessment yet. In Johnson's analysis, the United States entered a confrontation with Iran without the supply chain, the missile stocks, or the ground force capacity to win it — and is now facing the strategic consequences. Iran, he argues, has spent decades preparing for exactly this moment: investing in ballistic missiles, nuclear-attack-resistant shelters, and — most critically — consolidating control over the Strait of Hormuz, through which 40% of the world's usable oil passes. The result, in Johnson's assessment, is that Iran now holds what he calls "a nuclear option without nuclear weapons." On the US political system, Johnson is equally direct: a Congress he argues is incapable of acting as a check on the executive due to lobby dependency, and a president he says is "living in a delusional world" fed a fixed narrative about Iran that contradicts the intelligence. On the global economic consequences — shortages of helium, LNG, urea, and fertilizer, rising diesel and aviation fuel prices, and an inflationary spike Johnson attributes to genuine physical scarcity rather than monetary policy — he argues the world is witnessing something larger: what he calls "the death throes of the US hegemonic empire." These are Johnson's own analytical positions, presented here for debate and critical scrutiny.
🔍 In This Episode: • Johnson's core thesis: why he argues the US "lacks the supply chain and the military capability" to win a war with Iran • The Strait of Hormuz: 40% of global usable oil — and why Johnson calls Iranian control of it a "nuclear option without nuclear weapons" • Iran's missile stocks, nuclear-resistant underground shelters, and what Johnson says this means for any US ground invasion scenario • Saudi Arabia's role: why Johnson argues it is "committing suicide" by participating in US-led operations against the Houthis • Trump's "delusional world": Johnson's charge that the president is "fed a narrative from Israel" that contradicts the operational reality • Congress and lobby dependency: Johnson's argument that the legislative check on executive war-making has been neutralised — his view, presented for debate • The global economic cascade: helium shortages impacting chip production, fertilizer shortfalls threatening Southern Hemisphere harvests, and why Johnson says this inflation is structural — not monetary • De-dollarization accelerating: China's CIPS system vs SWIFT, countries selling US treasuries and buying gold, BRICS financial architecture
🕒 Timestamps: 00:00 Introduction & Larry Johnson's Core Thesis 02:30 Iran's Ballistic Missiles and Nuclear-Resistant Shelters 05:00 The Strait of Hormuz — Iran Controls 40% of Global Oil 08:00 Saudi Arabia "Committing Suicide" — The Yambu Risk 10:00 Houthis and the Bab al-Mandab Threat 11:00 Trump's "Delusional World" — The Strait Is Not Open 14:00 US Military Deficiencies: Supply Chain and Ground Forces 18:00 Depleted Missile Stocks and the Rare Earth Problem 22:00 Iran's Nuclear Capacity — and the Fatwa Under Pressure 26:00 The China-Russia-Iran Security Treaty (Signed Jan 28) 30:00 US Air Bases Hit — Jordan, Diego Garcia 34:00 Congress and the Lobby Dependency — Johnson's Assessment 38:00 Israel as "Pariah State" — US Losing the Narrative 42:00 De-dollarization: CIPS, BRICS, and the End of the Petrodollar 46:00 "Death Throes of the US Hegemonic Empire" — Johnson's Final Assessment
👤 About Larry C. Johnson: Larry C. Johnson is a former analyst at the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) and former deputy director at the US State Department's Office of Counter Terrorism. He served with the CIA from the 1980s and has since become a prominent independent commentator on US foreign policy, intelligence analysis, and West Asian geopolitics. He is a frequent critic of US military adventurism and has been an early commentator on both the Russia-Ukraine conflict and the US-Iran escalation. The views expressed in this interview are his own.
#LarryJohnson #USIranWar #StraitOfHormuz #Geopolitics #CIA
📢Disclaimer:
⚠️ The views expressed in this interview are those of the guest and are presented solely for the purposes of political commentary, analysis, and public debate. Deep Dive Perspective does not necessarily endorse the positions stated. Viewers are encouraged to consult a wide range of sources and form their own conclusions. Nothing in this video constitutes an attack on any religious, ethnic, or national group.

entry.title: [SYR] Trump's Massive Strike Plan for Iran | Elijah J. Magnier
entry.summary:
In this deep-dive analysis, renowned journalist and war correspondent Elijah J. Magnier joins us to unpack the reported U.S. plans for "devastating" strikes against Iran. We explore the strategic objectives behind these maneuvers, the critical role of global oil reserves, and the potential consequences of targeting Iranian infrastructure.
Magnier provides expert insight into Iran’s defensive capabilities, its leverage in the Strait of Hormuz, and why a ground invasion remains a geographical and military impossibility. We also discuss the shifting dynamics in Iraq, the legacy of the Soleimani assassination, and how regional players like the UAE are navigating the tension through strategic acquisitions like the Russian S-400 systems.
Follow Elijah J. Magnier: https://x.com/ejmalrai Read Elijah J. Magnier's Work: https://ejmagnier.com/

All things Iran, anti-imperialism, and empire’s decay on full sight.
Sina Rahmani is a producer extraordinaire and the mastermind behind a growing Tankieverse of spinoffs, including the very popular Everybody (still) loves Nora, AKA, TGT the musical. When he’s not suffering over bad lighting, a bad angle, or a horrible mic, he’s planting peas and planning for world domination through greenhouse farming. entry.title: [MR] EXCLUSIVE: SENIOR ANSAR ALLAH LEADER HASSAN MOHAMMAD TAHA SPEAKS
entry.summary:
In this exclusive interview, Sulaiman Ahmed speaks with senior Ansarullah leader Hassan Mohammad Taha about the ongoing conflict in Yemen, regional politics, and the challenges facing the country. The discussion explores the history of the war in Yemen, the Saudi-led military campaign, the blockade imposed on the country, and its humanitarian and political consequences. Hassan Mohammad Taha also shares Ansarullah's perspective on the movement's role in Yemen and the broader developments shaping the Middle East.
The interview also covers Ansarullah's position on Palestine and Gaza, relations with Iran, and the movement's message to Muslim nations and the international community. In addition, Hassan Mohammad Taha discusses the journey to Iran to attend the funeral of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, explaining how senior Ansarullah officials were able to travel despite what he describes as the long-standing restrictions and blockade affecting Yemen. This wide-ranging conversation offers insight into the movement's views on the region, current events, and the future of Yemen.
If you enjoy exclusive interviews, geopolitical analysis, and conversations with influential figures from around the world, subscribe for more in-depth interviews, on-the-ground reporting, and coverage from across the Middle East.
#HassanMohammadTaha #Ansarullah #Yemen #SulaimanAhmed #ExclusiveInterview #YemenWar #SaudiArabia #SaudiBlockade #Palestine #Gaza #Iran #MiddleEast #MiddleEastNews #Geopolitics #WorldNews #InternationalRelations #IndependentJournalism #Politics #CurrentAffairs #BreakingNews

entry.title: [SYR] LIVE From Iran: The Truth About Iran's New Supreme Leader | Ehsan Safarnejad
entry.summary:
The death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has transformed Iran's political landscape—but who is his successor, and what is really happening inside the country?
Joining me LIVE from Iran, political analyst Ehsan Safarnejad shares his firsthand experience after attending the funeral of Ayatollah Khamenei and explains the realities behind Iran's new Supreme Leader, the public mood, and the country's evolving political system.
We also examine the renewed military confrontation between the United States and Iran, why the latest diplomatic efforts appear to be collapsing, and whether the region is entering a prolonged period of escalation.
Follow Ehsan Safarnejad on X: https://x.com/Safarnejad_IR/ and check out his channel on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCn-_UlidwJz0jTP1opyZbmw

entry.title: [DDP] Iran's TRUMP CARD: Why Closing The Strait Of Hormuz Would End The US Economy
entry.summary:
Dimitri Lascaris was in the streets of Tehran. He watched tens of millions gather and says the Western narrative about Iran is, in his words, "complete and utter bollocks." So what did the funeral of Ayatollah Khamenei really mean — and what happens next?
Independent journalist and podcast host Dimitri Lascaris returns to Deep Dive Perspective with an eyewitness account from the funeral events of Ayatollah Khamenei — joined by writer and analyst Raza John Vedadi. Together they examine what the staggering turnout of an estimated 40-43 million people across Tehran, Qom, Mashhad, Najaf, and Karbala actually tells us about the state of Iranian society, the limits of US coercive strategy, and where the conflict goes from here. Lascaris describes not just the scale, but the emotional tenor on the streets — mass indignation directed at Trump and, significantly, at Iranian government negotiators pursuing dialogue with what Lascaris calls "the treacherous Trump regime." Vedadi argues that the funeral demonstrated something more fundamental: that the Islamic Republic is institutionalised, not personality-dependent, and that Khamenei's death has, in his assessment, reinvigorated rather than weakened Iran's revolutionary spirit. Both guests examine Iran's strategic "trump card" — the Strait of Hormuz — and the broader question of whether China and Russia are missing a historic window to establish genuine deterrence against US unilateralism. These are the guests' own analytical assessments, presented here for debate and scrutiny.
🔍 In This Episode: • Dimitri Lascaris's eyewitness account from Tehran — what he saw, what the crowds felt, and why he calls Western coverage "bollocks" • Mass indignation on the streets: calls for Trump's assassination, hostility toward Iranian negotiators — Lascaris's direct account • Why Vedadi argues the funeral has "nurtured the tree of revolution in Iran once again" — his analytical framework • Trump's fundamental miscalculation on Iran: the assumption of popular hatred for Khamenei vs what Lascaris witnessed • Iran's strategic trump card: closing the Strait of Hormuz — 40% of usable global oil, plus Bab el-Mandeb and Suez • Why the US "would rather destroy its own economy than accept Iran as an equal partner" — Vedadi's argument • China and Russia: Lascaris expresses concern at their restraint and argues they are missing a "moment of opportunity" — his view, for analytical debate • Iran's growing self-sufficiency vs Gulf state dependency on external military guarantees • The role of independent media in dismantling the controlled Western narrative on Iran
🕒 Timestamps: 00:00 Introduction 01:30 Dimitri Lascaris: Eyewitness from Tehran — What He Saw 04:15 The Scale: Were There Really 40-43 Million People? 07:00 Mass Indignation — Anger at Trump AND at Iranian Negotiators 10:20 Iranian Woman's Message to the West: "This Is The Real Iran" 12:45 Trump's Fundamental Miscalculation Exposed 15:30 US "Plan B" — The Syrian Option Applied to Iran 18:00 Vedadi: "Khamenei's Blood Has Nurtured the Tree of Revolution" 21:10 Iran's Institutionalised State — Not Reliant on One Leader 24:30 Iran's TRUMP CARD: Closing the Strait of Hormuz 28:00 Bab el-Mandeb and Suez — The Full Scenario 31:15 Why the US Refuses to Treat Iran as an Equal — Vedadi's Analysis 35:00 China and Russia: Why Aren't They Doing More? 40:00 Iran's Allies: Political Cover in the UN Security Council 43:30 The Western Narrative's Fatal Flaw — and Independent Media's Role 47:00 Final Assessment: Where Does This Go From Here?
👤 About the Guests:
Dimitri Lascaris is a Canadian lawyer, journalist, and host of the podcast "Reason to Resist." A widely cited independent voice on international affairs and social justice, he has reported from conflict zones and political flashpoints internationally. He was present in Iran during the Khamenei funeral events.
Raza John Vedadi is a writer, analyst, and blogger covering Iranian affairs, geopolitics, and the multipolar world order. He writes on Substack and other independent platforms and brings an Iranian-heritage perspective to the analysis of Western media narratives and Iran's strategic position.
#KhomeiniFuneral #Iran #DimitriLascaris #Geopolitics #MiddleEast
📢Disclaimer:
The views and opinions expressed in this video are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of Deep Dive Perspective. The content provided is for informational and entertainment purposes only and should not be construed as professional advice. We encourage viewers to do their own research and form their own opinions.
Deep Dive Perspective assumes no responsibility or liability for any errors or omissions in the content. All information is provided on an as-is basis. Viewer discretion is advised.

entry.title: [GZ] 'Millions of enraged people' hunting for revenge -top Iranian analyst on war's terrifying next phase
entry.summary:
The Grayzone's Max Blumenthal meets top Iranian analyst and journalist Seyed Mostafa Khoshcheshm in Tehran on July 9, moments after the US resumed airstrikes on Iran, and as the funeral procession for the assassinated Ayatollah Ali Khamenei proceeded through Iraq.
Blumenthal asks Khoshcheshm what the red flags of revenge seen throughout the funeral procession signify, and whether the many public calls for targeting the US president and his inner circle as retaliation for Khamenei's assassination, and the killings of other top Iranian leaders, are to be taken seriously.
Khoshcheshm explains why he sees a massive regional war over the horizon, and why this war may eventually reach the United States due to Trump's reckless behavior and "betrayal" of his own regional allies.
Seyed Mostafa Khoshcheshm has taught at a number of Iranian universities and academic centers, including the International Relations Faculty of the Iranian Foreign Ministry.
||| The Grayzone |||
Find more reporting at https://thegrayzone.com
On Substack: https://substack.com/@thegrayzone
Support our original journalism at Patreon: https://patreon.com/grayzone

#TheGrayzone

entry.title: [RTHINK] Lindsey Graham is in Hell, the US and Iran are exchanging blows...again! #lindseygraham #iran #trump
entry.summary:
Senator Lindsey Graham—one of the worst warmongers in American politics and a corrupt politician who repeatedly betrayed his country for Israel—has passed away under mysterious circumstances at the age of 71.
In this episode of Radical Thinker, we break down his evil legacy. We examine his extensive record, from his backing of the genocide in Gaza to his relentless cheerleading for the illegal US and Israeli war of aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran.
We also bring you the latest updates on the escalating regional conflict: Clashes in the Gulf: 🔴 Over the past few days, US occupation forces have exchanged heavy blows with the Iranian armed forces during a failed attempt to seize control of the Strait of Hormuz.
🔴 Turmoil in Iran: The Pezeshkian administration is under intense fire over the failure of recent negotiations. The nephew of Ruhollah Khomeini, the founder of the Islamic Republic, has openly criticized those pushing for diplomacy.
🔴 Calls for Retaliation: The current Supreme Leader of Iran is demanding severe revenge against those responsible for the assassination of former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.
Watch the full episode to uncover the truth behind the headlines, and find out more about what this means for the future of the region!
👇 Subscribe to Radical Thinker for more unfiltered geopolitical analysis and leave your thoughts in the comments below!
#lindseygraham #trump #iran #iranwar #khomeini #pezeshkian #khamenei #mojtabakhamenei #mojtaba #

You can also follow me on X: https://x.com/RadicalThinker5
entry.content:
Senator Lindsey Graham—one of the worst warmongers in American politics and a corrupt politician who repeatedly betrayed his country for Israel—has passed away under mysterious circumstances at the age of 71.

In this episode of Radical Thinker, we break down his evil legacy. We examine his extensive record, from his backing of the genocide in Gaza to his relentless cheerleading for the illegal US and Israeli war of aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran.

We also bring you the latest updates on the escalating regional conflict:
Clashes in the Gulf:
🔴 Over the past few days, US occupation forces have exchanged heavy blows with the Iranian armed forces during a failed attempt to seize control of the Strait of Hormuz.

🔴 Turmoil in Iran: The Pezeshkian administration is under intense fire over the failure of recent negotiations. The nephew of Ruhollah Khomeini, the founder of the Islamic Republic, has openly criticized those pushing for diplomacy.

🔴 Calls for Retaliation: The current Supreme Leader of Iran is demanding severe revenge against those responsible for the assassination of former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.

Watch the full episode to uncover the truth behind the headlines, and find out more about what this means for the future of the region!

entry.title: [SYR] 30 Minutes of Lindsey Graham Pushing for War
entry.summary:
Who is the real Lindsey Graham?
For decades, Senator Lindsey Graham has been one of Washington's most outspoken advocates of military intervention. From Iraq and Libya to Syria, Gaza, Iran and Ukraine, he has consistently argued for military escalation while dismissing the human cost of war.
In this episode, Kevork Almassian revisits Lindsey Graham's own words and public statements to examine his political legacy, his foreign policy record, and the consequences of America's interventionist doctrine.
Topics covered: - Lindsey Graham's calls for war across multiple conflicts. - His rhetoric on Iran, Gaza, Syria, Russia and Ukraine. - The human cost of interventionist foreign policy. - Has Washington learned anything from decades of war? - What does Lindsey Graham's record tell us about the U.S. foreign policy establishment?
This video presents publicly available statements for discussion and analysis. Viewers are encouraged to evaluate the evidence and draw their own conclusions.

entry.title: [GZ] Top Iranian official to The Grayzone: 'The people will not let go of the blood of their Imam'
entry.summary:
The Grayzone's Max Blumenthal interviews Tehran Mayor Alireza Zakani after he presided over the funeral of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei – the largest gathering in human history, where millions clamored for revenge for their leader's assassination.
Zakani is a leading conservative politician who campaigned for the presidency as an opponent of the JCPOA nuclear deal, and has since emerged as top critic of the MOU between Iran and the US.
In this exclusive interview with The Grayzone, Zakani addressed the popular calls for vengeance against Trump and Netanyahu during Khamenei's funeral, and whether they expressed an official policy.
As a former friend and ally of Maj. Gen. Qassem Soleimani and former President Ebrahim Raisi, Zakani discussed the impact of their deaths on the Iranian nation, and addressed whether Iran responded strongly enough to Soleimani's assassination.
Zakani explained his critical views on the MOU, and provided his perspective on the deadly riots that rocked Iran this January as well as the devastating US-Israeli military campaign that followed.
||| The Grayzone |||

#TheGrayzone

entry.title: [MR] EXCLUSIVE INTERVIEW WITH SENIOR ANSAR ALLAH LEADER MOHAMMED AL-BUKHAITI
entry.summary:
In this exclusive interview, Sulaiman Ahmed speaks with senior Ansarullah leader Mohammed al-Bukhaiti about some of the most consequential issues facing the Middle East today. The conversation explores the history of the war in Yemen, the Saudi-led military campaign and blockade, the humanitarian impact of the conflict, Ansarullah's political and military position, and the movement's perspective on regional developments.
One of the key topics discussed is how senior Ansarullah officials were able to travel to Iran despite what Mohammed al-Bukhaiti describes as a long-standing blockade and restrictions on Yemen's airspace. He explains the significance of their journey, the challenges involved, and why attending the funeral in Iran was important to the movement. The interview also covers Ansarullah's position on Palestine and Gaza, relations with Iran, the broader geopolitical situation, and Mohammed al-Bukhaiti's message to Muslim nations and the international community.
If you enjoy long-form interviews, geopolitical analysis, and conversations with key figures from around the world, subscribe for more exclusive interviews, in-depth discussions, and on-the-ground reporting.
#MohammedAlBukhaiti #Ansarullah #Yemen #SulaimanAhmed #ExclusiveInterview #MiddleEast #YemenWar #SaudiArabia #SaudiBlockade #Palestine #Gaza #Iran #Geopolitics #WorldNews #MiddleEastNews #InternationalRelations #BreakingNews #Journalism #Politics #CurrentEvents

entry.title: [GEO] The US is NOT a wealthy country: This is the real data
entry.summary:
Many Western economists and media outlets claim that Americans are wealthier than Europeans, but this is not true; it's based on a misunderstanding of the difference between average wealth and median wealth. Ben Norton explains how the extreme inequality in the US make its average wealth misleadingly look high, and why GDP per capita is not a good measurement. He debunks the myth of "Europoors", which is often spread by Donald Trump supporters.
Topics 0:00 The myth that Americans are wealthy 1:15 Average wealth vs median wealth 3:50 Data on US median wealth 6:42 Wealth inequality (Gini coefficient) 9:31 Poverty and homelessness 10:19 USA vs Europe 12:02 Wealth concentration of US elites 13:28 Distribution of growth in income 15:01 GDP per capita is misleading 21:18 US GDP by sector 23:13 Imputed rent and GDP 25:05 Life expectancy, mortality, etc. 27:10 US healthcare system 28:40 US health system vs OECD 32:58 Ridiculous "Europoor" myth 33:32 Trump cuts taxes on rich 34:27 Trump family corruption 36:20 Insider trading 36:56 Myth of American dream 38:18 Outro
|| Geopolitical Economy Report ||
Please consider supporting us at https://GeopoliticalEconomy.com/Support
Subscribe to our newsletter: https://GeopoliticalEconomy.Report
Join our community on Patreon: https://Patreon.com/GeopoliticalEconomy

He debunks the myth of "Europoors", which is often spread by Donald Trump supporters.

entry.title: [MR] I SPENT THE ENTIRE DAY IN MASHHAD DURING AYATOLLAH ALI KHAMENEI'S FUNERAL
entry.summary:
The day in Mashhad began long before the funeral ceremony. At 6:00 AM, nearly ten hours before the official procession, thousands of people had already gathered around the Imam Reza Shrine. As the morning progressed, more pilgrims and mourners continued arriving, and by the time the funeral began, millions had filled the streets of Mashhad for one of the largest public gatherings in Iran's recent history. This video follows the day's events from sunrise until late at night, showcasing the atmosphere, the funeral procession, the streets of Mashhad, and the remarkable scenes surrounding the funeral of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
From the early morning gathering outside the Imam Reza Shrine to the final moments inside the shrine complex near the grave of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, this video presents a chronological look at one of the most significant days in recent Iranian history. Along the way, you'll see the growing crowds, key moments from the funeral procession, and scenes from across Mashhad as the city welcomed millions of mourners throughout the day. Subscribe for more coverage, interviews, and updates from across Iran, including Tehran, Qom, Mashhad, and beyond.

entry.title: [EI] Has press freedom watchdog CPJ betrayed Gaza's slain journalists? with Ali Abunimah
entry.summary:
Ali Abunimah, executive director, reports on the revelations about how the Committee to Protect Journalists, facing pressure from pro-Israel groups, has been attempting to narrow the definition of who is a journalist in order to exclude some Palestinians.

entry.title: [DDP] Farage Is NOT Anti-Establishment — Chris Williamson Exposes Britain's Political Lie
entry.summary:
Chris Williamson — former Labour MP, now one of Britain's most uncompromising independent voices — says Nigel Farage is not anti-establishment. He IS the establishment. And that's just where he starts.
Former Derby North MP and political analyst Chris Williamson returns to Deep Dive Perspective for a forensic dismantling of the British political consensus. In Williamson's analysis, the public's choice at the ballot box is largely illusory: Labour, Conservative, Liberal Democrats and Reform UK all sit inside the same neoliberal, pro-corporate, pro-war framework — differentiated only by style, not substance. Farage, he argues, is the clearest example: privately educated, a former City commodities broker, multi-millionaire, donor-dependent — a figure who performs anti-establishment rhetoric while embodying establishment interests. On Starmer, Williamson is equally direct: a leader who made ten specific commitments to win his own party's leadership and broke every one of them. On economic policy, Williamson advances a Modern Monetary Theory-informed argument that a currency-sovereign government can never run out of money — and that the "bond vigilante" narrative used to justify austerity is, in his view, a managed myth. These are Williamson's own analytical positions, presented here for debate and scrutiny.
🔍 In This Episode: • Why Williamson argues Farage is "a pillar of the establishment, if truth be told" — his evidence and reasoning • Starmer's ten broken leadership pledges — what they were and why Williamson says this defines the man • Reform UK as "Thatcherite 2.0": what that means for working-class voters in Williamson's assessment • Andy Burnham: the most credible Labour alternative — and why Williamson still has serious reservations about him • Modern Monetary Theory explained: why Williamson argues Britain "can never run out of money" and what that means for public services • The case for public ownership: utilities, infrastructure, manufacturing — and why Williamson draws parallels with China's economic model • The National Security Act (2023): Williamson's charge that it is being used to silence political dissent • Palestine Declassified losing studio access following a Home Office letter — and what Williamson says this signals • Why he argues yielding to bad-faith accusations is the road to political extinction — the lesson he draws from Labour under Corbyn
🕒 Timestamps: 00:00 Introduction 01:15 Why Williamson Left Labour — and What He's Doing Now 03:40 The Illusion of Choice: Why All Mainstream Parties Serve the Same Interests 06:55 Nigel Farage: "A Pillar of the Establishment, If Truth Be Told" 11:20 Reform UK as Thatcherite 2.0 — Worse for Working-Class Britain 14:45 Keir Starmer: The Ten Broken Pledges 17:30 Andy Burnham: Best of a Bad Bunch or Genuine Alternative? 22:10 Modern Monetary Theory: "Britain Can Never Run Out of Money" 27:45 The "Bond Vigilante" Myth and Who It Serves 31:20 The Case for Public Ownership — and the China Model 36:00 Immigration: Cause, Effect and the "Fear" Narrative 39:15 The National Security Act — A Tool for Silencing Dissent? 43:30 Palestine Declassified and the Home Office Letter 47:00 Why You Cannot Yield to Bad-Faith Attacks — The Corbyn Lesson 51:20 What a Genuine Political Alternative Actually Requires
👤 About Chris Williamson: Chris Williamson is a British political activist and former Labour MP for Derby North (2010–2015 and 2017–2019). A close ally of Jeremy Corbyn, he was suspended by the Labour Party in 2019 and subsequently resigned. He is co-host of the show Palestine Declassified and a regular commentator on British and geopolitical affairs from an anti-imperialist, anti-neoliberal perspective. The views expressed in this interview are his own.
🌊 Deep Dive Perspective is an independent geopolitical channel featuring long-form conversations with journalists, analysts, academics, and independent thinkers on global power, international relations, media narratives, and the emerging multipolar world.
#ChrisWilliamson #UKPolitics #NigelFarage #KeirStarmer #ReformUK
📢Disclaimer:
⚠️ The views expressed in this interview are those of the guest and are presented solely for the purposes of political commentary, analysis, and public debate. Deep Dive Perspective does not necessarily endorse the positions stated. Viewers are encouraged to consult a wide range of sources and form their own conclusions.
Deep Dive Perspective assumes no responsibility or liability for any errors or omissions in the content. All information is provided on an as-is basis. Viewer discretion is advised.

entry.title: [EI] Iran forces US to face new reality in West Asia, with Helena Cobban
entry.summary:
Overnight, Iran launched attacks on US military targets in Bahrain, Kuwait and Qatar. This came in retaliation for US airstrikes on dozens of sites in Iran.
In recent days, the United States has been escalating threats and violence in West Asia – in flagrant violation of the June ceasefire agreement – while presenting itself as the guardian of stability.
But the basic reality remains the same since Washington and Tel Aviv launched their failed war of aggression and regime change in February: Iran controls the vital Strait of Hormuz and cannot be dislodged by military force.
The escalation in violence comes as millions of people across Iran and Iraq have been joining the funeral procession for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s leader murdered on the first day of the US-Israeli attack.
To discuss the latest regional developments, including Lebanon and Palestine, we’re joined by Helena Cobban.
She is an analyst with decades of experience covering the region. She directs Just World Educational and writes the newsletter Globalities. She is co-author with Rami Khouri of Understanding Hamas – And Why That Matters.
This is a segment from The Electronic Intifada's livestream on day 1007 of the Gaza genocide. Ali Abunimah, Nora Barrows-Friedman and Jon Elmer were joined by veteran journalist and analyst Helena Cobban. You can watch the full show here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wfkVIM6gVxw&sttick=0

entry.content:
Overnight, Iran launched attacks on US military targets in Bahrain, Kuwait and Qatar. This came in retaliation for US airstrikes on dozens of sites in Iran.

In recent days, the United States has been escalating threats and violence in West Asia – in flagrant violation of the June ceasefire agreement – while presenting itself as the guardian of stability.

But the basic reality remains the same since Washington and Tel Aviv launched their failed war of aggression and regime change in February: Iran controls the vital Strait of Hormuz and cannot be dislodged by military force.

The escalation in violence comes as millions of people across Iran and Iraq have been joining the funeral procession for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s leader murdered on the first day of the US-Israeli attack.

To discuss the latest regional developments, including Lebanon and Palestine, we’re joined by Helena Cobban.

She is an analyst with decades of experience covering the region. She directs Just World Educational and writes the newsletter Globalities. She is co-author with Rami Khouri of Understanding Hamas – And Why That Matters.

This is a segment from The Electronic Intifada's livestream on day 1007 of the Gaza genocide. Ali Abunimah, Nora Barrows-Friedman and Jon Elmer were joined by veteran journalist and analyst Helena Cobban. You can watch the full show here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wfkVIM6gVxw&sttick=0

entry.title: [SYR] Cold War 2.0 #76 | Is Israel Planning a False Flag Against Trump? | Tarik Cyril Amar
entry.summary:
In this episode of Cold War 2.0, Kevork Almassian and historian Tarik Cyril Amar dive deep into the latest geopolitical escalations. We begin by analyzing a viral clip of Professor Mohammad Marandi dismantling mainstream media narratives, before shifting to the Wall Street Journal’s explosive report on alleged Iranian "kill lists" targeting Donald Trump. Is this a genuine threat, or a calculated piece of disinformation designed to force a regional war?
We also examine the internal decay of the West, from Germany’s crumbling infrastructure to the European Union’s controversial new "Chat Control" legislation—a massive expansion of surveillance that threatens the privacy of every citizen.
Subscribe to Tarik's Substack: https://substack.com/@tarikcyrilamar

entry.title: [SYR] NATO’s Shadow Bank: How the West Plans to Finance the Next Great War | Mats Nilsson
entry.summary:
In this special broadcast, Kevork Almassian is joined by Mats Nilsson, senior analyst at the Dissident Club in Sweden, to peel back the layers of the recent NATO Ankara Summit. While mainstream headlines focus on diplomatic posturing, Nilsson reveals the emergence of a "transatlantic cash machine" designed to bankroll long-term militarization.
We dive deep into the creation of the "Defense Security and Resilience Bank," a multilateral entity that Nilsson argues is a nefarious tool to keep the West on a permanent war footing. We also discuss the shifting roles of regional players like Turkey and Israel, and why the ultimate target of these escalations may lie much further east than Ukraine or Iran.
Follow Mats Nilsson: https://x.com/mazzenilsson Follow Dissident Club: https://dissidentpodden.substack.com/
Topics discussed: - NATO's Ankara Summit: What's the alliance's new strategic direction? - Trump's meeting with Syria's new leadership and the future of the Golan Heights. - Will Turkey finally receive the F-35 despite Israeli objections? - Has the U.S.-Iran conflict entered a new and more dangerous phase? - Europe's military transformation after Ukraine. - What comes next for the Middle East?
📌 If you enjoy independent investigative journalism, consider supporting Kit Klarenberg's work and subscribe to Syriana Analysis for more in-depth geopolitical analysis.
🛡️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join

entry.title: [MR] ON THE GROUND IN QOM | AYATOLLAH ALI KHAMENEI'S HISTORIC FUNERAL
entry.summary: Millions of mourners gathered in the holy city of Qom as the funeral of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei continued, drawing people from across Iran and around the world. This report follows the day's events from morning until night, capturing the funeral procession as the coffin moved through the streets of Qom, the immense crowds, powerful chants and slogans from mourners, and the atmosphere surrounding one of the most significant events in Iran's modern history. The video also features interviews with people attending the funeral, providing firsthand perspectives on the day's events and what this moment means to them. Filmed entirely on location in Qom, this report documents the day's events as they unfolded, offering a ground-level perspective through exclusive footage and conversations from inside the city. As the coverage concludes, the video also examines breaking developments in the region, including reports that the United States is launching military operations connected to the Strait of Hormuz and what those developments could mean for the rapidly evolving situation. Subscribe for continuing on-the-ground coverage from Iran, including exclusive reports, interviews, and analysis from Tehran, Qom, Mashhad, and beyond. #AyatollahAliKhamenei #Khamenei #Qom #Iran #IranNews #QomNews #WorldNews #BreakingNews #MiddleEast #MiddleEastNews #Geopolitics #OnTheGround #FieldReport #IndependentJournalism #Journalism #CurrentEvents #InternationalNews #Documentary #IranFuneral #LiveFromQom #NewsReport #GlobalNews #StraitOfHormuz #USNews #IranUpdates
entry.content:

entry.title: [SYR] MI6’s Next Move: Is Venezuela the New Syria? | Kit Klarenberg
entry.summary:
Is the "Syrian Model" of regime change being exported to South America? Investigative journalist Kit Klarenberg joins us to discuss his latest findings on the arrival of the White Helmets in Venezuela following the recent earthquakes.
We dive deep into the history of the White Helmets—from their creation by British intelligence veterans to their role in alternative governance and propaganda in Syria. Is their deployment to Venezuela a humanitarian mission, or a "beachhead" for Western intelligence to penetrate Venezuelan state structures during a moment of extreme vulnerability?
We also explore the disturbing connection between Ukrainian military intelligence and al-Qaeda-linked groups in Africa’s Sahel region, and how these parallel strategies are redrawing the map of global proxy warfare.
Read Kit Klarenberg's investigations: https://substack.com/@kitklarenberg https://www.kitklarenberg.com/ Follow Kit on X: https://x.com/KitKlarenberg
📌 If you enjoy independent investigative journalism, consider supporting Kit Klarenberg's work and subscribe to Syriana Analysis for more in-depth geopolitical analysis.

entry.title: [MR] FUNERAL OF AYATOLLAH ALI KHAMENEI | FULL DAY COVERAGE FROM TEHRAN
entry.summary:
Millions of mourners gathered in Tehran on the third day of the funeral of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, transforming the Iranian capital into the center of one of the largest funeral processions in modern history. This video documents the day's events from morning until night, capturing the immense crowds, the funeral procession through the streets of Tehran, the coffin as it moved through the city, and the atmosphere surrounding a historic moment witnessed by people from across Iran and around the world.
This report provides a chronological look at the third day of the funeral through footage from the streets of Tehran. From the scale of the crowds to the emotion of the ceremony, the video offers an up-close perspective on an event that drew global attention. Millions of mourners gathered in Tehran on the third day of the funeral of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, transforming the Iranian capital into the center of one of the largest funeral processions in modern history. This video documents the day's events from morning until night, capturing the immense crowds, the funeral procession through the streets of Tehran, the coffin as it moved through the city, and the atmosphere surrounding a historic moment witnessed by people from across Iran and around the world.

entry.title: [RTHINK] Terror Bomb attacks in Damascus, during Macron's visit to Syria. Who is behind it? #macron #syria
entry.summary:
Explosions rock Damascus during French President Emmanuel Macron's high-profile visit to Syria. What is the real purpose of Macron's meetings with Syria's interim leader, Ahmad al-Sharaa (formerly known as Abu Mohammed al-Jolani)? Is France seeking to support Syria's reconstruction after years of devastating conflict, or is it pursuing renewed influence in a country it once controlled during the colonial era?
Who is responsible for the bomb blasts, and what objectives might these attacks serve? Are they intended to derail diplomatic efforts, fuel instability, or send a broader political message?
In today's episode of Radical Thinker, we examine the latest developments, the geopolitical stakes, and the competing narratives surrounding Macron's visit and the violence that unfolded in Damascus.
Watch now and share your thoughts in the comments. Don't forget to like, subscribe, and turn on notifications for more in-depth geopolitical analysis.
#syria #macron #bomb #terrorattack #jolani #damascus #iran #trump #iranwar #israel #usa #irannews #iranisraelwar #persiangulf #lebanon #straitofhormuz

entry.title: [SYR] BREAKING - Macron Arrives in Syria... Then Damascus Explodes
entry.summary:
French President Emmanuel Macron's landmark visit to Syria was overshadowed by explosions in Damascus, raising immediate questions about who was behind the attacks—and who stands to benefit from the timing.
In today's livestream, Kevork Almassian examines France's strategic objectives in Syria, the significance of the Damascus blasts, Netanyahu's controversial claims that Lebanese Christian villages sought annexation by Israel, reports that Donald Trump may approve Turkey's purchase of F-35 fighter jets despite Israeli objections, and the rapidly evolving balance of power in the Levant.
Topics discussed: - Macron's military and economic objectives in Syria - Who benefits from the explosions in Damascus? - Did the attacks send a political message? - Netanyahu's annexation claims and Lebanon's response - Trump, Turkey, and the reported F-35 deal - The new geopolitical contest over Syria and the Eastern Mediterranean

entry.title: [DDP] Is Anti-Zionism Enough? Rabbi Beck, Atzmon & Marouf in Unfiltered Debate
entry.summary:
Gilad Atzmon says anti-Zionism is failing Palestine. Rabbi Beck defends his movement's strategy. Laith Marouf issues a challenge to Jewish institutions. The debate nobody in the movement wants to have.
In one of Deep Dive Perspective's most debated episodes, three voices with sharply different analyses of the Palestine solidarity movement sit together to interrogate a central strategic question: is "anti-Zionism" — as a framing, a movement, and a political identity — sufficient to address what is happening to the Palestinian people? Gilad Atzmon argues that the movement has been structurally constrained by its own refusal to examine harder questions about the nature of the state it opposes — a position he roots in his reading of Israeli self-definition. Rabbi Beck of Neturei Karta argues that the most effective anti-Zionist work happens within Jewish communities, separating Jewish identity from Zionist ideology and demonstrating that opposition to Zionism is consistent with Jewish religious tradition. Laith Marouf offers a third position: that the moment for public-facing Jewish anti-Zionism has passed, and what is now required is a direct confrontation with Zionist control of Jewish institutions from within. These are the guests' own analytical frameworks, presented here for debate and critical scrutiny.
🔍 In This Episode: • Gilad Atzmon's argument that "anti-Zionism" obscures rather than confronts the core issue — his analytical position, presented for debate • Why Atzmon argues the Israel-Palestine movement has structurally deferred the hardest questions • Rabbi Beck: how the Neturei Karta approach works — and why he argues it demonstrates opposition to Zionism is a Jewish religious imperative, not antisemitism • Laith Marouf's challenge: why he argues Jewish anti-Zionists must move from public solidarity to internal institutional confrontation • The theological and historical split in Judaism over Zionism — from opposition to rabbinical acceptance post-1967 • Whether the small size of explicitly anti-Zionist Jewish communities undermines or amplifies their moral argument • The three guests' competing frameworks for what an effective Palestine solidarity movement now requires • Is the debate between these positions a distraction — or the most important conversation the movement is not having?
🕒 Timestamps: 00:00 Introduction & Context 02:32 Gilad Atzmon: Why He Questions the "Anti-Zionism" Label 04:00 "Zigzagging Around the Core Issue" — Atzmon's Critique of Movement Strategy 06:55 Laith Marouf: "Palestinians Have Defended Judaism for 100 Years" 09:23 Is Jewish Anti-Zionism "Just Talk"? The Demand for Action 17:07 The Movement's Crisis: Where Three Perspectives Converge and Diverge 18:40 Marouf: "The Rebellion Must Come From Inside Jewish Institutions" 21:55 Atzmon on Anti-Zionist Jewish Movements: A Statistical Reality Check 23:35 How Zionism Gained Rabbinical Legitimacy After 1967 24:49 Rabbi Beck Responds: The Role of Neturei Karta in the Wider Movement 34:56 The Debate Intensifies: What Are Anti-Zionist Jews Actually Prepared to Do? 39:39 Atzmon's Final Assessment — and Where the Three Guests End Up
👤 About the Guests:
Gilad Atzmon is a British-Israeli jazz musician, author, and political commentator. His books include The Wandering Who? (2011) and Being in time (2017). He is one of the most controversial voices in debates about Zionism, Jewish identity, and Palestine solidarity. The positions expressed in this interview are his own.
Rabbi Yisroel Dovid Weiss is a prominent spokesperson for Neturei Karta International — an Orthodox Jewish movement that considers Zionism a violation of Jewish religious law. He has spoken at international forums and pro-Palestine events worldwide, arguing that opposition to Zionism is a religious obligation, not antisemitism.
Laith Marouf is a Lebanese-Palestinian media producer and political commentator based in Canada. He has been a vocal critic of Israeli policies and a commentator on media representation of the Palestinian cause.
🌊 Deep Dive Perspective is an independent geopolitical channel featuring long-form conversations with journalists, analysts, academics, and independent thinkers on global power, international relations, media narratives, and the emerging multipolar world.
#AntiZionism #Palestine #GiladAtzmon #RabbiBeck #Geopolitics
📢Disclaimer:
⚠️ The views expressed in this interview are those of the individual guests and are presented solely for the purposes of political commentary, analytical debate, and the free exchange of ideas. Deep Dive Perspective does not endorse, adopt, or promote the positions of any guest. Viewers are strongly encouraged to consult a wide range of perspectives and reach their own conclusions. Nothing in this video constitutes an attack on any religious, ethnic, or national group.

entry.title: [GZ] Going to Khamenei's funeral with Prof. Marandi
entry.summary:
The Grayzone's Max Blumenthal joins Prof. Mohammad Marandi on his trip to Tehran's Mosalla to pay respects to Sayyed Ali Khameini.
The public mourning which began on July 4th for the martyred Iranian leader comprises the largest funeral in human history.
Marandi analyzes the significance of Khameini's life and legacy, and discusses his role in forging the Islamic Republic.

entry.title: [MR] I CAME TO TEHRAN TO WITNESS AYATOLLAH ALI KHAMENEI'S HISTORIC FUNERAL
entry.summary:
I'm officially leaving the United Kingdom and traveling to Iran to provide on-the-ground coverage from Tehran. In this first travel update, I announce my journey, share why I decided to make the trip, and take you along from the very beginning. You'll see my departure, beautiful visuals from Iran, a stopover at Istanbul Airport, and my first moments arriving in Tehran as I prepare to report from inside the country. Over the coming days, I'll be documenting my experience on the ground, speaking with people, capturing the atmosphere, and covering events as they unfold. If you're interested in international affairs, geopolitics, Middle Eastern history, and firsthand reporting from places rarely covered by independent creators, be sure to subscribe and follow this series. This is just the beginning of my journey, and there is much more to come from Tehran and across Iran.

entry.title: [SYR] Iran After Khamenei: The Question Nobody Can Answer | Radical Thinker
entry.summary:
In this special stream, Kevork Almassian is joined by Andy from Radical Thinker to dive deep into the mysteries surrounding Iran’s leadership following the reported assassination of Ali Khamenei. While Mojtaba Khamenei has been named the successor, his absence from his father’s funeral has sparked intense global speculation. Is he injured, in hiding for security, or is something even more complex happening behind the scenes?
In this episode, we discuss:
- The Mojtaba Mystery: Why the new Supreme Leader stayed hidden during the most defiant moment for the Iranian nation. - The "Revenge" Doctrine: Analyzing the calls for justice from key Iranian military and political figures, and what a direct response against Western or Israeli assets could actually look like. - Russia’s Final Warning: The symbolism of Dmitry Medvedev’s appearance at the funeral and his stark comparisons between the Strait of Hormuz and nuclear weapons. - EU Censorship: We break down the latest European Union laws targeting independent media and the reposting of "banned" content.
Follow the Radical Thinker: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCvJpN0vxULI9FjcCpOK4VPw

📢 Disclaimer: Guest opinions featured in this video do not necessarily reflect the views of Syriana Analysis.
📢 Syriana Analysis is an independent platform providing well-researched geopolitical insights, founded by Kevork Almassian. This program provides independent journalistic analysis for educational and documentary purposes, focusing on complex geopolitical shifts in West Asia and globally.
#Iran #Khamenei #MiddleEast #Israel #Geopolitics #SyrianaAnalysis
