entry.title: [BE] Block the Bombs Act Doesn't Block Any Bombs
entry.summary: A full arms embargo on Israel is very popular right now, and politicians have noticed. This has resulted in the 'Block the Bombs' act, H.R.3565, supported by people like Bernie Sanders, AOC, and the DSA - but this bill is not only not an arms embargo, IT ALSO BLOCKS NO BOMBS. It's all a scam to mislead you. Here I explain how. Support me on Patreon: https://patreon.com/BadEmpanada Subscribe to my Substack: https://ofpatmos.substack.com/ Become a BadEmpanada Live member: https://www.youtube.com/@BadEmpanadaLive/join Main Channel: https://youtube.com/BadEmpanada One-time donations: https://ko-fi.com/BadEmpanada Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/badempanada.com Spanish Channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCfqxvPxPVLuCzPeOsleOB3g

entry.title: [DDP] The Arab World's Dirty Secret: Who Is Really Helping Israel Win?
entry.summary:
Dr. Ali Al-Kabani and Ishan Safar-Innajad argue Arab leaders — led by the UAE — are not just failing Palestine, but actively enabling the occupation. The evidence is damning.
In a wide-ranging and forensic two-guest conversation, Egyptian journalist Dr. Ali Al-Kabani and geopolitical analyst Ishan Safar-Innajad deliver a detailed indictment of Arab leadership's record on Palestine — focusing specifically on the United Arab Emirates. In their analysis, the Abraham Accords were not a peace initiative but a strategic realignment that placed Gulf regimes on the side of a US-Israeli project designed to contain the resistance axis, neutralise Palestinian agency, and divide the wider Muslim world along manufactured sectarian lines. Both guests argue that the UAE's simultaneous operations in Sudan and Yemen — running parallel to its normalisation with Israel — reveal a coherent regional strategy rather than a collection of isolated policy decisions. With Iran's strategic depth and self-sufficiency growing, they contend the Gulf states' dependency on external military guarantees is becoming an existential liability rather than a source of security.
🔍 In This Episode: • Why Al-Kabani argues the UAE's normalisation with Israel makes it, in his words, "complicit" in events in Gaza — his view, presented for analytical debate • The UAE's alleged role in Sudan: destabilising the south to keep Egypt occupied on its own border • How the UAE is accused of operating a military facility in Yemen to monitor and constrain Ansar Allah movements • The gold mines of Sudan — and why guests argue UAE wealth extraction is directly tied to Sudanese instability • The Abraham Accords as a Gulf competition for US protection — not a peace process • Muslim Brotherhood as a regional proxy instrument — analysis of how guests assess its deployment across multiple conflicts • The Sunni-Shia divide: why both guests argue it is a manufactured colonial framework, not an organic religious schism • Iran vs the Gulf states: self-sufficiency, space industry, and strategic depth compared • What happens to Gulf regimes if the resistance axis is ever defeated — the guests' sobering assessment

entry.content:
Dr. Ali Al-Kabani and Ishan Safar-Innajad argue Arab leaders — led by
the UAE — are not just failing Palestine, but actively enabling the
occupation. The evidence is damning.

In a wide-ranging and forensic two-guest conversation, Egyptian journalist
Dr. Ali Al-Kabani and geopolitical analyst Ishan Safar-Innajad deliver a
detailed indictment of Arab leadership's record on Palestine — focusing
specifically on the United Arab Emirates. In their analysis, the Abraham
Accords were not a peace initiative but a strategic realignment that placed
Gulf regimes on the side of a US-Israeli project designed to contain the
resistance axis, neutralise Palestinian agency, and divide the wider Muslim
world along manufactured sectarian lines. Both guests argue that the
UAE's simultaneous operations in Sudan and Yemen — running parallel to
its normalisation with Israel — reveal a coherent regional strategy rather
than a collection of isolated policy decisions. With Iran's strategic depth
and self-sufficiency growing, they contend the Gulf states' dependency on
external military guarantees is becoming an existential liability rather
than a source of security.

🔍 In This Episode:
• Why Al-Kabani argues the UAE's normalisation with Israel makes it,
in his words, "complicit" in events in Gaza — his view, presented
for analytical debate

Egypt occupied on its own border

to monitor and constrain Ansar Allah movements

extraction is directly tied to Sudanese instability

— not a peace process
• Muslim Brotherhood as a regional proxy instrument — analysis
of how guests assess its deployment across multiple conflicts

manufactured colonial framework, not an organic religious schism

and strategic depth compared
• What happens to Gulf regimes if the resistance axis
is ever defeated — the guests' sobering assessment

entry.title: [SYR] Why Germany Still Can't Admit the Truth About Nord Stream | Tarik Cyril Amar

In this segment from Cold War 2.0, historian Tarik Cyril Amar joins Kevork Almassian to break down the bizarre political reaction to the Nord Stream sabotage—what Tarik calls "national masochism." We explore how the German elites are covering up a massive crime against their own people, the role of NATO "allies" in the attack, and why the truth is being suppressed to take away Germany’s future policy options.

entry.title: [SYR] Cold War 2.0 #75 | JD Vance’s Diplomacy Fail: The "Persian Tactic" Confusion | Tarik Cyril Amar
entry.summary:
In this episode of "Cold War 2.0," historian Dr. Tarik Cyril Amar joins Kevork Almassian to deconstruct the shifting landscape of US-Iran relations. We break down the "Forever Talks" strategy, the JD Vance "Persian Tactic" controversy, and the explosive new developments in the Nordstream investigation that Germany is seemingly desperate to ignore.
Topics covered:

entry.title: [EI] Don't stop speaking up for Gaza, with Asem Alnabih
entry.summary:
It has now been 1,000 days since 7 October 2023 – 1,000 days of Israel’s genocide in Gaza.
It is clear: This was a genocide from the start.
Immediately, Israeli leaders declared their intentions openly. On 9 October 2023, then-defense minister – and indicted war criminal – Yoav Gallant announced what he called a “complete siege” on Gaza: “There will be no electricity, no food, no fuel, everything is closed. We are fighting human animals and we act accordingly.”
In January 2024, the International Court of Justice cited that and other statements as evidence of a plausible genocide and ordered Israel to halt all genocidal acts – a ruling it has ignored with impunity.
To reflect on this terrible milestone, we’re joined by Asem Alnabih in Gaza. Asem is an engineer, PhD researcher and was the spokesperson for Gaza Municipality. He is now a correspondent for Al Araby TV.
Asem has been in Gaza since the start of the genocide. He has written powerfully for The Electronic Intifada about survival, loss, family, starvation, memory and the dreams, hopes and futures Israel has tried to steal from the people of Gaza.
This is a segment from The Electronic Intifada's livestream on day 1000 of the Gaza genocide. Ali Abunimah, Nora Barrows-Friedman and Asa Winstanley were joined writer, report and engineer Asem Alnabih in Gaza. You can watch the full show here: https://youtube.com/live/WQ0Hyp8r-wY
Theme Music by Greg Wilson
Your gift supports The Electronic Intifada's independent journalism on Palestine: • Donate by credit card, ApplePay, PayPal, Venmo or US bank account via Kindful: https://bit.ly/EIKindful
Gifts are welcome from anywhere and are tax-deductible for US taxpayers as allowed by law.

entry.title: [GEO] This scandal says everything about the West's hypocrisy!
entry.summary:
The US-based right-wing Chinese billionaire Miles Guo (Guo Wengui), who worked closely with Donald Trump advisor Steve Bannon, was sentenced to 30 years in prison for committing $1 billion of fraud. Ben Norton explains how Western governments and media outlets support "dissidents" from countries they don't like, who are often corrupt grifters and con artists; and how fake "experts" make money by spreading absurd disinformation about China, Iran, Cuba, Venezuela, North Korea, etc.

entry.title: [RTHINK] Will Israel attack the funeral procession of the late Supreme Leader of Iran, Ali Khamenei? #iran
entry.summary:
As the terms of the Memorandum of Understanding signed by Iran and the US are getting trampled by the US almost on a daily basis, the great funeral of the late Supreme Leader of Iran, Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei will begin this weekend. Will Israel try to attack the funeral, as it tried to do with the funerals of the former Secretary General of the Lebanese Resistance in Beirut? entry.title: [DDP] The UK's Political Psyop: Piers Corbyn on Farage, Starmer & The Hidden Agenda
entry.summary:
Piers Corbyn argues that Farage, Starmer and Reform are all playing the same game — and the real power pulling the strings has nothing to do with Westminster.
Political activist, former London Mayoral candidate and brother of Jeremy Corbyn, Piers Corbyn, joins Deep Dive Perspective for a wide-ranging and unfiltered assessment of Britain's political landscape. In Corbyn's analysis, the traditional left-right divide is now a managed performance: Labour has abandoned its base, Reform positions itself as a "dustbin of discontent" without coherent policy, and both, he argues, ultimately serve the same elite interests. He outlines a four-point programme he believes any credible leader must adopt, delivers a forensic critique of media bias and the BBC's role in narrative control, and explains why he believes corporate power — not Parliament — is the real locus of British political decision-making.
🔍 In This Episode: • Why Corbyn says Labour "lost its way" — and what voters are actually telling him on the doorstep • His argument that Reform is being used as a "dustbin of discontent" to divide the opposition • The four-point programme Corbyn says every leader must commit to • Immigration: why he calls the boat crisis a deliberate "psychological operation" — his words • Media bias, the BBC, and who Corbyn believes the coverage is designed to protect • The smearing of the Green Party — and why he sees the same playbook used against his brother Jeremy • "Corbynomics" vs the mega-corporations: what the establishment fears most • Corporate accountability and whether the new BBC director can rehabilitate public broadcasting
🕒 Timestamps: 00:00 Introduction 00:09 Piers Corbyn: Physics, Astrophysics, Activism & Political History 02:03 Why Corbyn Despairs at Modern British Politics 04:27 Starmer, Farage & Reform — "All Part of the Same Game" 06:59 Corbyn's Four-Point Programme for Political Change 08:12 Immigration: "It's a Psychological Operation" — Corbyn's View 12:43 Media Bias, the WEF & Why Corbyn Believes Starmer Serves the Agenda 15:25 The Smearing of the Greens — Same Playbook as Jeremy Corbyn 18:48 "Corbynomics" vs Mega-Corporations & Corporate Power 19:48 Government Accountability, the BBC & What Comes Next
👤 About Piers Corbyn: Piers Richard Corbyn is a British political activist, astrophysicist, and long-range weather forecaster. Brother of former Labour leader Jeremy Corbyn, he has stood for Mayor of London, served as a Southwark councillor, and has been involved in anti-racist campaigns and housing activism since the 1970s. He is known for his outspoken critique of mainstream political consensus.
🌊 Deep Dive Perspective is an independent geopolitical channel featuring long-form conversations with journalists, analysts, academics, and independent thinkers on global power, international relations, media narratives, and the emerging multipolar world.
🔔 SUBSCRIBE for more uncensored analysis that connects the dots the establishment wants to hide: https://www.youtube.com/@UCROsxSizsKbUzdARaocKDRQ
📌 WATCH NOW: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEq0jQ5NLgh_-RltiA-LSRCt https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEoqX56Fu2UbcO5_cEXf6tIC https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEqd42sdRwAXhuQbbGY22Bn0
#PiersCorbyn #UKPolitics #ReformUK #KeirStarmer #BritishPolitics

entry.title: [SYR] Israel vs Turkey: The Armenian Genocide Proxy War
entry.summary:
Is Israel’s recognition of the Armenian Genocide a moral breakthrough or a calculated move in a larger proxy war? In this deep-dive, we peel back the layers of transactional politics—from F-35 deals in Washington to billions in arms sales to Azerbaijan.
As a descendant of genocide survivors, I analyze why the Netanyahu government is making this move now, the hidden role of the "smart villages" on the Iranian border, and the dangerous game being played with Syrian national identity. We also look at the sharp reactions from Turkey, the Armenian government, and why some see this as a "trolling" of Trump and NATO.

entry.title: [GZ] Was Ecuadorian activist Monika Silva murdered for exposing govt-cartel links?
entry.summary:
The Grayzone's Oscar Leon spoke with close colleagues and friends of Ecuadorian activist Monika Silva, whose killing has shocked the nation, and deepened questions about the government's ties to narco cartels.
Monika Silva was one of Ecuador’s most tireless anti-corruption and environmental activists. On June 8, she was found dead in her home in Montañita, Ecuador. The government claims she was depressed, and had committed suicide.
This was misleading at best. A recent postmortem investigation revealed that Silva had been killed by a blunt trauma to the head, and strangulation.
In the months leading up to her death, Silva had repeatedly warned that her life was in danger. She also revealed that she had presented evidence to the US Embassy in Quito of Ecuadorian President Daniel Noboa’s links to the international drug trade.
Noboa happens to be a key Trump ally, a billionaire son of Miami, who is rapidly transforming Ecuador into a militarized bulwark of US control.
Silva's killing, and the government’s deceptive statements about it, demonstrate the harsh reality that forced so many Ecuadorians into silence. It is not safe to investigate corruption in this country.
||| The Grayzone |||
Find more reporting at https://thegrayzone.com

entry.title: [EAST] IN SESSION #28: Disrupting normalization in Palestine & Lebanon with Abdaljawad Omar
entry.summary:
This collaboration with my dear comrade Abboud has been a long time coming—and I’m so excited for you all to join us. You likely already know Abboud from his frequent appearances with our comrades at MAKC; I always look to him for some of the most meticulous analysis we have from the Arab world, and especially our region. We have a lot to get into since the last time we were together on screen, so make sure you tune in!!
Abdaljawad Omar is a Palestinian writer and Assistant Professor at Birzeit University. His work focuses on anti-colonial resistance and the modes of refusal that Palestinians have managed to create and sustain. He has two forthcoming books: Can the Palestinian Mourn? (Seagull Books, Spring 2027) and Tufan: Palestinian Resistance as Thought and Practice (Pluto Press, 2027).
Make sure to subscribe to our YouTube channel and for exclusive prereleases, consider becoming a member at our Patreon.
You can also find us on IG: @psychicmilitancypod

entry.title: [SYR] Israel & Lebanon Shock Deal: Will Hezbollah Survive? | Marwa Osman
entry.summary:
In this high-stakes episode of Syriana Analysis, Marwa Osman joins us to dissect the recent "historic deal" signed between the Lebanese government and Israel in Washington. We dive deep into the geopolitical implications of this agreement and what it truly means for the future of Lebanon and the resistance.
Is this the beginning of a new era in the Middle East, or a calculated move to dismantle Hezbollah’s influence? We analyze the secret layers of the pact, Washington’s role as the mediator, and the potential shifts in regional power dynamics.
In this stream, we discuss:
- The core terms of the Lebanon-Israel deal. - Hezbollah's reaction and their survival strategy moving forward. - How this agreement impacts the "Resistance Axis." - The broader consequences for regional stability and sovereignty.
📌Support Syriana Analysis: If you value independent geopolitical analysis, please consider supporting our work so we can continue bringing you these deep dives:
🛡️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join
00:00 Introduction: The Lebanon-Israel "Shock Deal" 02:08 Why the Framework Deal is Void and Unconstitutional 04:24 Recent Operations and Military Realities in South Lebanon 05:48 Iran-US Memorandum vs. The Lebanon Deal 08:18 The Human Cost: Immunity for Massacres and National Disgrace 09:10 20 Political Parties Reject the Agreement 11:07 Critiquing the Lebanese Negotiators and Leadership 13:15 Internal Anger and the Risk of Civil Strife 17:53 Resistance on the Front Line: "Lebanon is a Cemetery for Soldiers" 20:10 The Political Dead End: No Majority in Parliament 23:28 Responding to Pro-Peace Arguments and Colonial Narratives 28:25 The "Joint Objective": Arab Forces and the Role of Jolani 33:05 Syria Update: The Fall of Damascus and Future Resistance 38:22 Can Iran Negotiate a Ceasefire? 42:37 Israel’s Isolation and Global Economic Consequences 47:00 Hezbollah’s Hidden Cards and Advanced Weaponry 51:36 Reformists vs. IRGC: Internal Iranian Dynamics 57:38 Samir Geagea’s "Wet Dreams" and Christian Politics 1:00:54 The Strength of the People vs. Political Failure 1:05:26 Final Thoughts and Support for Syriana Analysis
📢 Disclaimer: This conversation is presented for news, educational, and analytical purposes. Views expressed by guests are their own and do not necessarily reflect the views of Syriana Analysis. Syriana Analysis does not endorse threats, hatred, or violence against civilians, ethnic, religious, or national groups. The discussion concerns war, occupation, resistance, diplomacy, and regional politics in a journalistic context.
📢 Syriana Analysis is an independent platform providing well-researched geopolitical insights, founded by Kevork Almassian. This program provides independent journalistic analysis for educational and documentary purposes, focusing on complex geopolitical shifts in West Asia and globally.
#Lebanon #Israel #Hezbollah #MarwaOsman #MiddleEastPolitics #SyrianaAnalysis
entry.content:
In this high-stakes episode of Syriana Analysis, Marwa Osman joins us to dissect the recent "historic deal" signed between the Lebanese government and Israel in Washington. We dive deep into the geopolitical implications of this agreement and what it truly means for the future of Lebanon and the resistance.

Is this the beginning of a new era in the Middle East, or a calculated move to dismantle Hezbollah’s influence? We analyze the secret layers of the pact, Washington’s role as the mediator, and the potential shifts in regional power dynamics.

In this stream, we discuss:

- The core terms of the Lebanon-Israel deal.
- Hezbollah's reaction and their survival strategy moving forward.
- How this agreement impacts the "Resistance Axis."

entry.title: [LOI] Socialist Humanism: The Struggle for LGBTQ+ Equality in Vietnam
entry.summary:
Vietnam has a vibrant and visible LGBTQ+ community which has made great progress in advancing equality in our socialist society. entry.title: [Duran] UK Burnham is Blair 3.0, UK Gets More of the Same as Britain Sinks
entry.summary:
The Duran: Episode 2556 Britain is getting a more Blairite government than Starmer ever ran, and Alex Christoforou and Alexander Mercouris of The Duran break down exactly what Andy Burnham's rise to prime minister really means: the same plan, the same loyalty to Project Ukraine, and the same drift back toward the EU that has driven Britain's longest decline in living standards since the industrial revolution. With a Blair loyalist installed as chief of staff, West Streeting likely as finance minister, and Burnham quietly signaling a Brexit reversal, the question is not whether things will change, but how much worse they have to get before Britain finally changes course.

entry.title: [DDP] Scott Ritter: Iran WON & Trump Knows It — The Hormuz MOU Is A Facade
entry.summary:
Military analyst Scott Ritter calls the US-Iran Strait of Hormuz MOU a "political facade" — and argues America under Trump is fundamentally "agreement incapable." What does that mean for the Middle East?
Former UN weapons inspector Scott Ritter delivers a forensic breakdown of the Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) signed between the United States and Iran on the Strait of Hormuz. According to Ritter, the agreement deliberately sidesteps every hard issue — Iran's nuclear programme, its ballistic missile capability, and its regional posture — deferring them to a 60-day consultation process designed, in his assessment, to hand Trump a domestic political win ahead of the midterms rather than produce a durable settlement. With Iran increasingly integrated into BRICS and the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation, Ritter argues the window for effective American economic leverage is closing fast — and may already be shut.
🔍 In This Episode: • Why Ritter characterises the Hormuz MOU as an "act of political desperation" • The three critical issues the agreement deliberately avoids • Why the Trump administration is, in Ritter's words, "agreement incapable" • How Iran's BRICS and SCO integration is rendering US sanctions increasingly ineffective • The strategic basis for Ritter's claim that "Iran won the war" • Israel's diminishing leverage and the prospect of a Trump–Netanyahu rupture • The collapse of the Abraham Accords and the "Sunni crescent" strategy • De-dollarisation, multipolarity, and the emerging post-American world order
🕒 Timestamps: 00:00 Introduction 00:09 The Hormuz MOU: Why Ritter Calls It a "Political Facade" 05:22 "The US Is Agreement Incapable" — Ritter's Core Verdict on Trump 06:22 Trump's Real Motive: Midterms, Oil Prices & Domestic Politics 09:31 "Iran Won the War" — A Strategic Assessment 10:39 BRICS Integration & The Collapse of US Sanctions Leverage 14:08 Israel's Diminishing Military and Diplomatic Power 15:22 A Coming Trump–Netanyahu Rupture? 17:24 Netanyahu's Strategic Bankruptcy — "His Ego Has Written Checks the Body Can't Cash" 19:17 The Collapse of the "Sunni Crescent" Strategy 20:22 The Abraham Accords: Dead and Buried 24:39 Multipolarity, De-dollarisation & the Emerging World Order
👤 About Scott Ritter: Scott Ritter is a former United States Marine Corps intelligence officer and Chief UN Weapons Inspector in Iraq (1991–1998). He is now a widely cited military analyst, author, and commentator on US foreign policy, arms control, and global security.
🌊 Deep Dive Perspective is an independent geopolitical channel featuring long-form conversations with journalists, analysts, academics, and independent thinkers on global power, international relations, media narratives, and the emerging multipolar world.
🔔 SUBSCRIBE for more uncensored analysis that connects the dots the establishment wants to hide: https://www.youtube.com/@UCROsxSizsKbUzdARaocKDRQ
📌 WATCH NOW: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEq0jQ5NLgh_-RltiA-LSRCt https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEoqX56Fu2UbcO5_cEXf6tIC https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEqd42sdRwAXhuQbbGY22Bn0
The views expressed in this interview are those of the guest and are presented for analytical and educational purposes. Deep Dive Perspective does not necessarily endorse the positions stated.
#ScottRitter #StraitOfHormuz #TrumpIran #IranDeal #Geopolitics

The views and opinions expressed in this video are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of Deep Dive Perspective. The content provided is for informational and entertainment purposes only and should not be construed as professional advice. We encourage viewers to do their own research and form their own opinions.
Deep Dive Perspective assumes no responsibility or liability for any errors or omissions in the content. All information is provided on an as-is basis. Viewer discretion is advised.

entry.title: [SYR] Cold War 2.0 #74 | America’s Great Implosion: The Israel Factor | Tarik Cyril Amar
entry.summary:
In this episode of Cold War 2.0, Kevork Almassian is joined by historian Dr. Tarik Cyril Amar to analyze the profound shifts in the U.S.-Israel relationship and what Amar describes as America’s "Great Political Implosion." We dive into the Reuters report on Benjamin Netanyahu’s legacy, the rise of JD Vance and the "restrainer" narrative, and how shifting U.S. public opinion is redrawing the political map.
We also examine the latest UN Commission report on the targeting of children in Gaza and the broader geopolitical implications of a rising multipolar world involving China and the UK.

entry.title: [TLorenz] Congress Just Voted to Track Your Web History
entry.summary:
The Kids Act Could End Internet Freedom As We Know It.
SUPPORT MY WORK: Buy a paid subscription to my newsletter at usermag.co Support my work on Patreon: http://patreon.com/taylorlorenz
A massive new package of legislation, dubbed the "Kids Act," is moving through Congress with unprecedented speed. The package is a broad-based censorship and surveillance scheme that will affect every single American.
In this episode of Free Speech Friday, I sit down with Adam Thierer, a senior fellow at the Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression (FIRE), to break down the terrifying reality of what’s happening in Washington D.C. right now. Adam, who has been fighting for internet freedom since the 1990s, explains how these laws demand mass age verification (which applies to adults too), regulate design features like infinite scroll, and even target messaging apps and VPNs.
We also dive into the 1,800 AI bills popping up across states, Bernie Sanders' misguided plans for AI, and why the government is moving to create an identity layer for the entire internet. We also discuss the toxic brew of "moral panic," fake anti-big tech sentiment, and censorship that is driving this legislation forward.
Topics covered: What the Kids Act is and how it passed committee Mass age verification and the internet ID layer The end of online anonymity Why messaging apps and video games are targets State laws controlling the national internet State AI preemption and Bernie Sanders' AI plans The history of internet censorship from 1996 to today
#AI #Tech #TechNews #InternetFreedom #KidsAct #Censorship #TechPolicy #OnlinePrivacy #AILaws #VPNBan #FreeSpeech #MassSurveillance #FirstAmendment #BigTech #BernieSanders #KOSA #DataPrivacy
entry.content:

A massive new package of legislation, dubbed the "Kids Act," is moving through Congress with unprecedented speed. The package is a broad-based censorship and surveillance scheme that will affect every single American.

In this episode of Free Speech Friday, I sit down with Adam Thierer, a senior fellow at the Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression (FIRE), to break down the terrifying reality of what’s happening in Washington D.C. right now. Adam, who has been fighting for internet freedom since the 1990s, explains how these laws demand mass age verification (which applies to adults too), regulate design features like infinite scroll, and even target messaging apps and VPNs.

We also dive into the 1,800 AI bills popping up across states, Bernie Sanders' misguided plans for AI, and why the government is moving to create an identity layer for the entire internet. We also discuss the toxic brew of "moral panic," fake anti-big tech sentiment, and censorship that is driving this legislation forward.

What the Kids Act is and how it passed committee
Mass age verification and the internet ID layer
The end of online anonymity
Why messaging apps and video games are targets
State laws controlling the national internet
State AI preemption and Bernie Sanders' AI plans

entry.title: [EI] How FPV drones shaped Lebanon battle, with Jon Elmer
entry.summary:
Jon Elmer, contributing editor, reports on how Hizballah’s armed FPV drones and anti-tank missiles shaped the battle in South Lebanon before the US-Iran Memorandum of Understanding paused the fight earlier this week; and a breakdown of the Hizballah ambush that destroyed a Merkava tank, killing the commander of the Israeli military’s 52nd Armored Battalion during the battle at Ali al-Taher ridge. https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLHEvpbppx_4rb9lCZ9fRyQiTc5A7eS9FA

entry.title: [Duran] Rutte claims NATO helped attack Iran as Trump pivots to Ukraine
entry.summary:
The Duran: Episode 2553 Alex Christoforou and Alexander Mercouris on The Duran break down the Rutte and Trump meeting ahead of the NATO summit, revealing how the failed Iran war has left both Trump and NATO significantly weakened on the world stage. With 5,000 US air missions flown from European bases and the full NATO alliance still unable to achieve its military objectives against Iran, the uncomfortable truth is that the collective West's greatest military alliance has been exposed as far less powerful than advertised. As a deflated Trump pivots back toward Project Ukraine with renewed pledges of support for Zelensky, is he being maneuvered into exactly the position NATO and the neocons always wanted him in?

entry.title: [GZ] Under Israeli fire, Lebanese struggle to return home
entry.summary:
After news emerged that the US and Iran had finally reached an agreement to cease hostilities, thousands of civilians raced to return to their homes in Southern Lebanon.
Days later, Israel responded to the elimination of its occupying soldiers by carrying out some of the worst massacres in its war on Lebanon, forcing civilians back to an uncertain future in the north — and forcing Iran to close the Strait of Hormuz again.
In an exclusive report for The Grayzone, Wyatt Reed traveled to Southern Lebanon to speak to the families in the line of fire, and see the destruction wrought by Israel firsthand.
||| The Grayzone |||
Find more reporting at https://thegrayzone.com
Support our original journalism at Patreon: https://patreon.com/grayzone

#TheGrayzone

entry.title: [RTHINK] Trump violates the MoU with Iran, Al-Jolani says he cares for the Shias of Lebanon #iran #trump #usa
entry.summary:
Donald Trump is already violating the terms agreed by the US and Iran in the Memorandum of Understanding, by adding new conditions for the release of Iranian frozen assets, only if Iran will agree to use them to buy agricultural products from American farmers. Trump is imposing AEIA inspections on Iranian nuclear sites, although the nuclear file has not been negotiated yet in the Memorandum of Understanding. Ahmad al-Sharaa al-Jolani, the so-called president of Syria and the former leader of Jabhat al Nusra, says he cares for the Shias of Lebanon and that not all Lebanese Shias are supporters of Hezbollah. Is he preparing the ground for a Syrian involvement in Lebanon, in support of Israel? Find out more from this episode of Radical Thinker!
#iran #iranwar #trump #irannews #iranisraelwar #usa #israel #persiangulf #straitofhormuz #lebanon #syria #radicalthinker

entry.title: [SYR] Is Trump’s Iran Failure the Secret to Saving America?
entry.summary:
In this deep-dive analysis, we explore the aftermath of Donald Trump’s military tensions with Iran. While many see a tactical debacle, a closer look at the geopolitical shift suggests this failure may have inadvertently "saved" America by exposing the limits of military force and shifting the focus back to diplomacy and domestic stability.
Has the failure of "Maximum Pressure" actually prevented a larger regional war? We break down the long-term consequences for U.S. foreign policy, the shift in Middle Eastern power dynamics, and the "Israeli factor" behind the strategy.
Key Discussion Points:
0:00 – Intro: The "Debacle" Explained 6:20 – Strategic Costs: Why the War on Iran Failed 12:00 – The China & Russia Pivot: Depleting U.S. Resources 20:15 – The Israeli Factor: Regime Change vs. Regional Reality 26:40 – The End of Pan-Arabism & Rise of the Three Regional Projects 37:10 – Is This the "Secret Gift" for the American Public? 48:00 – The New Regional Order: Who Emerges Victorious? 55:50 – Closing: Is Diplomacy the Only Way Forward?
📌Support Syriana Analysis: If you value independent geopolitical analysis, please consider supporting our work so we can continue bringing you these deep dives:
🛡️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join
📢 Syriana Analysis is an independent platform providing well-researched geopolitical insights, founded by Kevork Almassian. This program provides independent journalistic analysis for educational and documentary purposes, focusing on complex geopolitical shifts in West Asia and globally.
#Geopolitics #Iran #Trump #MiddleEastAnalysis #SyrianaAnalysis #ForeignPolicy #USNews

entry.title: [TLorenz] The Riskiest Bet in Tech History
entry.summary:
SpaceX just pulled off the biggest IPO in history and made Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire. But is it all built on a fantasy?
SUPPORT MY WORK: Buy a paid subscription to my newsletter at usermag.co Support my work on Patreon: http://patreon.com/taylorlorenz
In this week's episode of Power User, I sit down with Ryan Mac, the main New York Times reporter covering SpaceX and co-author of Character Limit: How Elon Musk Destroyed Twitter, to unpack how SpaceX went from a startup mocked by aerospace veterans to a $2+ trillion company that governments, militaries, and your retirement fund now depend on.
We trace the full story from the early rocket explosions, the near-bankruptcy before Falcon 1 reached orbit in 2008, the NASA contract that saved the company, and how reusable rockets and Starlink turned SpaceX into an unstoppable money machine with 10,000 satellites in orbit.
We dive deep into Elon's trillion-dollar bet on "orbital data centers," his plan to move AI infrastructure into space, the acquisition of xAI, the Cursor deal, a possible Tesla–SpaceX merger, and his 82% voting control that makes him almost impossible to challenge.
Is Elon Musk now too big to fail? Has he escaped the gravity of accountability? Or will his dreams of putting data centers in space come crashing back to Earth? And if/when that happens, who's left holding the bag? Ryan and I get into all of it.
In this episode: – How SpaceX was founded and almost died – Why Starlink became the company's cash engine – Reusable rockets, barge landings, and the "chopsticks" catch – The plan to put AI data centers in space (and why experts are skeptical) – Inside the record-breaking SpaceX IPO – How index funds and 401ks got pulled into SpaceX – Elon's 82% control and the road to "Elon Inc" – Whether Musk is now the most powerful man on Earth

entry.title: [Duran] US-Iran Ceasefire Talks. Conflict Round 3 Is Coming
entry.summary:
The Duran: Episode 2551 Alex Christoforou and Alexander Mercouris of The Duran break down the 18 hours of US-Iran negotiations in Geneva, where JD Vance and Steve Witkoff met with Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi and Ghalibaf in talks mediated by Pakistan and Qatar, producing a cautious framework agreement while leaving the deepest questions entirely unresolved. With neocon fury building in Washington, a contentious four and a half hour Trump-Graham meeting suggesting major behind-the-scenes pressure, and Iran maintaining its red line on Lebanon and the Strait of Hormuz, Alex and Alexander argue that the ceasefire is merely a pause before a far larger confrontation.

entry.title: [GEO] Are the US and Israel really fighting? Or is something bigger happening?
entry.summary:
The USA and Iran are holding negotiations to end the war, but Tehran condemned Washington for violating the agreement, because Israel keeps attacking Lebanon. US Vice President JD Vance appeared to criticize Israel, but Ben Norton explains the deep hypocrisy of the United States, which is still giving Netanyahu billions of dollars of weapons and military aid.

entry.title: [Duran] Keir Starmer Resigns as UK Prime Minister. Burnham's coronation awaits
entry.summary:
The Duran: Episode 2550 Keir Starmer has announced his resignation as UK Prime Minister, but in a move that exposes how deeply broken British politics has become, he will remain in office for weeks, possibly all the way through September, leaving Britain effectively without a functioning government. Alex Christoforou and Alexander Mercouris break down why Andy Burnham is now the inevitable successor, why the transition is being deliberately dragged out, and what the deep state is planning to tell Burnham before he ever sets foot in Downing Street. Is Britain about to swap one puppet for another, or is there any chance Burnham breaks from the script?
00:00:00 Starmer Announces Resignation 00:01:25 Why Starmer Lasted So Long Despite Failing 00:03:26 Andy Burnham Wins Makerfield and Changes Everything 00:04:29 Starmers Weekend Collapse and the Numbers That Weren't There 00:05:44 Britain's Broken Political Transition Problem 00:07:03 Weeks of Political Paralysis Ahead 00:08:17 The Burnham Coronation and Why It Was Delayed 00:11:35 Burnham Could Be PM Today But Chose Not to Be 00:12:21 The Deep State Steps In 00:14:48 What Burnham Will Be Told and What He Will Agree To

entry.title: [SYR] The War That Changed Me Forever | Kevork Almassian
entry.summary:
In this raw and personal 39th birthday special, I’m stepping away from the geopolitical maps of Syriana Analysis to share the real story of my journey—from growing up in Aleppo to seeking asylum in Germany. This is a reflection on the war that changed me forever and the personal battles I’ve fought behind the scenes.
For the first time, I discuss the weight of the smear campaigns and demonization I faced, the mental health breakdown that led me to the hospital in 2020, and the moment I realized that no one was coming to save me. I share how committing to fitness and "the gym" literally saved my life, helping me build the resilience needed to keep this channel independent and focused on the truth.
This video is for anyone facing their own demons, for the younger generation building their path, and for this incredible community that has supported me since 2017.
In this reflection, I cover:
Life in Aleppo: Growing up off-grid and the simplicity of nature. The Reality of War: How 14 years of conflict destroyed a nation and its intellectual class. Refugee Camps in Germany: The truth about my experience and the backlash that followed. The Turning Point: My 2020 hospital stay and how I reclaimed my physical and mental health. Building a Career, Not a Job: Why I left my easy job in Berlin to focus on Syrian Analysis. The Future of the Channel: New shows with Tarik Cyril Amar and our path forward. Thank you for 39 years of life lessons and for being the reason Syriana Analysis exists today.
📌Support Syriana Analysis: If you value independent geopolitical analysis, please consider supporting our work so we can continue bringing you these deep dives:
🛡️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join
#SyrianaAnalysis #KevorkAlmassian #BirthdaySpecial #AleppoToBerlin #MentalHealth #Resilience #Geopolitics #IndependentMedia

entry.title: [EI] Why I destroyed Israeli drones, with Zoë Rogers
entry.summary:
Asa Winstanley, associate editor, speaks with Palestine Action activist Zoë Rogers, who was acquitted of criminal damage after spending 18 months in custody for breaking into an Elbit Systems factory in Bristol and helping destroy drones that could have been used against Palestinians.
Rogers, 22, was one of six activists who participated in the direct action in August 2024.
This is a segment from The Electronic Intifada's livestream on day 986 of the Gaza genocide. Ali Abunimah, Nora Barrows-Friedman, Jon Elmer and Asa Winstanley were joined by former Palestine Action prisoner Zoë Rogers. You can watch the full show here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UKhtKKgXNBw

entry.title: [GZ] Colombians slam Trump's interference in presidential election
entry.summary:
US interference directed by the Trump administration has already influenced elections in Ecuador, Argentina, and Honduras, swinging votes to right-wing militarists determined to unravel what's left of their countries' social sectors while authorizing harsh repression against their opponents.
Now, as Colombia’s presidential runoff approaches, The Grayzone visits Huila, Colombia, near the border with Ecuador, to hear what voters think about Trump's backing of ultra-right candidate Abeldardo De La Espriella and his threats against the country if it elects leftist Ivan Cepeda.
Produced by Oscar Leon, shot by Bladimir Sanchez Espitia
||| The Grayzone |||

entry.title: [SYR] Cold War 2.0 #73 | Deep State Exposed: Gabbard’s Bombshell & Trump’s Iran Gamble | Tarik Cyril Amar
entry.summary:
In this episode of Cold War 2.0, host Kevork Almassian is joined by Dr. Tarik Cyril Amar to analyze the seismic political shifts occurring during the transition of power. We dive deep into Tulsi Gabbard’s final actions as Director of National Intelligence, where she released never-before-seen documents exposing Dr. Fauci’s alleged role in suppressing the lab leak theory and his ties to the intelligence community.
We also tackle the "Trump Iran Peace Memo" revealed by The Economist. Is this a strategic masterstroke or a massive gamble that ignores decades of geopolitical reality? From the future of the MAGA movement to the rising alliance between Netanyahu and Modi, we break down the hidden forces shaping the new global order.
Follow Tarik's Substack: https://substack.com/@tarikcyrilamar
WATCH more conversations with Tarik in our special playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLiReNnJYf0hLBGZv8NATxsgaOMJ0eifyy

entry.title: [GZ] UFC champ arrested for criticizing Israel?

He was arrested by US Park Police after entering the event.

entry.title: [BE] How They Make You Zionist - The 'Pro-Palestine' Counterinsurgency
entry.summary:
There is a concerted normalization effort by the political establishment to convert supporters of Palestine into liberal Zionists, being carried out to mitigate the political fallout of the Gaza Genocide in the West. So far, this has been highly effective. It is imperative that we are aware of this and do the exact opposite to what they want us to do. In this video I outline how this works, using some of its most prominent participants as key examples. entry.title: [EI] Iran won the war, but will Israel wreck the deal to end it? with Ali Abunimah
entry.summary:
The United States and Iran have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to end the war of aggression launched by Washington and Tel Aviv on 28 February.
President Donald Trump made huge concessions to get Iran to sign, as the world was about to head over an economic cliff. There is no doubt that Iran won the war.
Ali Abunimah, executive director, reports on what is in the agreement. And although the text confirms Iran’s enormous leverage, it won’t necessarily be smooth sailing, as many difficult issues remain, and Israel, which continues to attack and occupy Lebanon and Gaza, will be determined to unravel the agreement.
This is a segment from The Electronic Intifada's livestream on day 986 of the Gaza genocide. Ali Abunimah, Nora Barrows-Friedman, Jon Elmer and Asa Winstanley were joined by former Palestine Action prisoner Zoë Rogers. You can watch the full show here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UKhtKKgXNBw
Theme Music by Greg Wilson
Your gift supports The Electronic Intifada's independent journalism on Palestine: • Donate by credit card, ApplePay, PayPal, Venmo or US bank account via Kindful: https://bit.ly/EIKindful
Gifts are welcome from anywhere and are tax-deductible for US taxpayers as allowed by law.

entry.title: [TLorenz] Hasan Piker Reveals The Next Big Internet Battle
entry.summary:
Hasan Piker On The Terrifying Future Of Internet Surveillance
SUPPORT MY WORK: Buy a paid subscription to my newsletter at usermag.co Support my work on Patreon: http://patreon.com/taylorlorenz
Hasan Piker joined me for this week's Free Speech Friday for a wide-ranging conversation about online censorship, government surveillance, digital ID laws, Section 230, TikTok, KOSA, free speech, the JAWBONE Act, internet privacy, and the growing pressure campaigns targeting creators and political commentators.
We discuss:
• The rise of online censorship around the world • The Kids Online Safety Act (KOSA) • Government surveillance and digital ID requirements • The future of online anonymity • Section 230 and why it matters • TikTok, Big Tech, and content moderation • Government pressure on social media platforms • Free speech on the left and the right • The Jawbone Act • How new internet laws could impact creators, journalists, activists, and everyday users
As governments push new regulations in the name of safety, misinformation, and national security, the debate over free speech, privacy, censorship, and online freedom is becoming more important than ever.
#HasanPiker #FreeSpeech #Censorship #KOSA #Section230 #TikTok #Privacy #Surveillance #InternetFreedom #TechPolicy

entry.title: [RTHINK] US capitulation or tactical deception? Trump doesn't care about the dead girls in Minab #iran #trump
entry.summary:
The Memorandum of Understanding between Iran and the United States has been made public and it uncovers a lot of concessions that the Americans did. Is this MoU a capitulation of the United States in front of the Islamic Republic or just a new tactic of Trump and his administration to fool the Iranians and prepare the ground for new hostilities. When asked about the massacre in Minab, where the US bombed and killed over 160 Iranian school girls, Donald Trump minimised the American war crime and claimed that "wars are nasty". Find out more in today's episode of Radical Thinker!
#iran #iranwar #trump #iranisraelwar #irannews #usa #persiangulf #israel #straitofhormuz #lebanon

entry.title: [DDP] UK Surgeon Prosecuted for Criticising Israel — The Free Speech Crisis Exposed
entry.summary:
A British surgeon prosecuted, suspended & arrested — not for malpractice, but for criticising Israel. Dr Ranjeet Brar & Ryan D expose how the IHRA definition is being weaponised to criminalise dissent.
The IHRA definition of antisemitism was never about protecting Jewish people. According to guests Dr Ranjeet Brar— surgeon, Communist Party of Great Britain (ML) leader, and judicial review applicant — and independent analyst Ryan Dawson, it was designed to conflate a nation-state with an ethnic group, making criticism of Israel's policies legally and professionally dangerous.
Brar details being arrested three times at demonstrations, suspended from surgical duties, and facing two General Medical Council fitness-to-practice investigations — all for speaking against Israeli state violence. Dawson documents a systematic pattern of social media de-platforming, financial censorship, and academic suppression coordinated through organisations like the ADL and Canary Mission across NATO and Five Eyes countries.
This is not a debate about antisemitism. It's a forensic examination of how states weaponize the language of anti-racism to protect the commission of what both guests call genocide — and what happens to those who refuse to stay silent.
**In This Episode:** • Ranjeet Brar's three arrests, GMC investigations & surgical suspension • The origins of the IHRA definition — who created it and why • How the definition was rolled out across UK institutions, police, Labour & the NHS • Cenk Uygur banned from the UK and blocked from Oxford debate • Ryan D on being banned from PayPal, Patreon, YouTube, TikTok & more • Christian Zionism, Peter Thiel, Musk & Zuckerberg's ties to Zionist networks • Why the anti-Muslim hatred definition included free speech protections — and the IHRA didn't • The judicial review challenging IHRA's legality under Article 10 / Human Rights Act • Tommy Robinson, Nigel Farage & who is actually funding the far right • "If you don't have free speech, you can't fix anything downstream from that" • Why Brar argues: real antisemitism is being buried by false accusations
---
🕐 TIMESTAMPS
00:00 – Introduction: Ranjeet Brar & the Cost of Speaking Out 02:15 – What Is the IHRA Definition of Antisemitism? 05:30 – Origins: Who Created It and Why 09:00 – How It Was Rolled Out Across UK Institutions 13:20 – Ranjeet Brar: Arrested Three Times for Palestine Activism 17:45 – The GMC Investigations: "I'm Not Allowed to Save Lives if I Criticise Israel" 22:10 – Cenk Uygur Banned from the UK: A Case Study 25:40 – Ryan D: Banned from Every Platform — Canary Mission & the ADL 31:00 – The Nation vs Ethnicity Confusion: A Simple Trick 35:20 – Zionism Is Not Judaism — A Critical Distinction 39:50 – Who Funds the Far Right? Tommy Robinson, Nigel Farage & Israeli Networks 45:00 – The Judicial Review: Challenging IHRA Under the Human Rights Act 50:30 – Christian Zionism, Big Tech & the Architecture of Censorship 55:00 – "If You Don't Have Free Speech, You Can't Fix Anything" — Closing Arguments
---
👤 GUESTS
**Ranjeet Brar** — Surgeon, Head of the Communist Party of Great Britain (Marxist-Leninist) Ranjeet Brar is a practising NHS surgeon who has been arrested three times at pro-Palestine demonstrations, suspended from his surgical duties, and subjected to two GMC fitness-to-practice investigations — all stemming from his vocal criticism of Israeli state policy. He is currently pursuing a judicial review to challenge the legality of the IHRA definition under Article 10 of the European Convention on Human Rights and the UK Human Rights Act.
**Ryan D** — Independent Geopolitical Analyst & Commentator Returning Deep Dive Perspective guest and one of the most extensively censored independent analysts online. Ryan has been banned from PayPal, Patreon, YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, Discord, Coinbase, Streamlabs, Google Ads, and TikTok, largely through pressure from organisations like the ADL. He holds what he describes as "the longest Canary Mission rap sheet" and continues to produce forensic geopolitical analysis outside mainstream platform control.
---
🔔 Subscribe for long-form geopolitical analysis and independent conversations on global affairs: https://www.youtube.com/@UCROsxSizsKbUzdARaocKDRQ
#IHRA #FreeSpeech #Palestine #Israel #Antisemitism #CriticismOfIsrael #DeepDivePerspective #Geopolitics #Censorship #BDS
📢Disclaimer:
The views and opinions expressed in this video are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of Deep Dive Perspective. The content provided is for informational and entertainment purposes only and should not be construed as professional advice. We encourage viewers to do their own research and form their own opinions.
Deep Dive Perspective assumes no responsibility or liability for any errors or omissions in the content. All information is provided on an as-is basis. Viewer discretion is advised.

A British surgeon prosecuted, suspended & arrested — not for malpractice, but for criticising Israel. Dr Ranjeet Brar & Ryan D expose how the IHRA definition is being weaponised to criminalise dissent.

The IHRA definition of antisemitism was never about protecting Jewish people. According to guests Dr Ranjeet Brar— surgeon, Communist Party of Great Britain (ML) leader, and judicial review applicant — and independent analyst Ryan Dawson, it was designed to conflate a nation-state with an ethnic group, making criticism of Israel's policies legally and professionally dangerous.

Brar details being arrested three times at demonstrations, suspended from surgical duties, and facing two General Medical Council fitness-to-practice investigations — all for speaking against Israeli state violence. Dawson documents a systematic pattern of social media de-platforming, financial censorship, and academic suppression coordinated through organisations like the ADL and Canary Mission across NATO and Five Eyes countries.

This is not a debate about antisemitism. It's a forensic examination of how states weaponize the language of anti-racism to protect the commission of what both guests call genocide — and what happens to those who refuse to stay silent.

**In This Episode:**
• Ranjeet Brar's three arrests, GMC investigations & surgical suspension
• The origins of the IHRA definition — who created it and why
• How the definition was rolled out across UK institutions, police, Labour & the NHS
• Cenk Uygur banned from the UK and blocked from Oxford debate

He holds what he describes as "the longest Canary Mission rap sheet" and continues to produce forensic geopolitical analysis outside mainstream platform control.

entry.title: [GEO] It's official: Iran won the war, and the US lost. This is how
entry.summary:
The US government has signed an agreement to end its war on Iran. It is now widely admitted that Washington lost, and Tehran won. Ben Norton explains why Donald Trump failed, and how this has massive geopolitical implications for the Global South.
Check out our related report on how Iran destroyed US military bases: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=m_wC4KuEcWI

The US government has signed an agreement to end its war on Iran. It is now widely admitted that Washington lost, and Tehran won. Ben Norton explains why Donald Trump failed, and how this has massive geopolitical implications for the Global South.

Check out our related report on how Iran destroyed US military bases: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=m_wC4KuEcWI

entry.title: [SYR] US-Israel Split: New Middle East Map | Ehsan Safarnejad & Radical Thinker
entry.summary:
Is the decades-long US-Israel alliance in the Middle East reaching a breaking point?
In this deep-dive analysis, Ehsan Safarnejad and Radical Thinker join Syriana Analysis to break down the seismic shifts in the Middle East. As geopolitical dynamics shift, we explore whether the U.S. is pivoting its strategy in Lebanon and Syria—and what this means for Netanyahu’s government.
In this video, we discuss:
- The reported "split" between Washington and Tel Aviv. - The "New Middle East Map" and the role of Syria and Lebanon. - How regional players are reacting to the changing U.S. stance. - Strategic implications for the resistance axis and regional stability.
Follow Ehsan Safarnejad on X: https://x.com/Safarnejad_IR/ and check out his channel on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCn-_UlidwJz0jTP1opyZbmw

entry.title: [TLorenz] Why Timothée Chalamet Is Shilling Kalshi
entry.summary:
Why is Kalshi suddenly everywhere?
SUPPORT MY WORK: Buy a paid subscription to my newsletter at usermag.co Support my work on Patreon: http://patreon.com/taylorlorenz
Prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket have become one of the most powerful forces in American media, politics, and culture. In this episode of Power User, I sit down with NPR's Bobby Allyn, the country's top prediction-markets reporter, to break down how Kalshi went from a tiny startup that 50 lawyers refused to represent to an inescapable cultural juggernaut backed by the Trump family, celebrities, and major news organizations.
We dig into how Kalshi exploited a legal loophole to bring sports betting and election betting to every state, why Donald Trump Jr. became an advisor to both Kalshi and Polymarket, and how the companies called the 2024 election for Trump before the mainstream media would, cementing their place in the MAGA universe. Bobby explains the rise of Kalshi CEO Tarek Mansour, Polymarket's offshore "crypto bad boy" founder Shane Coplan, and the regulatory war happening right now inside the CFTC.
We also cover the dark side nobody is talking about: undisclosed influencer marketing flooding your feed, the Timothée Chalamet Kalshi deal, secret ads during the NBA playoffs, the Mr. Beast video editor insider trading scandal, betting markets on wildfires and war, and the casinoification of the entire economy. We dig into the lawsuits, the looming Supreme Court showdown, and whether this whole gambling empire could vanish overnight!
We cover: How Kalshi went from a rejected idea to a multi-billion dollar empire. The shocking court ruling that changed everything in 2024. Why MAGA influencers embraced prediction markets early. The Mr. Beast video editor insider trading scandal. How sports gambling bans were bypassed with a loophole. Timothée Chalamet's controversial Kalshi ad. The media's role in normalizing this "gambling for everything". Why Democrats and Republicans are fighting over regulation. The lawsuits that could end Kalshi. What happens when you can bet on wildfires, wars, and more.

entry.title: [RTHINK] Iran & the US claim to be very close to an agreement, Israel says it will not abide by it #iran #usa
entry.summary:
After Iran threatened to hit Israel hard for disrupting once again the ceasefire by targeting the suburbs of Beirut, the US made more concessions, and announced the end of the naval blockage and the withdrawal of Israeli forces from the areas occupied in South Lebanon. The Israelis announced that they are not part of the deal, and that they don't intend to leave from the occupied territories in Gaza, Lebanon and Syria. The fragile memorandum of understanding is almost dead on arrival, as long as the US doesn't force Israel to back down. Find out more from this episode of Radical Thinker!
#iran #iranwar #trump #iranisraelwar #irannews #usa #persiangulf #israel
entry.content:

entry.title: [SYR] Cold War 2.0 #72 | Humiliated by Trump? Netanyahu May Set the Middle East Ablaze | Tarik Cyril Amar
entry.summary:
Is the new US-Iran preliminary agreement a masterstroke of diplomacy or a strategic trap? Join Syriana Analysis and historian Dr. Tarik Cyril Amar for a "Cold War 2.0" deep dive into the shifting balance of power in the Middle East following the brokered ceasefire between Washington and Tehran.
In this episode, we break down the key elements of the Geneva memorandum, including the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, the lifting of the US naval blockade, and the unfreezing of $24 billion in Iranian assets. We also examine the internal fallout in Israel, where Netanyahu faces mounting criticism for what many call a historic strategic failure.
In this video, we discuss:
- Why the US and Israel failed to achieve their primary war aims. - The "Western Preacher" mindset vs. the reality of regional chess players. - Whether Netanyahu will escalate in Gaza or Lebanon to stay in power. - The role of nuclear deterrence and unconventional warfare. - Turkey’s aggressive rhetoric vs. its actual policy toward Israel.
Follow Tarik's Substack: https://substack.com/@tarikcyrilamar
WATCH more conversations with Tarik in our special playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLiReNnJYf0hLBGZv8NATxsgaOMJ0eifyy

entry.title: [GZ] 'Our only representative is the Resistance': Lebanese rally against negotiations w/ Israel
entry.summary:
The Grayzone's Wyatt Reed reports from the Dahiya district of Beirut, Lebanon, where hundreds of supporters of Hezbollah rallied against the unconstitutional negotiations between the Lebanese government and Israel aimed at compelling Resistance factions to disarm.
The negotiations come as Israel bombards Lebanon's south, killing scores of civilians each week and destroying countless homes, while Iran conditions a deal with the US on a ceasefire in the country.

entry.title: [EI] New survey shows global support for Israel collapsing, with Ali Abunimah
entry.summary:
Trump’s remark lines up with what a new Pew Research Center survey shows: Israel’s global standing is collapsing.
Ali Abunimah, executive director, discusses the findings of the survey of tens of thousands of people across 36 countries.
Meanwhile, Israel and its lobby are responding not by ending the genocide, starvation and massacres, but by pouring record sums into propaganda and possibly more sinister schemes.
This is a segment from The Electronic Intifada's livestream on day 979 of the Gaza genocide, with Ali Abunimah, Nora Barrows-Friedman, Jon Elmer and Asa Winstanley. You can watch the full show here: https://youtube.com/live/oZG8yr7CeL8

entry.title: [RTHINK] Donald Trump claims it has reached a deal with Iran, while pushing Syria to attack Hezbollah #trump
entry.summary:
Donald Trump is yet again claiming that he reached a deal with Iran, and that Iran will accept all the US demands and in essence capitulate. Iran denies that a deal has been reached for now, while Trump is inviting Ahmed al Sharaa al Joulani, the Syrian president, to White House, to discuss most probably a way for Syria to open a new front in Lebanon against Hezbollah. Find out more from this episode of Radical Thinker!
#iran #iranwar #iranisraelwar #irannews #usa #persiangulf #israel #straitofhormuz #lebanon #syria

entry.title: [SYR] I Lived in Lebanon: Here's Why They'll Never Disarm
entry.summary:
In this spontaneous video, I share my deeply personal journey through Lebanon—a country that defined my career and shaped my understanding of Middle Eastern geopolitics.
From my early days as an exchange student in Paris to finding my first professional footing in Beirut during the start of the Syrian war, I discuss the mentors and experiences that made me the journalist I am today. I also delve into the complex, often misunderstood reality of Lebanon's internal divisions, the lack of a unified national identity, and the heavy influence of foreign powers.
In this video, we explore:
- My Journey: How I moved from Aleppo to Beirut and landed my first job in media. - The Beirut Era: Working with Prof. Jamal Wakim and getting published in the legendary As-Safir newspaper. - The "Two Lebanons": The contrast between Beirut’s vibrant nightlife and the intense struggles of the south. - The Geopolitical Core: Why the question of disarmament is more than just political—it's a matter of survival for many in the region. - Identity & Resistance: An analysis of why Lebanon remains fragmented and the role of regional actors like Iran, Syria, and Israel. - Lebanon is a small country with a massive impact on the world stage. Whether you are interested in personal storytelling or high-level political analysis, this video aims to provide a perspective you won't find in mainstream media.
📌Support Syriana Analysis: If you value independent geopolitical analysis, please consider supporting our work so we can continue bringing you these deep dives:
🛡️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join
00:00 - Introduction & A Rainy Day in Berlin 01:23 - Early Years: Studying in Paris & Moving to Lebanon 03:00 - Experience at the University of Balamand 05:25 - Starting a Career in Beirut Media 06:31 - Learning Geopolitics from Dr. Jamal Wakim 08:04 - Publishing My First Article in As-Safir Newspaper 11:53 - Why I Chose My Political Path in Syria 13:24 - The Unique Culture and Nightlife of Beirut 15:55 - The Fragmented National Identity of Lebanon 17:51 - Historical Roots: The Creation of Greater Lebanon 19:14 - Sectarian Democracy and Demographic Shifts 20:54 - Foreign Influence and Political Money in Lebanon 22:23 - Why the South Sees Disarmament as "Suicide" 24:44 - Facing Radical Threats: ISIS, Al-Qaeda, and the Jolani Side 27:42 - Iran’s Role and the New Rules of Engagement 29:06 - Critique of the Current Lebanese Government 31:35 - The Reality of US-Israeli Relations in the Region 33:57 - Lebanon's Geography and Its Place in the World 36:31 - The Role of Hezbollah in the Current Conflict 39:48 - Final Thoughts and Channel Update
📢 Syriana Analysis is an independent platform providing well-researched geopolitical insights, founded by Kevork Almassian. This program provides independent journalistic analysis for educational and documentary purposes, focusing on complex geopolitical shifts in West Asia and globally.
#Lebanon #Geopolitics #MiddleEast #SyrianaAnalysis #Beirut

In this spontaneous video, I share my deeply personal journey through Lebanon—a country that defined my career and shaped my understanding of Middle Eastern geopolitics.

From my early days as an exchange student in Paris to finding my first professional footing in Beirut during the start of the Syrian war, I discuss the mentors and experiences that made me the journalist I am today. I also delve into the complex, often misunderstood reality of Lebanon's internal divisions, the lack of a unified national identity, and the heavy influence of foreign powers.

In this video, we explore:

- My Journey: How I moved from Aleppo to Beirut and landed my first job in media.
- The Beirut Era: Working with Prof. Jamal Wakim and getting published in the legendary As-Safir newspaper.
- The "Two Lebanons": The contrast between Beirut’s vibrant nightlife and the intense struggles of the south.
- The Geopolitical Core: Why the question of disarmament is more than just political—it's a matter of survival for many in the region.
- Identity & Resistance: An analysis of why Lebanon remains fragmented and the role of regional actors like Iran, Syria, and Israel.
- Lebanon is a small country with a massive impact on the world stage. Whether you are interested in personal storytelling or high-level political analysis, this video aims to provide a perspective you won't find in mainstream media.

entry.title: [TLorenz] The Bill That Could Erase LGBTQ Content
entry.summary:
Just in time for Pride month, Marsha Blackburn and the Heritage Foundation's anti-LGBTQ censorship is back (now supported by OpenAI).
SUPPORT MY WORK: Buy a paid subscription to my newsletter at usermag.co Support my work on Patreon: http://patreon.com/taylorlorenz
The Kids Online Safety Act (KOSA) is back, and this time, the White House is reportedly negotiating a deal to pass it in exchange for federal preemption of state AI laws. In this episode of Free Speech Friday, I break down why KOSA is one of the most dangerous internet censorship bills in Congress, why tech billionaires like Sam Altman and Elon Musk actually SUPPORT it, and what the media is getting completely wrong about this so-called "trade-off."
I'm joined by Ari Cohn, one of the top First Amendment lawyers in the country, to explain exactly how KOSA's "duty of care" provision will force platforms to mass-censor content, including LGBTQ voices, mental health support communities, reproductive health information, and more.
We cover the junk science behind the social media moral panic, the age verification privacy nightmare, how the FTC could become the internet's censor-in-chief, and why we've seen this exact panic before with comic books, video games, and television.
Click the link below to take action now!!! https://www.badinternetbills.com
In this video, we cover: What the Kids Online Safety Act actually does How KOSA could impact social media platforms Age verification and online privacy issues The potential effects on LGBTQ communities, mental health resources, and online speech Why Elon Musk and OpenAI support KOSA. The reality of age verification and the death of online privacy. How KOSA targets niche communities and support groups.

entry.title: [SYR] Does Iran Have the Bomb? The New Rules Reshaping the Middle East | Mohammad Marandi
entry.summary:
As the conflict between Iran and Israel enters a dangerous new phase, the question of nuclear deterrence has moved from the shadows to the forefront of global geopolitics. In this LIVE broadcast, Professor Mohammad Marandi joins us to analyze whether the "rules of engagement" have permanently shifted and what this means for the future of the region.
In this stream, we explore:
- Nuclear Ambiguity: Analyzing the recent claims of a potential Iranian nuclear test and the reality of Iran's breakout capacity. - The New Rules of War: How Iran’s conventional strikes have challenged Israeli and US air superiority. - US-Israel Strategic Dilemma: Can Washington actually restrain Tel Aviv, or is a regional escalation now inevitable? - Resistance Strategy: The state of the "Unity of Fields" and the strategic depth of the IRGC in the current crisis.
Mohammad Marandi is a Professor of English Literature and Orientalism at the University of Tehran and a prominent political analyst on West Asian affairs.

entry.title: [DDP] Israel Is Collapsing From Within — Jewish Anti-Zionist Exposes Why
entry.summary:
A Jewish anti-Zionist says Israel is dismantling itself from within — and the evidence is mounting. With Sam Weinstein (IJAN) & Ryan Dawson, we name what mainstream media won't.
Israel's military campaigns across West Asia — Lebanon, Gaza, Iran — aren't projecting strength. They're accelerating a collapse no Western outlet will acknowledge. Sam Weinstein, Coordinator at the International Jewish Anti-Zionist Network (IJAN), argues that every bomb dropped is another nail in Zionism's coffin.
Ryan D. examines the geopolitical mechanics: Iran's red lines, the JCPOA failures, and a US foreign policy vacuum now filled by — in Dawson's words — "two real estate agents from New York."This is not a debate about whether Israel will face accountability. It's a forensic examination of how, when, and from which direction the reckoning is already arriving.
In This Episode: • Iran's red lines violated — and the military responses that followed • Trump's "knock it off" diplomacy and why it's failing in real-time • Sam Weinstein on IJAN's call to dismantle the state of Israel • Why there are more Christian Zionists in the US than Jews in the world • The Strait of Hormuz, Red Sea disruption, and global energy consequences • How social media and TikTok have irreversibly shifted youth opinion • Internal Israeli dissent: draft refusal, BDS, and generational rupture • The replacement of real US diplomats with political amateurs • Iran's resistance economy and asymmetric strategic depth • IJAN's campaign against the Jewish National Fund's charitable status • "Never Again for Anyone" — the Jewish tradition of standing with the oppressed
🕐 TIMESTAMPS: 00:00 – Introduction: Sam Weinstein & the International Jewish Anti-Zionist Network 02:07 – The Global Stakes: West Asia, Hormuz & the World Economy 04:23 – Iran's Red Lines, JCPOA & US Policy in Free Fall 08:48 – Trump, Netanyahu & the Architecture of US-Israel Support 10:00 – Iran's Strategic Gains Despite Overwhelming Aggression 12:27 – "Every Bomb Is A Nail In Zionism's Coffin" — Sam Weinstein 13:29 – Lebanon, the Spread of Conflict & Iran's New Regional Role 16:30 – What Israel Actually Wants: Regional Ambition Exposed 18:41 – The Diplomatic Vacuum: "Two Real Estate Agents from New York" 22:00 – The Jewish Case for Dismantling the State of Israel 24:22 – Judaism Is Not Zionism: A Critical Distinction 30:00 – The Netanyahu Problem — and Why It's Bigger Than One Man 36:00 – The Internet, TikTok & the Collapse of the Narrative 45:00 – BDS, Institutional Targeting & IJAN's Strategy 55:40 – "Never Again for Anyone" — Closing Statement
👤 GUESTS: Sam Weinstein — Coordinator, International Jewish Anti-Zionist Network (IJAN) Sam is a dual UK-US citizen and a leading voice in the global Jewish anti-Zionist movement. IJAN organises Jews in opposition to Zionism, Israel's actions, and the institutions — like the Jewish National Fund — that sustain them. His position: being Jewish means standing with the oppressed, never the oppressor.
Ryan D. — Independent Geopolitical Analyst & Commentator Ryan analyses global conflicts, US foreign policy failures, and the long-term mechanics of empire from outside mainstream media constraints. A returning DDP guest, he brings forensic clarity to the Iran-Israel escalation and the hollowing-out of American diplomacy.
#Israel #Zionism #Palestine #Iran #Gaza #Lebanon #BDS #Geopolitics #DeepDivePerspective #MiddleEast

entry.title: [EAST] World Cup 2026: Geopolitics and the Beautiful Game
entry.summary:
This Guerrilla History series previewing and analyzing the major international football (soccer) tournaments on and off the pitch continues with the disastrous US/Canada/Mexico hosted World Cup 2026. Amid war on Iran, anti-immigrant ICE terror gangs, dismal initial attendance numbers and astronomical costs, this world cup looks to be one of the worst in history. No wonder there have been calls to boycott it and the US. A terrific global panel joins Guerrilla History hosts Adnan and Henry to discuss the geopolitics of the once beautiful game and review the myriad ways this world cup violates the spirit of sport. Gazan journalist Abubaker Abed , Resistance is Fertile podcast co-host Indi.ca, and returning panelist and great friend of both Guerrilla History and the Adnan Husain Show Latin American historian Alex Aviña will break this down. Join us live Tuesday June 9th, 2pmET/7pmDublin/9pmKazan/12amColombo.

entry.title: [RTHINK] Iran and the US exchange blows, while Israel exposes the traitors inside the Lebanese state #iran
entry.summary:
Iran and the US exchanged fire again, after an US Apache helicopter was downed over the Strait of Hormuz by the IRGC navy. The US responded with retaliatory strikes but found itself hit by Iran, who launched ballistic missiles and drones against the American bases in Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain. These skirmishes show that the end of the conflict is far away, and an agreement is far from happening anytime soon. In the meantime, the Israeli press is exposing the traitors inside the Lebanese state, those working of the interests of the US and of Israel. Find out more about it from this episode of Radical Thinker!
#iran #iranwar #iranisraelwar #trump #irannews #usa #persiangulf #israel #straitofhormuz #lebanon

entry.title: [TLorenz] Exposing OpenAI's $125M Secret Meme Army
entry.summary:
Exposing The Dark Money Machine Behind AI Propaganda
SUPPORT MY WORK: Buy a paid subscription to my newsletter at usermag.co Support my work on Patreon: http://patreon.com/taylorlorenz
I break down my investigation into a network of pro-AI and anti-AI meme accounts that I found were secretly being run and funded by OpenAI, Palantir, and Andreessen Horowitz's big $125M super PAC and dark money group.
I reveal how these accounts operated, who is connected to them, why they promoted both sides of the AI debate, and how the organization at the center of the story confirmed key aspects of the reporting after publication.
I talk about how a self-described “Meme Lord” named Jason Levin, founder of Memelord Technologies was hired by the super PAC, Leading the Future, to create and run sock puppet accounts like “DoomersAreDumb” and “Jonathan Doomer” to attack AI critics, mock disabled people, post violent threats, and even pretend to be an anti-AI activist.
OpenAI’s president Greg Brockman donated millions to this campaign. OpenAI’s head of strategy follows these meme accounts. And when confronted, Build American AI confirmed it all.
Topics covered: AI propaganda and influence campaigns OpenAI and AI policy politics Dark money groups and Super PACs Fake activist accounts AI-generated content networks Meme pages and online manipulation Political lobbying and artificial intelligence Social media influence operations Tech industry power and public opinion
#AI #ArtificialIntelligence #TechNews #OpenAI #SiliconValley #MemeCulture #InvestigativeJournalism #InfluenceCampaign #AIDebate #TechPolicy #PowerUser

I talk about how a self-described “Meme Lord” named Jason Levin, founder of Memelord Technologies was hired by the super PAC, Leading the Future, to create and run sock puppet accounts like “DoomersAreDumb” and “Jonathan Doomer” to attack AI critics, mock disabled people, post violent threats, and even pretend to be an anti-AI activist.

entry.title: [SYR] Iran vs Israel: Have the Rules of War Permanently Changed? | Elijah Magnier
entry.summary:
In this deep-dive geopolitical analysis, Elijah Magnier joined Syriana Analysis to break down whether Iran has established new rules of engagement against Israel and the shifting balance of power in the Middle East following recent missile strikes.
Topics covered include:
- Iran's new military doctrine and the "Rules of War." - The Trump & Netanyahu dynamic: Are they on the same page? - Syria's role under Jolani: Will they intervene in Lebanon? - Hezbollah's strategic role as a "hybrid actor" vs. an army. - The truth about IRGC involvement in southern Lebanon.
Elijah J. Magnier is a veteran war correspondent and a senior political risk analyst with over 38 years of experience covering West Asia.
Follow Elijah J. Magnier: https://x.com/ejmalrai Read Elijah J. Magnier's Work: https://ejmagnier.com/

In this deep-dive geopolitical analysis, Elijah Magnier joined Syriana Analysis to break down whether Iran has established new rules of engagement against Israel and the shifting balance of power in the Middle East following recent missile strikes.

Topics covered include:

- Iran's new military doctrine and the "Rules of War."
- The Trump & Netanyahu dynamic: Are they on the same page?
- Syria's role under Jolani: Will they intervene in Lebanon?
- Hezbollah's strategic role as a "hybrid actor" vs. an army.
- The truth about IRGC involvement in southern Lebanon.

Elijah J. Magnier is a veteran war correspondent and a senior political risk analyst with over 38 years of experience covering West Asia.

entry.title: [BE] Response to a Response on Guns, Germs & Steel
entry.summary: I was recently refuted by another YouTuber who responded to my 6 year old video on the very popular book, Guns, Germs & Steel by Jared Diamond. I felt compelled to respond to this response largely because I think it's a good opportunity to discuss historiography (how history is studied) and some things related to it, and also because I was bored. entry.title: [RTHINK] Iran strikes Israel, the War Resumes, IRAN WAR - DAY 101 #iran #Israel #iranwar #lebanon #trump
entry.summary:
Iran hit various targets inside Israel after the IDF unwisely decided to strike the suburbs of Beirut. Previously, the Iranian foreign minister warned that if the IDF will strike Beirut, then Iran will answer and hit Israel. Are these the beginning ours of the second phase of the war, or is just an escalatory flare which will calm down pretty fast? Find out from today's episode of Radical Thinker!
