entry.title: [GZ] Colombia's pro-Trump candidate accused of stealing election
entry.summary:
The Trump administration directly interfered in Colombia's presidential election in favor of Abelardo De La Espriella, a right-wing hardliner, against his leftist opponent, Ivan Cepeda.
The Grayzone's Oscar Leon investigates well-documented charges of electoral fraud which may have tipped the scales for De La Espriella in the first round.
If he wins, De La Espriella has pledged to emulate Trump’s draconian approach toward narco-trafficking, foreshadowing a revival of Plan Colombia, the US-sponsored counter-insurgency program that saw widespread human rights violations including the massacre of rural social leaders, mass displacement and the notorious false positives scandal in a failed war on drugs during the early 2000s.
||| The Grayzone |||
Find more reporting at https://thegrayzone.com

entry.content:
The Trump administration directly interfered in Colombia's presidential election in favor of Abelardo De La Espriella, a right-wing hardliner, against his leftist opponent, Ivan Cepeda.

The Grayzone's Oscar Leon investigates well-documented charges of electoral fraud which may have tipped the scales for De La Espriella in the first round.

If he wins, De La Espriella has pledged to emulate Trump’s draconian approach toward narco-trafficking, foreshadowing a revival of Plan Colombia, the US-sponsored counter-insurgency program that saw widespread human rights violations including the massacre of rural social leaders, mass displacement and the notorious false positives scandal in a failed war on drugs during the early 2000s.

entry.title: [DDP] The Question Nobody Wants to Ask About Israel | Gilad Atzmon
entry.summary:
Gilad Atzmon joins Deep Dive Perspective for a wide-ranging conversation on Israel, Zionism, Jewish identity, political ideology, and the philosophical questions that continue to shape one of the world's most debated conflicts.
Rather than focusing solely on military events or current headlines, this discussion explores deeper questions surrounding identity, nationalism, political movements, historical narratives, and the ideas influencing contemporary debates about Israel and the Middle East.
Whether you agree or disagree with the perspectives presented, this conversation aims to examine arguments that are often absent from mainstream geopolitical discussions.
Guest: Gilad Atzmon International Jazz Musician, Author, and Former IDF Conscript
Subscribe to Deep Dive Perspective for long-form conversations on geopolitics, international affairs, media narratives, global power, and emerging world trends.

entry.title: [SYR] Cold War 2.0 #71 | Rothschild & Kushner: Inside The $5 Billion Albania Takeover | Tarik Cyril Amar
entry.summary:
In this episode of "Cold War 2.0," host Kevork Almassian is joined by historian Dr. Tarik Cyril Amar to unpack the multi-billion-dollar investment plans by Jared Kushner in Albania and their connection to Rothschild and broader Middle East strategy.
What we cover in this stream:
- Is Albania a "Resort" or a private island? We dive into the $5 billion project on Sazan Island and the Zvernec Peninsula. Is this simply luxury tourism, or a new secluded hub for global elites with no accountability?
- The Role of the Rothschilds & Qatar: Exploring the reported involvement of Nat Rothschild and Qatari investors in these massive infrastructure projects.
- The "Integration" of U.S. & Israeli Defense: We analyze the implications of Section 224 of the NDAA, which aims to fuse Israeli power into the American defense apparatus in a way that may be irreversible.
- Germany’s Diplomatic Defeat: Why did the UN General Assembly vote against Germany for a non-permanent Security Council seat? We discuss the fallout of Germany’s recent foreign policy decisions and their impact on international law.
- Iran & The Nuclear Question: Addressing recent rumors and the propaganda war surrounding Iran’s potential nuclear capabilities.
Follow Tarik's Substack: https://substack.com/@tarikcyrilamar

entry.title: [TLorenz] Cops Are Tracking Anyone Who Hates AI
entry.summary:
Are you on a government watchlist just for criticizing AI?
SUPPORT MY WORK: Buy a paid subscription to my newsletter at usermag.co Support my work on Patreon for bonus episodes, monthly Q&A livestreams, and more: http://patreon.com/taylorlorenz
A shocking new investigation from The Intercept reveals that police are tracking seemingly anyone who expresses skepticism or anger about the tech industry online.
In this episode of Free Speech Friday, journalist Sam Biddle joins me to dive deep into a terrifying new report from The Intercept exposing how Philadelphia police officers admitted to monitoring legitimate First Amendment activity. Law enforcement documents show that specialized "Fusion Centers" are scanning social media, message boards, and Facebook to flag users posting anti-AI and anti-data center sentiments.
Sam and I discuss the post-9/11 surveillance apparatus, how local police use tools like Data Miner to monitor online discussions, and why the Trump administration is aligning with tech billionaires and crypto lobbyists to turn AI into a national security priority.
Is your private Signal group chat safe from police infiltration? Why are corporate platforms like Meta incentivized to censor anti-tech activism? We break down the chilling effect of mass surveillance and the elite effort to frame community civics and political advocacy as domestic extremism.
SUPPORT INDEPENDENT JOURNALISM: This channel has ZERO sponsors and relies 100% on viewer support! Keep my reporting alive by subscribing: 👉 Support on Patreon: http://patreon.com/taylorlorenz 👉 Read my Substack Newsletter: https://usermag.co
#Surveillance #BigTech #ArtificialIntelligence #FirstAmendment #FreeSpeech #Privacy #DataCenter #TheIntercept #TechNews #CivilLiberties
entry.content:

In this episode of Free Speech Friday, journalist Sam Biddle joins me to dive deep into a terrifying new report from The Intercept exposing how Philadelphia police officers admitted to monitoring legitimate First Amendment activity. Law enforcement documents show that specialized "Fusion Centers" are scanning social media, message boards, and Facebook to flag users posting anti-AI and anti-data center sentiments.

Sam and I discuss the post-9/11 surveillance apparatus, how local police use tools like Data Miner to monitor online discussions, and why the Trump administration is aligning with tech billionaires and crypto lobbyists to turn AI into a national security priority.

Is your private Signal group chat safe from police infiltration? Why are corporate platforms like Meta incentivized to censor anti-tech activism? We break down the chilling effect of mass surveillance and the elite effort to frame community civics and political advocacy as domestic extremism.

entry.title: [GZ] “We will never leave”: Defying Israeli ethnic cleansing in South Lebanon
entry.summary:
In his latest on the ground report for The Grayzone, Wyatt Reed travelled to the ancient city of Tyre to document the aftermath of a brutal wave of Israeli strikes on residential buildings and local businesses.
Despite Israel’s claims that their attacks target Hezbollah, he found no evidence of militant activity. Instead, he only encountered civilians attempting to rebuild their lives — and, in many cases, refusing to obey a forced evacuation notice imposed by Israeli forces seeking to ethnically cleanse southern Lebanon.

entry.title: [GEO] Before the AI bubble pops, billionaires want to take your retirement funds
entry.summary:
The AI bubble has caused the most concentrated and overvalued stock market in US history. The billionaire CEOs of Big Tech corporations fear it may pop soon, so they changed the rules on Wall Street so that, mere days after their IPOs, SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic will be given "fast track" inclusion in major index funds, despite the fact that they are losing tons of money.
These corporate oligarchs want to use average retail investors as exit liquidity, looting pensions, 401(k)s, and other retirement funds, all so Elon Musk can become the world's first trillionaire. Ben Norton explains the scandal.

entry.title: [TLorenz] Is AI Destroying Our Environment?
entry.summary:
Are AI data centers destroying the environment, driving up power bills, and consuming massive amounts of water, or is the backlash overblown?
SUPPORT MY WORK: Buy a paid subscription to my newsletter at usermag.co Support my work on Patreon for bonus episodes, monthly Q&A livestreams, and more: http://patreon.com/taylorlorenz
In this episode of Power User, I sit down with Wired senior climate and energy reporter Molly Taft to unpack the AI data center boom. We discuss how AI infrastructure actually works, why companies like OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, and xAI are racing to build more data centers, and what the environmental consequences could be.
We dive into the controversy around electricity demand, fossil fuel expansion, water use, local community opposition, AI infrastructure, data center moratoriums, and whether these facilities are genuinely necessary for the future of artificial intelligence.
Topics covered: What AI data centers actually do Why ChatGPT and AI require so much compute The environmental impact of AI Energy consumption and power grids Natural gas, nuclear, and renewable energy Elon Musk's xAI Colossus project Water usage and cooling systems Community backlash against data centers Climate concerns and AI development The future of AI infrastructure
If you've been seeing headlines about AI data centers, energy crises, environmental impacts, or the race between OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, and China, this conversation breaks down what's really happening. #AI #DataCenters #ChatGPT #OpenAI #ArtificialIntelligence #ClimateChange #Technology #TechNews #Energy #Environment

entry.title: [DDP] The Next Middle East War May Already Have Started
entry.summary:
The conflict involving Israel, Lebanon and Iran continues to raise questions about the possibility of a wider regional confrontation.
In this episode of Deep Dive Perspective, journalist and media analyst Laith Marouf joins us to discuss developments on the Israel–Lebanon front, the role of Hezbollah, the strategic calculations of Iran, and the broader implications for regional stability.
We explore military developments, political narratives, international diplomacy, the role of the United States, and what a prolonged conflict could mean for the future of the Middle East.
Whether you agree or disagree with the perspectives discussed, this conversation aims to examine the forces shaping one of the most consequential geopolitical crises of our time.
📌 Chapters: 00:00 Introduction 00:52 Why Israel Keeps Returning to Lebanon 01:59 Battlefield Reality vs Political Narrative 04:43 The Israeli Military and the Question of Morale 09:52 Escalation and Civilian Displacement 11:42 The Wider Regional Stakes 18:11 Iran, Hezbollah and Ceasefire Dynamics 23:59 Global Power and Political Influence 28:32 The Future Beyond the Conflict 32:29 Democracy, Power and Political Systems 38:32 The Future of the Resistance Axis 46:20 Closing Thoughts
About the Guest: Laith Marouf is a Canadian journalist, media consultant and political commentator. He founded Free Palestine TV in 2023 and has worked extensively in community media, communications strategy and international affairs. He currently serves as a Senior Consultant at the Community Media Advocacy Centre and regularly provides analysis on Middle East politics, media narratives and geopolitical developments.

entry.title: [BE] Myths of the Bosnian War
entry.summary:
The Bosnian War of 1992-1995 is one of the most misunderstood modern historical events. In this video I set the record straight. Support me on Patreon: https://patreon.com/BadEmpanada Subscribe to my Substack: https://ofpatmos.substack.com/ One-time donations: https://ko-fi.com/BadEmpanada Second Channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCjY9R3_6sxfUViabj93Vnhw Follow me on Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/badempanada.com
Sources (in order of first appearance) [1] Burn This House: The Making and Unmaking of Yugoslavia, Jasminka Udovicki & James Ridgeway. [2] https://web.archive.org/web/20111120214424/http://www.ushmm.org/wlc/en/article.php?ModuleId=10007332 [3] https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/nationalities-papers/article/abs/anticipating-the-disintegration-nationalisms-in-former-yugoslavia-19801990/3843E2823CDB1F87A857BBECE64B8A01 [4] Economic reasons for the break-up of Yugoslavia, Viachaslau Yarashevich & Yuliya Karneyevab [5] https://www.theguardian.com/world/from-the-archive-blog/2019/mar/20/how-milosevic-stripped-kosovos-autonomy-archive-1989 [6] https://worldhistorycommons.org/serbian-academy-arts-and-sciences-sanu-memorandum-1986 [7] https://iwpr.net/global-voices/milosevic-ally-defends-belgrade-takeover-kosovo [8] Balkan holocausts?, David Bruce MacDonald. [9] https://www.icty.org/x/cases/karadzic/tjug/en/160324_judgement.pdf [10] https://hrcak.srce.hr/file/151907 [11] https://www.hlc-rdc.org/en/publications/dossier-the-jna-in-the-wars-in-croatia-and-bih/ [12] https://www.icty.org/x/cases/tadic/tjug/en/tad-tsojmcd970507e.htm [13] https://www.icty.org/en/content/prosecution-case-croatia-and-bosnia-and-herzegovina [14] https://www.icj-cij.org/index.php/node/140381 [15] https://asil.org/insights/volume-11-issue-9/ [16] The Issue of Genocidal Intent and Denial of Genocide: A Case Study of Bosnia and Herzegovina, Edina Becirevic. [17] https://www.cambridge.org/core/books/abs/western-intervention-in-the-balkans/bosnia/5712288681DD1BC9E4D18F279D585AD3 [18] https://pesd.princeton.edu/node/701 [19] Islam and Bosnia Conflict Resolution and Foreign Policy in Multi-Ethnic States, Maya Shatzmiller. [20] https://www.haguejusticeportal.net/Docs/HJJ-JJH/Vol_2(3)/The%20Foca_Fiori_EN.pdf [21] https://www.upi.com/Archives/1991/09/25/Security-Council-bans-arms-sales-to-Yugoslavia/2070685771200/ [22] Postwar: A History of Europe Since 1945, Tony Judt. [23] https://www.obscuredbalkans.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/OB2024KK-173-179.pdf [24] “Celebrating” Srebrenica Genocide: Impunity and Indoctrination as Contributing Factors to the Glorification of Mass Atrocities, Olivera Simic. [25] Intervention in Internal Conflict: The Case of Bosnia, Steven L. Burg. [28] Addressing a “Tragic Legacy”: The Dayton Peace Accords [29] https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/8/6/separatist-bosnian-serb-leader-dodik-sacked-from-office-by-bosnia-officials [30] https://www.pbs.org/wgbh/frontline/article/timeline-ratko-mladic-and-his-role-in-war-crimes-during-the-bosnian-war/ [31] https://www.icty.org/x/cases/karadzic/trans/en/100301IT.htm [32] https://www.nbcnews.com/id/wbna35638938

entry.title: [DDP] Ken Loach: Why the Media Still Gets Palestine Wrong
entry.summary:
Why does coverage of Palestine remain one of the most contested issues in modern journalism? Ken Loach joins Deep Dive Perspective to discuss media narratives, international law, and public opinion.
Award-winning filmmaker and long-time Palestine advocate Ken Loach joins Deep Dive Perspective for a wide-ranging discussion on media coverage of Palestine, international law, public opinion, and the role of Western political institutions.
🔍The conversation explores:
✔️Media narratives around Palestine ✔️Reporting on Gaza ✔️Language and framing in journalism ✔️International law and accountability ✔️The role of the BBC and Western media ✔️Political responses in the UK ✔️The future of international institutions ✔️Public opinion and social media
Guests Info: Ken Loach is one of Britain's most acclaimed filmmakers, known for award-winning films including Kes, I, Daniel Blake, and The Wind That Shakes the Barley. Throughout his career, he has combined filmmaking with political activism, becoming one of the UK's most prominent voices on social justice, workers' rights, anti-war movements, and Palestinian solidarity.
🔔 Subscribe for long-form geopolitical analysis and independent conversations on global affairs: https://www.youtube.com/@UCROsxSizsKbUzdARaocKDRQ
📌 WATCH NOW: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEq0jQ5NLgh_-RltiA-LSRCt https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEoqX56Fu2UbcO5_cEXf6tIC https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEqd42sdRwAXhuQbbGY22Bn0
Timestamps and Highlights: 00:00 Introduction 01:47 Palestine and Media Narratives 05:37 How Western Media Frames Gaza 09:26 October 7 and Competing Narratives 13:45 Language, Journalism, and Public Opinion 20:41 Keir Starmer, Labour, and Palestine 27:58 The Crisis of the Political Left 32:25 International Law and Accountability 34:12 Can the United Nations Be Reformed? 38:45 The Future of Palestine and Global Politics 41:21 Closing Remarks
A documentary-style conversation examining how stories are told, who tells them, and what impact that has on public understanding of global events.
#Palestine #KenLoach #Media #Gaza #Geopolitics #DeepDivePerspective
📢Disclaimer:
The views and opinions expressed in this video are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of Deep Dive Perspective. The content provided is for informational and entertainment purposes only and should not be construed as professional advice. We encourage viewers to do their own research and form their own opinions.
Deep Dive Perspective assumes no responsibility or liability for any errors or omissions in the content. All information is provided on an as-is basis. Viewer discretion is advised.

entry.title: [TLorenz] You Are One Click Away From Being Debanked
entry.summary:
What if you woke up tomorrow and completely lost access to your bank account, credit cards, PayPal, and Venmo, all because of something you posted online?
SUPPORT MY WORK: Buy a paid subscription to my newsletter at usermag.co Support my work on Patreon for bonus episodes, monthly Q&A livestreams, and more: http://patreon.com/taylorlorenz
In this episode of Free Speech Friday, Rainey Reitman, advisor at the Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF) and author of the new book "Transaction Denied" joins me to dive deep into the terrifying reality of financial censorship and how major banks and payment processors are quietly becoming the ultimate gatekeepers of internet speech.
Reitman reveals how a handful of massive financial institutions are bypassing the First Amendment to act as privatized censors, deciding what journalism you can support, what political nonprofits you can donate to, and what ideas are considered "misinformation." From the early financial blockade of WikiLeaks to recent debanking scandals involving independent journalists and nonpartisan advocacy groups, this hidden digital infrastructure controls your life more than you realize.
SUPPORT INDEPENDENT JOURNALISM: This channel has ZERO sponsors, and speaking out on financial censorship isn't advertiser-friendly. If you get value from this show, support my work so I can keep making these videos!
👉 Support on Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/taylorlorenz 👉 Support on Substack: https://www.usermag.co/subscribe
GET THE BOOK: 👉 Pick up a copy of Rainey Reitman's "Transaction Denied" here: https://www.penguinrandomhouse.com/books/804543/transaction-denied-by-rainey-reitman/ *(Note: Half of all book proceeds go directly to the Freedom of the Press Foundation)*
We discuss: The Nightmare of Financial Censorship How Payment Processors Got So Much Power The Rise of E-Commerce & Speech Dependency PayPal, WikiLeaks, and Chelsea Manning Censorship by Proxy: How the Government Pressures Banks The Visa Lawsuit That Could Ruin the Internet

entry.title: [RTHINK] Donald Trump threatens to blow up Oman, and why Iran should boycott the FIFA World Cup #worldcup
entry.summary:
Donald Trump has threatened to blow up the Sultanate of Oman, if it moves further with the plan of controlling the Strait of Hormuz together with Iran. Trump tries to force Oman to back down, although even if this happens, Iran will still control by itself the Strait of Hormuz. In this episode I also discuss why Iran should boycott the FIFA World Cup, co-hosted by the US, and why many other countries, including Russia and China should also boycott this tournament.
#iran #iranwar #oman #trump #worldcup #boycott #iranisraelwar #irannews #radicalthinker #geopolitics

entry.title: [TLorenz] Billionaires Are Funding 'Anti AI' Content
entry.summary:
The Hidden Billionaire AI War
Have you noticed your social media feeds suddenly flooding with terrifying content about AI existential risk? From TikTok clips warning about "AI psychosis" to viral YouTube shorts claiming chatbots are pushing people to the brink, the anti-AI movement has taken over the internet... But what if these "anti-AI" influencers aren't actually what they seem?
SUPPORT MY WORK: Buy a paid subscription to my newsletter at usermag.co Support my work on Patreon for bonus episodes, monthly Q&A livestreams, and more: http://patreon.com/taylorlorenz
In this week's episode of Power User, Washington Post journalist Natasha Tiku join me to pull back the curtain on the massive, billionaire-funded "anti-AI" astroturf campaign. We track the money and expose how the exact same millionaires and billionaires building the world's largest AI companies (like OpenAI and Anthropic) are quietly paying influencers thousands of dollars to promote AI doomsday scenarios.
We dive deep into the 25-year history of the AI safety movement, from Eliezer Yudkowski’s rationalist blogs and Harry Potter fan fiction to Effective Altruism and the "paperclip maximizer" theory. We talk about how Silicon Valley elites are co-opting VERY genuine public anxieties about jobs and real-world harms related to AI technology in order to push their own pro-AI, anti-regulatory agendas.
We also talk about how to spot the hidden corporate pipelines, dark money nonprofits, and staged research papers designed to control the narrative around AI. If you're seeing AI doomer videos all over your feed, please watch this one!
✨ SUPPORT THE CHANNEL: 👉 Substack Newsletter: http://usermag.co 👉 Patreon: patreon.com/taylorlorenz Nitasha's WaPo story on all this: https://www.washingtonpost.com/technology/2026/04/18/ai-doom-influencers-safety/

entry.title: [SYR] Iran’s Final Move: Redrawing the Middle East Map Forever | Ehsan Safarnejad
entry.summary:
In this deep dive on Syriana Analysis, we are joined by Ehsan Safarnejad from Iran to examine the profound and irreversible shifts currently redrawing the geopolitical map of the Middle East. Our conversation explores the strategic maneuvers through the Strait of Hormuz, the nuclear program, and the long-term vision that have allowed Iran to fundamentally alter the regional power balance, effectively challenging decades of established American dominance. Ehsan Safarnejad provides an expert perspective on why these changes are permanent and what they signify for the future of international relations and regional stability.

entry.title: [TLorenz] The Hidden Ideology Running Silicon Valley
entry.summary:
Elon Musk has evolved far beyond a billionaire CEO.
SUPPORT MY WORK: Buy a paid subscription to my newsletter at usermag.co Support my work on Patreon for bonus episodes, monthly Q&A livestreams, and more: http://patreon.com/taylorlorenz
According to political theorists Quinn Slobodian and Ben Tarnoff, Musk has created an entirely new ideology called “Muskism” — a system where technology companies merge with state power, AI shapes politics, and billionaires become more powerful than governments.
In this episode of Free Speech Friday, I sit down with Quinn Slobodian, co-author of the new book Muskism, to break down how Elon Musk’s worldview went from Silicon Valley optimism to tech authoritarianism. We discuss Tesla, SpaceX, Starlink, Neuralink, AI censorship, OpenAI, Grok, surveillance, DOGE, data centers, Silicon Valley’s shift toward defense tech, and why Musk’s influence now reaches into every part of society.
We also unpack: Elon Musk’s political transformation The rise of Muskism AI paranoia and “woke AI” Why Silicon Valley is embracing militarization Musk’s obsession with reproduction and population decline How SpaceX and Starlink could reshape global power The future of AI and surveillance capitalism Why tech billionaires are becoming impossible to escape
entry.content:

We also unpack:
Elon Musk’s political transformation
The rise of Muskism
AI paranoia and “woke AI”
Why Silicon Valley is embracing militarization
Musk’s obsession with reproduction and population decline
How SpaceX and Starlink could reshape global power
The future of AI and surveillance capitalism
Why tech billionaires are becoming impossible to escape

entry.title: [TLorenz] You Are About to Become Economically Worthless
entry.summary:
What happens if AI becomes smarter than humans at most jobs?
SUPPORT MY WORK: Buy a paid subscription to my newsletter at usermag.co Support my work on Patreon for bonus episodes, monthly Q&A livestreams, and more: http://patreon.com/taylorlorenz
Researchers, economists, and top AI executives are increasingly warning about the rise of a “permanent underclass," a future where millions of workers lose not just their jobs, but their economic value altogether. In this episode of Power User, I sit down with writer Jasmine Sun to unpack the terrifying idea that artificial intelligence could permanently reshape class, wealth, labor, and power in America.
We discuss OpenAI, Anthropic, AGI, automation, white collar layoffs, Silicon Valley ideology, AI job displacement, universal basic income, data center protests, populism, and whether society is prepared for what’s coming next.
Could AI destroy upward mobility? Will automation create mass unemployment? Are tech companies being honest about the future they’re building?
We cover: The permanent underclass theory explained Who is actually pushing this idea OpenAI's shifting stance on job displacement Leading the Future and AI lobbying Why AI is different from past tech hype Policy failures and democratic backlash What China is doing differently What workers should actually do now
#AI #Tech #Technology #ArtificialIntelligence #FutureOfWork #AGI #OpenAI #Anthropic #SiliconValley #TechNews #JobAutomation #AIJobs #TechIndustry

entry.title: [DDP] Israel Hijacked 22 Ships. The UN Called It Illegal. Nobody Did Anything.
entry.summary:
What really happened during the Gaza flotilla interception in the Mediterranean?
In this episode of Deep Dive Perspective, flotilla participants and commentators discuss the maritime operation, the detention of activists, the geopolitical implications of the Gaza blockade, and the wider regional tensions involving Israel, Iran, Lebanon, and the international community.
🔍The conversation explores:
✔️the Gaza flotilla mission ✔️maritime law and international waters ✔️detention and treatment of participants ✔️media narratives surrounding Gaza ✔️international law and geopolitical power ✔️the broader Middle East conflict ✔️resistance movements and global reactions
Featuring first-hand accounts and geopolitical analysis, this discussion examines one of the most controversial international incidents connected to the ongoing Gaza crisis.
Guests Info: Fra Hughes: Journalist, Author and Activist Tadhg Hickey: Irish comedian, Writer, Filmmaker

entry.title: [TLorenz] The AI Backlash Nobody Saw Coming
entry.summary:
Thanks to Monarch for partnering with me! Start your free trial and get 50% off your first year of total money clarity using my link https://monarchmoney.yt.link/NgYhRTY or code tl50 for 50% Off Monarch Core tier.
What’s really driving the growing backlash against AI and Silicon Valley?
From burning data centers to celebrating an attack on Sam Altman’s house, the anti‑tech backlash has escalated. In this episode of Free Speech Friday, I sit down with journalist and author Brian Merchant to unpack the rise of anti AI sentiment, the growing anger toward Big Tech, and why public hostility toward companies like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google keeps escalating.
We discuss: Why the AI backlash exploded so quickly The connection between AI fear and distrust in capitalism Why people are protesting data centers The rise of anti tech politics AI doomers and effective altruism Sam Altman and Silicon Valley PR failures Whether AI companies created their own crisis How automation fears are shaping public opinion Why people feel powerless against Big Tech The future of AI regulation and resistance
Brian Merchant is the author of Blood in the Machine, a book about the original Luddites and the history of anti technology movements.
If you’re interested in AI, ChatGPT, Silicon Valley, automation, tech criticism, labor politics, or the future of the internet, we break down the cultural and political forces shaping the AI era.
Subscribe for more interviews and deep dives on AI, internet culture, media, tech, and politics.

entry.title: [DDP] Inside Iran: What the West Still Doesn’t Understand About This War
entry.summary:
Iran and the United States are moving deeper into one of the most dangerous geopolitical confrontations in decades — but how is the conflict being viewed from inside Iran itself?
In this episode of Deep Dive Perspective, geopolitical analyst Rayan D. and Dr. Setareh Sadeqi of the University of Tehran discuss the Iran–US crisis, media narratives, propaganda warfare, sanctions, regional escalation, and how ordinary Iranians are reacting to growing tensions with Washington and Israel.
GUEST INFO: Rayan D.: Geopolitical analyst and author Dr. Setareh Sadeqi: Assistant Professor at the University of Tehran's Faculty of World Studies
🔔 Subscribe for long-form geopolitical analysis and independent conversations on global affairs: https://www.youtube.com/@UCROsxSizsKbUzdARaocKDRQ
📌 WATCH NOW: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEq0jQ5NLgh_-RltiA-LSRCt https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEoqX56Fu2UbcO5_cEXf6tIC https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEqd42sdRwAXhuQbbGY22Bn0
The conversation explores:
US–Iran relations ✔️information warfare and propaganda ✔️media narratives around Iran ✔️the role of social media and cultural influence ✔️sanctions and their impact on Iranian society ✔️generational shifts inside Iran ✔️geopolitics of the Middle East ✔️ideology, resistance, and international power
Rather than headlines and soundbites, this discussion focuses on long-form analysis, historical context, and perspectives rarely heard in Western media coverage.
Timestamps
00:00 Introduction 01:10 Trump, Iran, and political instability 06:20 Media narratives and propaganda wars 12:00 Information warfare and public perception 20:00 Social media, culture, and Iran’s younger generation 29:00 TikTok, censorship, and narrative control 35:00 Iranian identity and resistance culture 44:00 The American Dream vs modern geopolitical reality 47:00 Shia political philosophy, dignity, and justice 56:00 Final thoughts on power, media, and the future
#Iran #USA #Geopolitics #MiddleEast #IranUSA #InternationalRelations #DeepDivePerspective
This episode features a panel discussion with multiple participants, appearing in a unique split screen video format. The conversation touches on the ongoing situation in Iran and the broader Middle East war, with discussions on the complexities of the iran war and the potential for an israel iran war. Ryan Dawson, a frequent guest, joins us from a remote location, alongside other participants in Isfahan and the far east.

entry.title: [GEO] This is the REAL reason for Trump's visit to China
entry.summary:
Donald Trump took a trip to China to meet President Xi Jinping. He was joined by the CEOs of a dozen top US corporations, including billionaires Elon Musk, Jensen Huang of Nvidia, Tim Cook of Apple, Larry Fink of BlackRock, and Stephen Schwarzman of Blackstone.
Ben Norton explains how Beijing won the trade war that was started by Washington, how China has significant leverage (in manufacturing, supply chains, critical minerals, and rare earths), and how the US economy is in a very bad state.

entry.title: [TLorenz] The Ben Shapiro Era Is Finally Over
entry.summary:
Ben Shapiro’s Media Empire Is Collapsing
Support my independent journalism: 🙏 Patreon: ⁠https://www.patreon.com/cw/taylorlorenz ⁠ 🗞️ Buy a paid subscription to my Substack: ⁠https://www.usermag.co
For the last decade, Ben Shapiro and The Daily Wire dominated the digital landscape. From Facebook to chart-topping podcasts, they seemed untouchable. But in 2025, the data tells a different story. With a 90% drop in YouTube views, massive layoffs, and the high-profile exit of CEO Jeremy Boreing, the "Conservative Disney" empire is crashing and burning.
The Bulwark's Will Sommer joins me on this week's episode of Power User to dive deep into the rise and fall of Ben Shapiro. We break down the explosive internal feud with Candace Owens, the catastrophic failure of his Hollywood ambitions, how a changing Facebook algorithm killed their traffic overnight, and what his fall reveals about the future of conservative media.
We cover: The Daily Wire layoffs and financial struggles. Why Ben Shapiro is losing his audience to creators like Candace Owens and Tucker Carlson. The failure of "Anti-Woke" entertainment and the "Conservative Hollywood" dream. How the death of Charlie Kirk created a massive fracture in the MAGA movement. Why Ben Shapiro is irrelevant to Gen Z and Gen Alpha.
#tech #internet #media #technology

entry.title: [TLorenz] Stop Feeling Helpless About AI
entry.summary:
What if everything you've been told about technological progress is a lie?
Support my independent journalism: 🙏 Patreon: ⁠https://www.patreon.com/cw/taylorlorenz ⁠ 🗞️ Buy a paid subscription to my Substack: ⁠https://www.usermag.co
AI is changing our lives faster than ever, but resistance to technology is not new. For this week's Free Speech Friday I spoke with academic Thomas Dekeyser, author of the new book Techno-Negative: A Long History of Refusing the Machine, about the history of anti-technology movements, from ancient Greece to Luddites smashing machines in the Industrial Revolution to anarchist groups bombing computer companies in the 1980s.
We discuss AI, labor automation, surveillance, moral panic, Silicon Valley ideology, tech CEOs, machine breaking, environmental destruction, and why backlash against technology keeps returning throughout history. We also explore whether technological progress actually benefits ordinary people, or mainly serves corporations and power. Sometimes it's not what you think!
Topics include:
Why anti-AI movements are growing Big Tech and labor exploitation AI moral panic and conspiracy theories Environmental costs of AI Surveillance and control The politics of technological progress Why people resist new technology Silicon Valley and deregulation
#AI #BigTech #Technology #SamAltman #OpenAI #Luddites #TechCriticism #SiliconValley #ChatGPT #AIEthics #Podcast #TechPodcast #TechHistory #Resistance #Anarchism #Capitalism #Surveillance #DataCenters #LaborAutomation #FutureOfWork

entry.title: [TLorenz] Debunking CNN's Viral 62 Million Men Hoax
entry.summary:
The Lie Created To Strip Your Rights Online
Support my independent journalism: 🙏 Patreon: ⁠https://www.patreon.com/cw/taylorlorenz ⁠ 🗞️ Buy a paid subscription to my Substack: ⁠https://www.usermag.co
In March, a CNN investigation into a "Global Academy" of 62 million men allegedly drugging and assaulting women broke the internet. But the most viral story of the year was built on a foundation of misleading statistics and bad journalism.
Journalist Kat Tenbarge joins me to debunk the "62 Million Men" narrative. We dig into how this story was used to manufacture consent for dangerous new censorship laws and who is actually behind the movement to repeal Section 230.
In this video, we cover: - Why the "62 million" figure is a misleading traffic stat, not a count of criminals. - The connection between CNN’s reporting and far-right Christian nationalist groups like Exodus Cry. - How the "Global Academy" branding was manufactured from a single interview quote. - The real-world harm of FOSTA-SESTA and why new "safety" bills like KOSA actually endanger victims. - How mainstream media organizations lobby against free speech to protect their own business interests.
Don't let emotional propaganda dictate tech policy. We look at the systemic failures of the legal system and why villainizing platforms instead of perpetrators is a losing battle for women's rights. If you’ve seen the “62 million men” claim, you need to watch this.

- The connection between CNN’s reporting and far-right Christian nationalist groups like Exodus Cry.
- How the "Global Academy" branding was manufactured from a single interview quote.
- The real-world harm of FOSTA-SESTA and why new "safety" bills like KOSA actually endanger victims.

entry.title: [SYR] Historian Dismantle the “Trump 4D Chess” Theory on Iran
entry.summary:

In this segment, Tarik Cyril Amar dismantles the theory that Washington is secretly executing a flawless long-term plan in West Asia through the UAE, energy corridors, and controlled destabilization. He argues that this theory ignores the obvious: Trump is irrational, U.S. policy is deeply chaotic, Israel exerts enormous influence, and the war has damaged American credibility rather than strengthened it.

entry.title: [DDP] What Syria Can Teach Us About Iran — Dr. Piers Robinson
entry.summary:
They said Syria used chemical weapons. The OPCW's own scientists now say the evidence was manipulated. They're making the same claims about Iran. Dr. Piers Robinson has spent years documenting exactly how this is done.
A researcher who works alongside former OPCW Director-General Jose Bustani — the man John Bolton had removed from his post before the Iraq invasion — Robinson lays out a forensic case that the same institutional machinery used to justify the Syria intervention has been reactivated against Iran. The OPCW's Douma and Ghouta investigations were contested from the inside: internal whistleblowers leaked documents to WikiLeaks, testified publicly in Brussels in 2019, and were corroborated by mainstream journalists including Peter Hitchens. The official story didn't hold — and almost nobody covered it.
Now the IAEA is making assessments about Iran's nuclear programme. The UN is providing the political framework. The same playbook. The same institutions. The same pattern — one episode earlier in the timeline.
🔍 In This Episode: ✔️The OPCW: what it was built to do — and what it became ✔️Jose Bustani removed by John Bolton before Iraq — and what that tells you about institutional independence ✔️Ghouta 2013 and Douma 2018: what the OPCW whistleblowers actually said ✔️The White Helmets — UK-government-linked intelligence on the ground in Syria ✔️The WikiLeaks OPCW documents and the 2019 Brussels testimony nobody reported ✔️The IAEA's double standard: Iran's alleged programme vs. Israel's undeclared nuclear arsenal ✔️The UN's structural inability to restrain the most powerful states — and its resemblance to the League of Nations ✔️Wesley Clark's "seven countries in five years" — and where Iran fits in that sequence ✔️Whether these institutions can be reformed — or whether the BRICS/Eurasian alternative architecture is the only viable path ✔️What ordinary people can do when the watchdogs have become attack dogs
🎙️ Guest: Dr. Piers Robinson — Academic Researcher & Author (writing on OPCW investigations in Syria; collaborator with Hans von Sponik & Jose Bustani). Expert specializing in propaganda, international relations, global conflicts, and the integrity of international oversight institutions.
In this episode, Dr. Piers Robinson joins us to discuss chemical weapons, propaganda, and the ongoing push for regime change. We explore how these factors shape international relations, particularly concerning Iran and the broader Middle East, offering a deep dive into foreign policy.
🔔 Subscribe for the geopolitical analysis the mainstream won't commission | 👍 Like if this changed how you see the institutions claiming authority over the Iran conflict | 💬 Share with someone who still trusts the IAEA's Iran assessments
🔔 Subscribe for more unfiltered geopolitical analysis: https://www.youtube.com/@UCROsxSizsKbUzdARaocKDRQ
Timestamps and Highlights: 00:00 - Introduction: The Global Security Crisis 02:22 - The Creation and Role of the OPCW 04:37 - Fact-Finding Missions and Global Institutions 06:11 - Institutional Whistleblowers and The Hidden Truth 09:23 - How Major Powers Influence Global Institutions 11:06 - Diplomatic Pressures and Global Treaties 14:19 - The Crisis of Independence in Oversight Bodies 18:06 - Non-Proliferation, Geopolitics, and Accountability 22:15 - Media Influence and Manufacturing Consent 28:31 - Public Health, Policy, and Global Control 31:15 - Trust Issues: Why the Public is Waking Up 36:10 - Future of Geopolitics: Multipolarity and BRICS 39:31 - Nuclear Diplomacy and the Shifting Balance 43:00 - Conclusion: The Need for Institutional Reform
#Geopolitics #GlobalInstitutions #HiddenTruth #DeepDivePerspective #Crisis #InternationalRelations #iran

The views and opinions expressed in this video are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of Deep Dive Perspective. entry.content:
They said Syria used chemical weapons. The OPCW's own scientists now say the evidence was manipulated. They're making the same claims about Iran. Dr. Piers Robinson has spent years documenting exactly how this is done.

A researcher who works alongside former OPCW Director-General Jose Bustani — the man John Bolton had removed from his post before the Iraq invasion — Robinson lays out a forensic case that the same institutional machinery used to justify the Syria intervention has been reactivated against Iran. The OPCW's Douma and Ghouta investigations were contested from the inside: internal whistleblowers leaked documents to WikiLeaks, testified publicly in Brussels in 2019, and were corroborated by mainstream journalists including Peter Hitchens. The official story didn't hold — and almost nobody covered it.

Now the IAEA is making assessments about Iran's nuclear programme. The UN is providing the political framework. The same playbook. The same institutions. The same pattern — one episode earlier in the timeline.

🔍 In This Episode:
✔️The OPCW: what it was built to do — and what it became
✔️Jose Bustani removed by John Bolton before Iraq — and what that tells you about institutional independence
✔️Ghouta 2013 and Douma 2018: what the OPCW whistleblowers actually said
✔️The White Helmets — UK-government-linked intelligence on the ground in Syria
✔️The WikiLeaks OPCW documents and the 2019 Brussels testimony nobody reported
✔️The IAEA's double standard: Iran's alleged programme vs. Israel's undeclared nuclear arsenal
✔️The UN's structural inability to restrain the most powerful states — and its resemblance to the League of Nations
✔️Wesley Clark's "seven countries in five years" — and where Iran fits in that sequence
✔️Whether these institutions can be reformed — or whether the BRICS/Eurasian alternative architecture is the only viable path
✔️What ordinary people can do when the watchdogs have become attack dogs

🎙️ Guest: Dr. Piers Robinson — Academic Researcher & Author (writing on OPCW investigations in Syria; collaborator with Hans von Sponik & Jose Bustani). Expert specializing in propaganda, international relations, global conflicts, and the integrity of international oversight institutions.

In this episode, Dr. Piers Robinson joins us to discuss chemical weapons, propaganda, and the ongoing push for regime change. We explore how these factors shape international relations, particularly concerning Iran and the broader Middle East, offering a deep dive into foreign policy.

🔔 Subscribe for the geopolitical analysis the mainstream won't commission | 👍 Like if this changed how you see the institutions claiming authority over the Iran conflict | 💬 Share with someone who still trusts the IAEA's Iran assessments

🔔 Subscribe for more unfiltered geopolitical analysis: https://www.youtube.com/@UCROsxSizsKbUzdARaocKDRQ

Timestamps and Highlights:

02:22 - The Creation and Role of the OPCW

entry.title: [GEO] Why the US pressured the UAE to leave OPEC: Big Oil corporations benefit
entry.summary:
OPEC, the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, is in crisis after the United Arab Emirates (UAE), its third-largest producer, left the group. Donald Trump openly praised the decision, saying it is good for the United States. Ben Norton explains why the US government opposes OPEC, which is rooted in the Third World nationalist movements of the 1960s, which sought more economic resources for the Global South. The USA wants its Big Oil corporations to dominate the industry.

The show will be hosted by Nina Farnia, with Corinna Mullin reporting live from New York City's May Day march.

entry.title: [TLorenz] AI Censorship Is Getting Out Of Control
entry.summary:
Should AI be protected by free speech laws?
Support my independent journalism: 🙏 Patreon: ⁠https://www.patreon.com/cw/taylorlorenz ⁠ 🗞️ Buy a paid subscription to my Substack: ⁠https://www.usermag.co
In this episode of Free Speech Friday, I sit down with Corbin K. Barthold, Internet Policy Counsel at Tech Freedom, to discuss one of the most controversial legal papers of the year: "Why the First Amendment Protects Artificial Intelligence."
While many are calling for strict AI regulation to prevent "AI psychosis" or the spread of misinformation, Corbin argues that AI outputs are a protected form of expression. If we allow the government to dictate what an LLM can and cannot say, are we inadvertently giving them the power to control our access to information?
In this video, we cover: Why AI content qualifies as First Amendment protected speech. The theories the government uses to justify censorship. Analysis of recent tragic cases involving Character AI. Why New York’s S7263 law might be "rank protectionism" for doctors and lawyers. The progressive case for why we should actually want free and open AI.
#siliconvalley #tech #technology #internet #ai #artificialintelligence

entry.title: [DDP] I Spent 11 Days Inside Iran During the War — Here's What They're Not Telling You
entry.summary:
He crossed into Iran overland from Turkey. Spent 11 days on the ground. Visited 7 cities. Boarded a civilian vessel into the Strait of Hormuz. Journalist and lawyer Dimitri Lascaris went inside the country Western media calls America's greatest enemy — and what he found contradicts almost everything you've been told.
Lascaris — who has reported from conflict zones across the globe — returned from Iran with a forensic account of a nation that is far more sophisticated, more resilient, and more united than any Western outlet will admit. In this episode he breaks down the real motivation behind US aggression against Iran (it's not the Israel lobby alone — it's a 75-year strategy for global domination and control of West Asia's resources), the catastrophic economic consequences already in motion if the Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted, and why Trump has failed every single stated objective of this war — while a "Round Two" may already be in preparation.
🔍 In This Episode: ✔️Dimitri's 11-day journey: 7 cities, the Strait of Hormuz & crossing into Iran by land from Turkey ✔️Why Iran is "far more sophisticated, resilient and united" than Western media portrays ✔️The real US motivation: global domination, strategic resources & the Wesley Clark "7 countries" plan ✔️Why Dimitri rejects the "Israel lobby as sole driver" narrative — and what the real driver is ✔️Economic catastrophe: economists predict famine within 3–5 months if Hormuz stays disrupted ✔️"September is the deadline" — the supply chain timeline the West isn't discussing ✔️Three US carrier battle groups in the region: preparation for Round Two? ✔️Trump's failed objectives: regime change, insurrection, decapitation, missile destruction ✔️How the war has united Iranians "to an extraordinary degree" — what Dimitri witnessed on the streets ✔️Sanctions as economic terrorism: the real cost to Iranian civilians over decades ✔️What Dimitri actually saw in Iran: infrastructure, food supply, homelessness, women, democracy ✔️The Minnaab strike: 168 students and teachers killed on the first day of the war ✔️Is a deliberate global economic crash part of a larger strategy? Dimitri's assessment ✔️What meaningful resistance looks like — and what ordinary people can actually do
🎙️ Guest: Dimitri Lascaris — Journalist, Lawyer & War Correspondent
🔔 Subscribe for on-the-ground reporting mainstream media won't commission | 👍 Like if this changed what you thought you knew about Iran | 💬 Share with someone who's only seen Iran through a Western lens

entry.title: [GEO] How the US uses oil as a geopolitical weapon
entry.summary:
Oil has been at the center of the war on Iran. Ben Norton explains how the United States became the biggest oil producer on Earth, and how the US empire uses petroleum as a geopolitical and economic weapon, pushing for monopolistic Big Oil corporations to dominate the global market. Ben also discusses why Trump pressured the UAE to withdraw from OPEC, and he debunks the myth that the USA is "energy independent".
Check out our related video on the biggest oil crisis in history: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bUdyPJM_rOw

entry.title: [TLorenz] Why Rich People Use The Internet Differently
entry.summary:
There’s a hidden social hierarchy on the internet
Support my independent journalism: 🙏 Patreon: ⁠https://www.patreon.com/cw/taylorlorenz ⁠ 🗞️ Buy a paid subscription to my Substack: ⁠https://www.usermag.co
Is your Instagram "low class"? While we like to think of the internet as a universal playground, a new digital class war is emerging. From the way you use filters to the number of emojis in your bio, your digital habits are increasingly signaling your socioeconomic status to the world.
In this episode of Power User, I sit down with etymologist and cultural commentator Adam Aleksic to decode the hidden semiotics of "Low Class Instagram."
We explore why billionaires keep their accounts private with 200 followers, why the "photo dump" has become a sophisticated narrative tool for elites, and how algorithms are siloing us into class-based bubbles.
We break down: The "Puppy Filter" Trap: Why technical illiteracy is the new class signifier. Billionaire Behavior: Why the ultimate luxury is being completely offline. Digital White Flight: Why elite users are fleeing Facebook and Instagram for "curated" spaces like Bluesky vs Substack. AI & Class: How your choice of LLM (Claude vs. ChatGPT) and even your font choice (Serif vs. Sans Serif) reveals your status. The Death of Anonymity: Why the "surveillance state" makes it harder for lower-class users to experiment with their identities.
entry.content:

entry.title: [DDP] The Iran Conflict Is No Longer Staying Overseas
entry.summary:
A British-Iranian citizen was stabbed outside Downing Street at a peaceful protest. Who allowed it to happen — and why does it trace back to Britain's foreign policy on Iran?
When Mohammed Raza attended a peaceful rally in London, he was attacked from behind with knives. His attackers, allegedly linked to pro-Pahlavi organisations operating freely on British soil, had previously threatened his four-year-old daughter. The police were slow to respond. The ambulance was delayed. And the organisations allegedly responsible continue to operate with impunity.
This is blowback — and it is happening on our own streets.
Deep Dive Perspective brings together Hos Shafi (Workers Party Leadership Council), Nili Brook (journalist and founder of Iran's Resistance Rising), Raza Qasim (Chief Steward, Islamic Human Rights Commission), and Dr. Marzia Ansari (lawyer) to examine the foreign policy decisions and domestic failures that created the conditions for this attack — and what it reveals about who is really controlling British political space.
The views expressed in this interview are those of the guests and are presented for analytical and educational purposes. Deep Dive Perspective does not necessarily endorse all positions stated.
In This Episode: 🔪 The Downing Street stabbing: what happened to Mohammed Raza, the police response, and why the attackers may have been operating a planned operation 🚔 Why British police treat pro-Palestinian and pro-Iranian protesters through a lens of systemic bias — while violent counter-groups operate freely 📺 Iran International: its alleged foreign funding, its role in inciting violence against British-Iranians, and why the British government refuses to act ☠️ The MKO (Mujahedin-e Khalq): once a proscribed terrorist organisation, now attending UK parliamentary events — how did that happen? 🏛️ How foreign influence is shaping British domestic policy — and what Hussain Shafiei means by Britain being "occupied by a foreign power" ⚖️ Dr. Marzia Ansari on the legal case for proscribing dangerous groups operating on British soil 🌍 The manipulation of the asylum system: how asylum claims are being manufactured by organisations inciting violence against the Iranian state
🔔 Subscribe to Deep Dive Perspective for geopolitical analysis that goes beyond the headlines. 👍 Like this video if it challenged your understanding of what's happening on British streets. 💬 Comment below — should Iran International be investigated and held to account in the UK?
Guests Info: Hussain Shafiei: Member of the Workers Party Leadership Council. Nilly Brook: Independent journalist and founder of Iran's Resistance Rising. Reza Kazim: Chief Steward and representative from the Islamic Human Rights Commission. Dr. Marzieh Ansari: Legal professional and expert in international and domestic law.

entry.title: [DDP] Why Europe Still Can't Act Independently | Mick Wallace
entry.summary:
"Europe is the loyal tail that wags every time America barks." Former EU MP Mick Wallace and journalist Myriam Charabaty expose the foreign policy scandal Europe's leaders won't admit.
Europe presents itself to the world as a defender of human rights, democracy, and international law. But as the war on Gaza enters its second year and NATO's grip on European decision-making tightens, a very different picture emerges. Deep Dive Perspective sits down with former EU Member of Parliament Mick Wallace and Beirut-based journalist Myriam Charabaty to dissect why Europe cannot — or will not — act independently of Washington on the most consequential geopolitical questions of our time.
From the economics of energy dependency to the military intelligence role European nations play in West Asia, this is a forensic examination of the gap between what European leaders say and what they actually do.The views expressed in this interview are those of the guests and are presented for analytical and educational purposes. Deep Dive Perspective does not necessarily endorse all positions stated.
Guests Info: Mick Wallace: Former Member of the European Parliament (MEP) and outspoken political commentator on global macroeconomic affairs. Myriam Charabaty: Independent geopolitical journalist and analyst based in Beirut, Lebanon.
In This Episode: 🐕 Why Europe has become a "loyal tail" to US foreign policy — and what NATO's true function really is 💰 How following US foreign policy is costing European economies billions — the LNG price scandal ✈️ The secret military role of European nations (France, UK, Germany) in the West Asia conflict 🇺🇳 Why the EU still refuses to condemn Israel despite the deaths of over 20,000 children in Gaza 🇸🇾 Europe's role in the destabilisation of Syria — and what it reveals about Western "values" 🗳️ Is European democracy failing? The disconnect between public opinion and elite foreign policy 🌍 What Mick Wallace's advice to ordinary European citizens actually means — and why it matters
🔔 Subscribe to Deep Dive Perspective for geopolitical analysis that goes beyond the headlines.
👍 Like this video if it challenged your view of European foreign policy.
💬 Comment below — do you think Europe will ever achieve genuine foreign policy independence?
🕒Timestamps and Highlights 00:00 - Introduction: The Global Geopolitical Shift 02:40 - Mick Wallace on European Foreign Policy 04:15 - NATO, US Influence & The Global Economy 06:34 - Energy Markets and Europe's Economic Crisis 08:25 - Western Leadership vs. Domestic Stability 10:35 - Miriam Charabazi on Middle East Geopolitics 14:10 - The Hidden Reality of Global Intelligence 17:20 - Regional Alliances and the Shift in Global Power 20:00 - EU Foreign Policy Failures Explained 23:25 - Corporate Interests: The Mozambique Example 30:23 - International Relations & Maritime Trade Routes 43:10 - Analyzing the Conflict in Eastern Europe 51:15 - The Illusion of Western Democracy 56:05 - Conclusion: How Citizens Can Reclaim Their Future

entry.title: [TLorenz] The Shocking Secrets of Madison Square Garden’s Surveillance Machine
entry.summary:
Thanks to Monarch for partnering with me! Start your free trial and get 50% off your first year of total money clarity using my link https://monarchmoney.yt.link/pywCdKs or code TL50.
Support my independent journalism: 🙏 Patreon: ⁠https://www.patreon.com/cw/taylorlorenz ⁠ 🗞️ Buy a paid subscription to my Substack: ⁠https://www.usermag.co
Sports arenas and concert venues are becoming high-tech surveillance "deep states." In this video, I dive into the explosive Wired report by Noah Shachtman and Robert Silverman that reveals how the owner of a ton of NYC concert venues and sports arenas uses facial recognition and private investigators to track, monitor, and ban anyone he deems an enemy.
From tracking trans women at Pride Night to banning lawyers and 14-year-old kids for social media posts, the level of surveillance at MSG, Radio City, and The Sphere is unprecedented.
Is this the future of privacy for sports fans and concert-goers? I sat down with Noah Shachtman to discuss the chilling secrets of the new billionaire surveillance machine.
Full WIRED story: https://www.wired.com/story/madison-square-garden-jim-dolan-surveillance-machine

entry.title: [TLorenz] Big Tech is Pinkwashing AI
entry.summary:
AI companies are secretly waging a massive PR campaign targeting women.
Support my independent journalism: 🙏 Patreon: ⁠https://www.patreon.com/cw/taylorlorenz ⁠ 🗞️ Buy a paid subscription to my Substack: ⁠https://www.usermag.co
In the past year, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Meta have launched an aggressive PR campaign to rebrand artificial intelligence for women, especially mothers and teen girls. Suddenly AI isn't an existential threat or a labor displacement machine. It's a "lifestyle accessory," a "creative tool," and your new girly best friend.
Kat Tenbarge of Spitfire News joins me to expose the AI "Hot Girl Economy" and how tech giants are using aesthetic trends to Trojan-horse AI into our daily lives. We cover everything from viral Studio Ghibli filters and the AI puppy photo craze, to how startups like Higgsfield are targeting fashion influencers.
We discuss what this "AI girly pop era" is designed to distract you from: extreme labor displacement, massive energy consumption, and the tech industry becoming increasingly intertwined with the US War Machine.

entry.title: [GEO] US economy is based on Ponzi scheme that could collapse, warns economist Michael Hudson
entry.summary:
There are growing signs that the United States may be on the verge of another major financial crisis, one that could start in the $3 trillion private credit market, which is already seeing significant turmoil, before spreading to other sectors.
Geopolitical Economy Report editor Ben Norton interviewed economist Michael Hudson to discuss the serious problems on Wall Street. Hudson warned that the US economy is built on a Ponzi scheme that depends on continuing to pour money into a bloated, bubbly financial system based on unsustainable speculation, not industrial production.
Check out our related short video about the private credit crisis on Wall Street: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S1cx9Zk6WZk

entry.title: [DDP] The Iran Ceasefire Is More Fragile Than It Looks | Deep Dive
entry.summary:
Iran controls the Strait of Hormuz. The ceasefire is already expiring. And according to Ehsan Safar Najad, Iran hasn't even played its full deck of cards yet.
As the dust settles on the Iran-US-Israel semi-stalemate, the critical question isn't whether there was a ceasefire — it's whether it means anything at all. Deep Dive Perspective sits down with independent analyst and host of The Political Aficionado, Ehsan Safar Najad, to break down Iran's strategic position in the aftermath of conflict: from its stranglehold on the Strait of Hormuz, to the 10-point negotiating framework the US refuses to accept, to the nuclear deterrence calculation that has Washington and Tel Aviv at a standstill.
The ceasefire, as Ehsan puts it, "has an expiration date like a carton of milk left outside during July."
The views expressed in this interview are those of the guest and are presented for analytical and educational purposes. Deep Dive Perspective does not necessarily endorse all positions stated.
In This Episode: 🚢 Why the Strait of Hormuz remains Iran's most powerful strategic lever — and why the US knows it 🤝 Iran's 10-point peace framework vs. the US's 15-point maximalist demands — why no deal is likely ☢️ Iran's nuclear deterrence calculus: the North Korea comparison and what 460kg of enriched uranium means 💰 How Iran is maintaining oil revenues at 2.3 million barrels/day despite Western sanctions 🌍 The global economic consequences: 9 strategic commodities, fertiliser shortages, and the famine horizon ⚔️ Why the next round of conflict will be "even more devastating" — and what Iran's unused cards look like
🔔 Subscribe to Deep Dive Perspective for geopolitical analysis that goes beyond the headlines. 👍 Like this video if it changed how you see the Iran-US standoff. 💬 Comment below — does this ceasefire hold, or are we heading for round two?

As the dust settles on the Iran-US-Israel semi-stalemate, the critical question isn't whether there was a ceasefire — it's whether it means anything at all. Deep Dive Perspective sits down with independent analyst and host of The Political Aficionado, Ehsan Safar Najad, to break down Iran's strategic position in the aftermath of conflict: from its stranglehold on the Strait of Hormuz, to the 10-point negotiating framework the US refuses to accept, to the nuclear deterrence calculation that has Washington and Tel Aviv at a standstill.

The ceasefire, as Ehsan puts it, "has an expiration date like a carton of milk left outside during July."

entry.title: [DDP] How a Smaller Force Keeps Challenging Israel | Laith Marouf
entry.summary:
3,000 Hezbollah fighters. 120,000 Israeli soldiers. Two years of war — and Israel hasn't captured a single village. Laith Marouf breaks down who's really winning in West Asia.
As Western media scrambles to explain the military stalemate in Southern Lebanon, broadcaster and analyst Laith Marouf — host of Free Palestine TV — joins Deep Dive Perspective to cut through the propaganda and assess the real balance of forces on the ground. From the strategic depth of Lebanon's terrain, to Iran's calculated acceptance of a ceasefire, to the slow collapse of Israel's military mythology in the age of citizen journalism — Marouf delivers a forensic, unfiltered reading of where this 100-year conflict is heading.The views expressed in this interview are those of the guest and are presented here for analytical and educational purposes. Deep Dive Perspective does not necessarily endorse all positions stated.
In This Episode: 🪖 Why 3,000 Hezbollah fighters have stalled 120,000 Israeli soldiers in Southern Lebanon 🤝 The strategic logic behind Iran's ceasefire acceptance — weakness or calculation? 📱 How citizen journalism is dismantling decades of Western and Zionist propaganda 👑 The role of Arab monarchies in sustaining Western influence in the region ⚔️ Whether the Axis of Resistance can deliver a decisive military outcome 🗺️ The "100-year war" framework — and whether the next chapter has already begun
🔔 Subscribe for geopolitical analysis that goes beyond the headlines. 👍 Like this video if it challenged your understanding of the conflict.
💬 Comment below — we read every response.

entry.title: [LOI] Vietnam's Textbook Privatization Disaster
entry.summary:
School textbooks are a vital social institution for a socialist nation. Since the 1950s, Vietnam has had several textbook reforms, with varying degrees of success. Unfortunately, in 2013, Vietnam privatized the writing and publishing of school textbooks, leading to severe problems. In this video, we examine the catastrophic failure of textbook privatization in Vietnam.

entry.title: [GEO] Why is Trump blockading Iran's blockade? This is the crazy, and dangerous, US strategy
entry.summary:
The peace talks held between the US and Iran in Islamabad, Pakistan failed. Despite a temporary, two-week ceasefire Donald Trump announced a naval blockade of the Iranian blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Ben Norton explains the USA's strategy, and how it is targeting China and Asian economies that import oil, natural gas, and other important commodities from the Persian Gulf.

entry.title: [TLorenz] Silicon Valley is Breaking Everything
entry.summary:
Is Silicon Valley actually making progress, or are they just breaking the world for profit?
Support my independent journalism: 🙏 Patreon: ⁠https://www.patreon.com/cw/taylorlorenz ⁠ 🗞️ Buy a paid subscription to my Substack: ⁠https://www.usermag.co ⁠
In this episode of Free Speech Friday, I’m joined by legal scholar and surveillance expert Albert Fox Cahn to dismantle the "Move Fast and Break Things" ethos that has dominated tech for a decade. From the multibillion-dollar failure of the Metaverse to the "innovation theater" of the Apple Vision Pro, we explore why the most hyped technologies are often the most disastrous.
We dive deep into the dark side of home security, revealing how Ring cameras have turned from safety tools into a massive surveillance network for law enforcement, sometimes even recording your most private moments without a warrant. We also tackle the "AI bubble," the myth of police body cameras, and why the "boring" analog solutions are often the safest bet for our future.
Albert is the founder of the Surveillance Technology Oversight Project (S.T.O.P.) and the author of the new book, Move Slow and Upgrade: The Power of Incremental Innovation.

entry.title: [TLorenz] How AI Legos Became Iran's Secret Weapon
entry.summary:
Iranian LEGO videos mocking the US government are taking over the internet.
Support my independent journalism: 🙏 Patreon: ⁠https://www.patreon.com/cw/taylorlorenz ⁠ 🗞️ Buy a paid subscription to my Substack: ⁠https://www.usermag.co ⁠
We are living in an era where global internet culture and meme warfare are just as influential as traditional media and LEGOs are being used to create the most effective messaging around the US-Iran war.
I sat down with Areeba Fatima, a journalist for Dropsite News, who's been reporting on the team behind "Explosive Media," the group of seemingly Gen Z Iranians producing this viral content.
We dive deep into how these LEGO videos are actively reshaping Americans' perceptions of modern warfare, and why this content is uniquely bridging the gap across the American political spectrum. We discuss:
How these viral LEGO videos are actively reshaping how Americans view our war with Iran. - The fascinating Telegram history of Explosive Media, revealing they started as young internet natives posting about sports, exams, and movies before the war escalated. - Why mainstream liberal accounts and right-wing podcasters in America are both sharing this content by the millions. - How this Gen Z team uses AI to bypass sanctions, generate rap songs, and tap directly into global internet culture. - The relentless cat-and-mouse game of Explosive Media getting banned from platforms like YouTube and X, only to immediately return.
🔥 EXCLUSIVE BONUS EPISODE: I actually tracked down and interviewed a member of Explosive Media. To hear directly from the Iranians making these LEGO propaganda videos, watch the full bonus interview on my Patreon or Substack linked below.
🙏 Patreon: ⁠https://www.patreon.com/cw/taylorlorenz ⁠ 🗞️ Buy a paid subscription to my Substack: ⁠https://www.usermag.co ⁠
Support Independent Journalism: I currently have zero brand sponsors or advertising partners. My work is solely funded by viewers like you. If you value this content, please consider supporting me so I can keep producing these deep dives
🙏 Patreon: ⁠https://www.patreon.com/cw/taylorlorenz ⁠ 🗞️ Buy a paid subscription to my Substack: ⁠https://www.usermag.co ⁠

🗞️ Buy a paid subscription to my Substack: ⁠https://www.usermag.co ⁠

We are living in an era where global internet culture and meme warfare are just as influential as traditional media and LEGOs are being used to create the most effective messaging around the US-Iran war.

I sat down with Areeba Fatima, a journalist for Dropsite News, who's been reporting on the team behind "Explosive Media," the group of seemingly Gen Z Iranians producing this viral content.

We dive deep into how these LEGO videos are actively reshaping Americans' perceptions of modern warfare, and why this content is uniquely bridging the gap across the American political spectrum. We discuss:

How these viral LEGO videos are actively reshaping how Americans view our war with Iran.
- The fascinating Telegram history of Explosive Media, revealing they started as young internet natives posting about sports, exams, and movies before the war escalated.
- Why mainstream liberal accounts and right-wing podcasters in America are both sharing this content by the millions.
- How this Gen Z team uses AI to bypass sanctions, generate rap songs, and tap directly into global internet culture.
- The relentless cat-and-mouse game of Explosive Media getting banned from platforms like YouTube and X, only to immediately return.

🔥 EXCLUSIVE BONUS EPISODE: I actually tracked down and interviewed a member of Explosive Media. To hear directly from the Iranians making these LEGO propaganda videos, watch the full bonus interview on my Patreon or Substack linked below.

entry.title: [DDP] The Epstein Files Exposed: Transhumanism, Media Control & the World They're Building Without You
entry.summary:
The Epstein files are finally in the public domain. But two investigators argue the real story goes far deeper than one man, one island, or one network.
Tony Gosling — investigative journalist and former BBC broadcaster — and Ryan Dawson — geopolitical analyst and author — trace the architecture of what they call the "Epstein class": an interconnected network of power that spans media institutions, technology corporations, financial systems, and political structures. Their argument is not about one individual but about a systemic, generational project — one designed, they claim, to engineer a techno-feudalist future in which wealth consolidates upward, institutions are deliberately corrupted, and the public is reduced to passive consumers of a pre-designed reality.
From Yuval Harari's "hacking humans" philosophy to the World Economic Forum's "own nothing, be happy" agenda; from the acquisition of food systems and land to the weaponisation of AI — Gosling and Dawson map what they see as a convergent, coordinated strategy of control, and ask: what does meaningful resistance actually look like?
🔍 In This Episode:
✔️What is the "Epstein class" — and why does the term matter beyond Epstein himself? ✔️The historical roots of the techno-feudalist agenda: eugenics, social Darwinism and the 1930s ✔️Yuval Harari and the philosophy of "hacking humans" — inevitable evolution or deliberate agenda? ✔️How the "Epstein class" profits from major global crises: the pattern across decades ✔️Media monopoly and institutional capture: how the "wrong people" are placed in positions of power ✔️The WEF "own nothing" agenda and the acquisition of land, food systems and resources ✔️Peter Thiel, Palantir, and the digitalisation of surveillance — the Eye of Sauron becomes real ✔️AI, privacy and the collapse of consent ✔️Elon Musk's grandfather and the historical roots of technocratic ideology ✔️The role of alternative media, boycotts and moral re-armament in building genuine resistance ✔️Why withdrawing consent — from products, platforms and ideology — is the most powerful act available ✔️What virtue ethics across traditions (Taoism, Islam, Christianity, Bushido) offer as a counter-framework
🎙️ Guests: Tony Gosling — Investigative Journalist & Former BBC Broadcaster Ryan Dawson — Geopolitical Analyst & Author

entry.title: [GEO] What just happened in China is huge
entry.summary:
The US government has pushed for years for a war with China, selling billions of dollars of weapons to separatists in Taiwan. However, those plans are falling apart, after the leader of the KMT, the Chinese Nationalist Party based in Taipei, took a historic trip to the mainland, promoting peace. Ben Norton explains the importance of Cheng Li-wun's meeting with Xi Jinping, and the complex political history of Taiwan.

entry.title: [DDP] Ex-IDF, Ex-Israeli: Gilad Atzmon on What's Really Driving Israel's Genocidal Logic
entry.summary:
Gilad Atzmon served in the IDF. He left Israel at 30. He now identifies as neither Israeli nor Jewish. And he argues that what's driving the war on Iran isn't Zionism — it's something older, deeper, and far more dangerous.
In this episode, Atzmon — international jazz musician, bestselling author and former IDF conscript — makes the case that Israel has undergone a fundamental transformation: from a secular Zionist project into a theocratic, religiously-driven state shaped by Judaic scripture, Rabbi Kook's philosophy, and a political culture of impunity. He traces this shift from the kibbutz era through Likud's 1977 victory, Netanyahu's 1996 electoral realignment, and into the religious nationalism of Ben-Gvir and Smotrich that now drives Israeli state policy — including the war on Iran.
🔍 In This Episode:
✔️Why Gilad Atzmon identifies as "ex-Israeli" and "ex-Jew" — and what it cost him ✔️The difference between Zionism and Judaism — and why that distinction matters for understanding the current war ✔️How Israel shifted from a secular to a theocratic state: from kibbutzim to Ben-Gvir ✔️Netanyahu's 1996 electoral discovery — and how it accelerated Israel's religious turn ✔️Rabbi Kook's philosophy and the roots of Greater Israel and the settler movement ✔️The role of AIPAC, political influence, and the "tail wagging the dog" debate ✔️Purim and Passover as religious frameworks shaping Israel's military conduct ✔️How the Nakba of 1948 was driven by secular ideology — and why Gaza is fundamentally different ✔️The distinction between anti-Zionist Orthodox Jews (Satmar, Neturei Karta) and the mainstream ✔️Jewish "chosenness" — as duty, or as supremacy? The political implications ✔️Why Atzmon argues Zionism has been defeated by Judaism — and what that means for peace ✔️The Iran war as the logical endpoint of Israel's religious-ideological transformation
🎙️ Guest: Gilad Atzmon — International Jazz Musician, Bestselling Author & Former IDF Conscript
📌 Views expressed are those of the guest and are presented for purposes of informed political and historical debate. Gilad Atzmon is a public intellectual whose work is widely discussed in academic and political contexts. Deep Dive Perspective presents multiple perspectives across our content. All assessments of Israeli state policy and ideology are the analytical opinions of the guest.
🔔 Subscribe for geopolitical analysis the mainstream won't touch | 👍 Like if this reframed how you understand the conflict | 💬 Share with someone who still thinks this is only about Zionism

⏱️ CHAPTERS: 0:00 Introduction — Gilad Atzmon: Ex-IDF, Ex-Israeli, Ex-Jew 1:11 Why He Rarely Does Interviews — and Why Now 2:01 Israel as a "Jewish State" — America as Its Most Dedicated Colony 3:52 AIPAC, Political Influence & the Tail That Wags the Dog 5:57 Jewish Texts, Metaphysics & the Spirit Behind the Violence 7:00 Rejecting the "Jew" Label — A Personal and Political Journey 9:52 Do Judaism, Torah & Talmud Have Genocidal Elements? 12:25 The Exception: Anti-Zionist Orthodox Jews (Satmar & Neturei Karta) 13:01 How Israel Shifted from Secular Zionism to a Religious State 16:30 Netanyahu's 1996 Electoral Discovery — The Religious Realignment 19:10 The 1948 Nakba — Secular Ideology, Not Religious Hatred 22:02 The "J Element" — What Jewish Intellectuals Won't Say 24:42 Jewish Supremacy — Where Does It Come From? 27:15 Chosenness as Duty vs. Chosenness as Supremacy 29:26 A Culture of Impunity: Madoff, Kushner, Epstein, Netanyahu 31:39 Rabbi Kook's Philosophy & the Roots of Greater Israel 35:00 Ben-Gvir, Smotrich & the Settlers — Children of Kook's Vision 40:00 Zionism Defeated by Judaism — What That Means for the War 45:00 The Iran War as the Endpoint of Israel's Religious Transformation 52:00 What Would Peace Actually Require? Atzmon's Assessment
#IranWar #StraitOfHormuz #IranWarUpdate #usforeignpolicy #ceasefire #israel #trump

entry.title: [TLorenz] The Woman Taking On Big Tech
entry.summary:
Are we losing our right to online privacy entirely?
Support my independent journalism: 🙏 Patreon: ⁠https://www.patreon.com/cw/taylorlorenz ⁠ 🗞️ Buy a paid subscription to my Substack: ⁠https://www.usermag.co ⁠
In this episode of Free Speech Friday, I sat down with Cindy Cohn, former head of the Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF) and author of the new book Privacy's Defender. We dive deep into the secret history of the early internet, the terrifying reality of mass online surveillance by the US government, and how "age verification" laws sweeping the US will destroy digital civil liberties for everyone.
Cindy explains how the NSA and FBI use massive legal loopholes like Section 702 to secretly access your data for domestic purposes, the massive security dangers of forcing users to upload their IDs to access apps like Discord, and why the fight to protect encryption is more crucial now than ever.
If you care about your digital rights, free speech, and the future of the open web, this is a must-watch conversation!!
Topics Covered: The 90s hacker origins of the Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF) How the Bernstein case saved internet encryption Post-9/11 mass surveillance and the government's data grab Why the FBI and NSA's use of Section 702 threatens your privacy The truth about the government buying your personal data from private brokers Why age verification laws banning users under 16 create massive security risks How to fight back and become part of the next generation of privacy advocates
#privacy #surveillance #internet #technology #freedom #dataprivacy #bigtech #security

entry.title: [GEO] Ceasefire proves Iran is winning the war. But it's not over. This is why
entry.summary:
