entry.title: [TLorenz] The Downfall of Skibidi Toilet
entry.summary:
Skibidi Toilet changed how storytelling works on YouTube. But behind the scenes, things got messy.
Support my independent journalism: 🙏 Patreon: ⁠https://www.patreon.com/cw/taylorlorenz ⁠ 🗞️ Buy a paid subscription to my Substack: ⁠https://www.usermag.co ⁠
Back in 2023, a creator named Alexey (better known as DaFuqBoom) launched a super weird, viral series about a singing head in a toilet called Skibidi Toilet. It went viral and quickly became one of the most popular online series to ever exist. Over the course of 79 episodes, it evolved into an epic, fan-favorite story about an intergalactic war.
Then, Hollywood stepped in. A traditional (or "tra-digital") entertainment company called Invisible Narratives bought the Skibidi Toilet rights. By early 2025, Alexey took a step back to become an executive producer, meaning he was no longer making the daily videos. The studio released a new spin-off series called "Emergence," but fans instantly noticed a massive change.
Now, the Skibidi Toilet fandom community is furious. After a disastrous, leaked call between the studio and popular YouTubers, fans are demanding the original creator take his show back with the hashtag #BringBackBoom. Meanwhile, Skibidi Toilet has become one of the most valuable franchises in Hollywood.
Steven Asarch is a journalist who's been covering this controversy. In this video, he joins me to break down what is going on with the Skibidi Toilet franchise, the #BringBackBoom movement's true goals, and answer the biggest question fans are all asking: is Skibidi Toilet officially dead?
We cover: How Skibidi Toilet became so popular The creator’s role and what changed The studio takeover and what it means Why fans are upset and pushing back The future of internet-created IP

entry.title: [DDP] What Trump Got Wrong About Iran — Dr. Bresheth Explains Why He Was Wrong
entry.summary:
"A whole civilisation will die tonight, never to be brought back again." Those are Donald Trump's own words on the night he declared Complete and Total Regime Change in Iran. Dr. Haim Bresheth — historian and geopolitical analyst — explains why that calculation is not just wrong, but catastrophically dangerous. From the destruction of F-35s over Iranian airspace to the collapse of Israel's air defence stockpiles, from Hezbollah's battlefield resilience to the terrifying logic of the Samson Option — Dr. Bresheth delivers one of the most forensic analyses of this conflict available anywhere. Trump told the Iranian people "God Bless you" while signing off on the bombing. Dr. Bresheth explains what that really means — and who is actually pulling the strings.

entry.title: [SYR] Tarik Cyril Amar: Norman Finkelstein Is Wrong on Israel's War With Iran
entry.summary:
Historian Tarik Cyril Amar responds to Dr. Norman Finkelstein’s analysis of Israel’s war with Iran and argues that Finkelstein gets several key points wrong. In this segment, Tarik says Finkelstein makes “colossal mistakes” by too quickly dismissing possible Israeli influence in major U.S. decisions, by treating Donald Trump as if he could not be fooled, and by downplaying how deeply Israeli intelligence and pro-Israel networks penetrate the American system. Tarik also argues that the Iraq war and the war on Iran cannot be separated so easily from the neoconservative-Israeli nexus, even if Washington ultimately bears primary responsibility. The core of Tarik’s criticism is respectful but sharp: Finkelstein is right to insist that the United States is to blame, but wrong in the way he argues the case.
Subscribe to Tarik's Substack: https://www.tarikcyrilamar.com/subscribe?coupon=169c702d

entry.title: [GEO] Iran is winning the war. This is why
entry.summary:
Iran is winning the war that was started by the USA and Israel. This is now admitted even by major Western media outlets. Tehran has successfully destroyed the US military bases in West Asia (aka the Middle East), forcing the Pentagon to operate from Europe.
Ben Norton explains Iran's goals, and how it is seeking to establish deterrence and escalation dominance, using asymmetric tactics.
Links and sources here: https://www.geopoliticaleconomy.report/p/iran-winning-war-us-trump

entry.title: [EAST] IN SESSION #13: Empire's problem with sovereignty with Helyeh Doutaghi, Bikrum Gill & Alex Aviña
entry.summary:
Between the so-called Middle-East and Latin America, oh my, we have a lot to talk about how this empire implodes, psychically and otherwise, in the face of sovereign nations refusing to bend to its violence. Who better to help us unpack this than three of the most fierce anti-imperialist comrades I know--all of whom have a lot to say about sovereignty, empire, and, importantly, resistance.
Helyeh Doutaghi is scholar of international law and geopolitical economy. Her research explores the intersections of the Third World Approaches to International Law (TWAIL), encompassing postcolonial critiques of law, sanctions, and international political economy. Dr. Doutaghi’s research draws on the mechanisms, harms, and beneficiaries of the sanctions regime imposed on Iran, centering questions of value transfer and wealth drain. Additionally, she is interested in International Humanitarian Law (IHL), having written about its history, practice, and the production of knowledge (and ignorance), particularly in the context of the US military. She was expelled from Yale Law School and the LPE project for speaking up for Palestinian liberation last year. She is currently a post-doctoral fellow at the University of Tehran, where she will focus on completing her manuscript on the Iranian sanctions regime and neoliberalism.
Bikrum Gill is a scholar of international political economy, and author of "The Political Ecology of Colonial Capitalism: Race, Nature, and Accumulation", published by Manchester University Press.
Alex Aviña is an Associate Professor of History at Arizona State University. He is a historian of Mexico and Latin America.

Alex Aviña is an Associate Professor of History at Arizona State University. He is a historian of Mexico and Latin America.

entry.title: [GEO] The Iran war changes everything: The world will never be the same
entry.summary:
The US-Israeli war on Iran is transforming the geopolitical order, and could even unleash a global economic crisis. The conflict has caused the largest oil shock in history, disrupting markets and driving up fuel and food prices.
To better understand the implications for the world, Geopolitical Economy Report editor Ben Norton interviewed economist Michael Hudson, who discussed how Iran is challenging US dollar dominance and undermining Washington’s control over the global oil industry, which has been a key pillar of the USA's foreign policy.
Read the transcript here: https://www.geopoliticaleconomy.report/p/war-iran-change-economy-michael-hudson
Topics 0:00 Intro 4:29 Highlights 7:21 Interview starts 10:10 US plan to control oil 12:25 US war strategy 18:55 Struggle for sovereignty 21:06 Why the war started now 26:05 Petrodollar system 30:16 Strait of Hormuz 32:02 Largest oil crisis ever 34:14 Energy and food crisis 37:10 Debt crisis 39:31 Outro
|| Geopolitical Economy Report ||

entry.title: [DDP] Iran Has Been Preparing for This for Decades
entry.summary:
Vanessa Beeley, Kit Klarenberg & Dr. Mohammad Haidar expose how 47 years of Iranian preparation have left US strategy in ruins — as the Hormuz shutdown threatens global oil markets. 🔴 As the 74 iran war enters a critical new phase, three of the sharpest independent analysts in geopolitics break down what the mainstream refuses to report. Vanessa Beeley, Kit Klarenberg, and Dr. Mohammad Haidar make the case that the US and its allies fundamentally misread Iran's capabilities — and are now paying the price. With the 68 strait of hormuz under pressure, oil prices already at $100 a barrel, and Iran's underground missile stockpile estimated at 6,000–10,000 weapons, the picture that emerges is one of catastrophic Western miscalculation. This is the unfiltered analysis that corporate media won't air. In This Episode:
✅ Why Iran's 47-year preparation — 6,000 to 10,000 missiles in hardened underground facilities — has rendered the US "shock and awe" doctrine obsolete against a prepared adversary ✅ The devastating economics of a Strait of Hormuz closure: 20% of world oil supply, 80% of Gulf food imports, and a global insurance shock — Lloyd's premiums up 25–30% overnight ✅ Why the $7,000 Shahid drone vs. the $1.5 million US interceptor missile exposes a fundamental asymmetry that is bankrupting Washington's military posture in the region ✅ How the attempted decapitation of Iran's leadership created the opposite effect — uniting Iranian public opinion and producing a harder-line successor with deeper popular legitimacy ✅ Russia and China's calculated abstentions at the UN Security Council — and why this matters for the future of the multipolar order ✅ What Iran's conditions for ending the conflict reveal about the coming geopolitical reframing of the entire Middle East region
⏱️ CHAPTERS: 00:00 — Introduction: Iran's 47-Year Trap Sprung [TIMESTAMP] — Iran's Military Capabilities: 6,000 Missiles & a Strategy 47 Years in the Making [TIMESTAMP] — The Strait of Hormuz Shutdown: Global Oil, Food & Insurance in Crisis [TIMESTAMP] — The $7,000 Drone vs $1.5 Million Interceptor: Asymmetric Warfare Explained [TIMESTAMP] — US Decapitation Strategy Backfires: The Leadership Succession Effect [TIMESTAMP] — Russia & China at the UN: Abstention, Oil Revenue & the Multipolar Calculation [TIMESTAMP] — Iran's Peace Conditions: What the End of This War Looks Like [TIMESTAMP] — Closing Analysis: The Collapse of US Strategic Deterrence
🎙️ ABOUT VANESSA BEELEY: Vanessa Beeley is an independent investigative journalist and associate editor at 21st Century Wire, based in Beirut. Specialising in Middle East conflicts, Syria, and Western foreign policy propaganda, she is one of the most prominent voices in independent journalism on the region. 🎙️ ABOUT KIT KLARENBERG: Kit Klarenberg is an investigative journalist with The Grayzone, specialising in intelligence agencies, covert operations, and US foreign policy. His reporting has exposed Western information operations across the Middle East and Eastern Europe. 🎙️ ABOUT DR. MOHAMMED HAIDAR: Dr. Mohammad Haidar is a journalist and financial analyst specialising in Middle East economics, geopolitical risk, and the intersection of energy markets and imperial strategy.
🌊 ABOUT DEEP DIVE PERSPECTIVE: Deep Dive Perspective explains how power, politics, and global systems really work — in a clear, honest, and unfiltered way. We cover geopolitics, war analysis, Middle East news, US foreign policy, and international relations with guests and experts from around the world. 📌 Subscribe for weekly deep dives: https://www.youtube.com/@DeepDivePerspective
🔗 WATCH NEXT: ▶️ https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7UvTTuhNIJY&t=86s ▶️ https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kU6JjFKYt7M

entry.content:
Vanessa Beeley, Kit Klarenberg & Dr. Mohammad Haidar expose how 47 years of Iranian preparation have left US strategy in ruins — as the Hormuz shutdown threatens global oil markets.
🔴
As the 74 iran war enters a critical new phase, three of the sharpest independent analysts in geopolitics break down what the mainstream refuses to report. Vanessa Beeley, Kit Klarenberg, and Dr. Mohammad Haidar make the case that the US and its allies fundamentally misread Iran's capabilities — and are now paying the price. With the 68 strait of hormuz under pressure, oil prices already at $100 a barrel, and Iran's underground missile stockpile estimated at 6,000–10,000 weapons, the picture that emerges is one of catastrophic Western miscalculation. This is the unfiltered analysis that corporate media won't air.
In This Episode:

✅ Why Iran's 47-year preparation — 6,000 to 10,000 missiles in hardened underground facilities — has rendered the US "shock and awe" doctrine obsolete against a prepared adversary
✅ The devastating economics of a Strait of Hormuz closure: 20% of world oil supply, 80% of Gulf food imports, and a global insurance shock — Lloyd's premiums up 25–30% overnight
✅ Why the $7,000 Shahid drone vs. the $1.5 million US interceptor missile exposes a fundamental asymmetry that is bankrupting Washington's military posture in the region
✅ How the attempted decapitation of Iran's leadership created the opposite effect — uniting Iranian public opinion and producing a harder-line successor with deeper popular legitimacy
✅ Russia and China's calculated abstentions at the UN Security Council — and why this matters for the future of the multipolar order
✅ What Iran's conditions for ending the conflict reveal about the coming geopolitical reframing of the entire Middle East region


⏱️ CHAPTERS:
00:00 — Introduction: Iran's 47-Year Trap Sprung
[TIMESTAMP] — Iran's Military Capabilities: 6,000 Missiles & a Strategy 47 Years in the Making
[TIMESTAMP] — The Strait of Hormuz Shutdown: Global Oil, Food & Insurance in Crisis
[TIMESTAMP] — The $7,000 Drone vs $1.5 Million Interceptor: Asymmetric Warfare Explained
[TIMESTAMP] — US Decapitation Strategy Backfires: The Leadership Succession Effect
[TIMESTAMP] — Russia & China at the UN: Abstention, Oil Revenue & the Multipolar Calculation
[TIMESTAMP] — Iran's Peace Conditions: What the End of This War Looks Like
[TIMESTAMP] — Closing Analysis: The Collapse of US Strategic Deterrence

🎙️ ABOUT VANESSA BEELEY:
Vanessa Beeley is an independent investigative journalist and associate editor at 21st Century Wire, based in Beirut. Specialising in Middle East conflicts, Syria, and Western foreign policy propaganda, she is one of the most prominent voices in independent journalism on the region.
🎙️ ABOUT KIT KLARENBERG:
Kit Klarenberg is an investigative journalist with The Grayzone, specialising in intelligence agencies, covert operations, and US foreign policy. His reporting has exposed Western information operations across the Middle East and Eastern Europe.
🎙️ ABOUT DR. MOHAMMED HAIDAR:
Dr. Mohammad Haidar is a journalist and financial analyst specialising in Middle East economics, geopolitical risk, and the intersection of energy markets and imperial strategy.

🌊 ABOUT DEEP DIVE PERSPECTIVE:
Deep Dive Perspective explains how power, politics, and global systems really work — in a clear, honest, and unfiltered way. We cover geopolitics, war analysis, Middle East news, US foreign policy, and international relations with guests and experts from around the world.

entry.title: [EAST] Office Hours 28-Unity in Struggle against Empire w/Sina Rahmani
entry.summary:
Office Hours returns and the radical academy is open! Fri March 27 4pmET, Sina Rahmani, host of East is a Podcast and producer of the Adnan Husain Show joins to talk about the larger context of empire and imperialism at this moment. We'll take up the questions like what is empire and imperialism, its history and contemporary operation? How do we as the "left" in the "imperial core" fit into it and the question of solidarity and its barriers for internationalist worker/peoples' resistance? What is the place of Islamophobia in empire? What resources do the "Islamic resistance" in Palestine and across West Asia derive from its traditions? How do we decolonize the spirit and the material realities we live in? What role does the diaspora have in the Empire? We are told to listen to "Iranian voices" and "Syrian voices" even when their critiques undermine sovereignty and prepare for imperial wars--how do we navigate these questions?
Join us!
Follow Sina's channel East is a Podcast: youtube.com/@theeastisapodcast
Please consider help Mohammed and his siblings here: https://chuffed.org/project/168100-mohammed-and-siblings-need-our-help

entry.title: [EAST] Colonial Psyops with Lara Sheehi in conversation with Nick Estes
entry.summary:
In this special episode Lara Sheehi will return to the platform for a conversation co-hosted by Nick Estes, and co-streamed on The Red Nation podcast. We will talk about some elements of her new book From the Clinic to the Streets: Psychoanalysis for Revolutionary Futures. And specifically we will dig into some of the work she does in the book discussing the history of the development of the fields of Psychological Operations and Psychological Warfare and how those fields were shaped by psychoanalysis in the 20th Century. We will get into the current implications for “domestic counterinsurgency,” colonialism, and imperialism, including in the settler colonial contexts on Turtle Island and Occupied Palestine. Pre-order a copy of From the Clinic to the Streets: Psychoanalysis for Revolutionary Futures.
Lara Sheehi is a Research Fellow at the University of South Africa’s Institute for Social and Health Sciences, a licensed clinical psychologist, and the host of the Psychic Militancy podcast. Lara’s work focuses on psychoanalysis, the psychic refusals central to liberation struggles and life-making in the Global South, the psychic dimensions of resistance and revolution, and critical Zionism studies. She is co-author with Stephen Sheehi of Psychoanalysis Under Occupation: Practicing Resistance in Palestine which won the Middle East Monitor’s 2022 Palestine Book Award for Best Academic Book. Lara is a member of the founding collective for the Institute for the Critical Study of Zionism and is on the advisory board for Forensic Architecture.
Make sure you are subscribed and support the Psychic Militancy podcast and The Red Nation podcast and Red Media.

entry.title: [EAST] Iran War Diary (Day 29) w/ Kumars Salehi and Navid Zarrinnal
entry.summary:
**We managed to get Navid online for a conversation on life in Tehran. This episode is co-published with Kumars at Delete Ur Account**
This week’s episode is another collaboration with The Colony Archive and The East is a Podcast. As Roqayah continues to document the resistance to Israel’s attempted ethnic cleansing of south Lebanon, Kumars and friend of the show Sina Rahmani reconnected with Navid Zarrinnal in Tehran after two weeks.
Navid updates us on the current mood in Iran as US-Israeli bombardment continues, sharing his experiences of the significance of Quds Day and Palestinian liberation to people in Iran, and how Iranians are responding to their country’s resilience. The gang also discusses the political divisions within the Iranian establishment, the source of accusations of corruption and economic mismanagement, and what a strategic defeat for the US and Israel means for Iran and the world.
Don’t miss Navid’s urgent reporting and analysis for The Colony Archive on YouTube.
@TheColonyArchive

entry.title: [DDP] How Close Did Iran and America Really Come? | Bushra Shaikh & Dr. Helyeh Doutaghi
entry.summary:
Bushra Shaikh & Dr. Helyeh Doutaghi expose Trump's War on Iran as illegal aggression — why US miscalculations are now pushing Iran toward nuclear deterrence. 🔴 As Trump's threats to destroy Iranian civilian infrastructure dominate the iran war news cycle, the question of US intent has never been more urgent. Bushra and Dr. Helyeh — a geopolitical analyst and an academic based in Tehran — argue this is not regime change. It is balkanization: a deliberate strategy to fracture Iran and plunder its resources, driven by Israel and wrapped in Western propaganda designed to manufacture consent for illegal war. With the Israel-Iran war escalating and Iranian public opinion shifting dramatically toward nuclearization, this is the conversation the mainstream won't have.
In This Episode:
✅ Why Trump's attacks on gas plants, desalination facilities, and civilian energy grids constitute war crimes under international law — and what the US is actually targeting ✅ How the US war strategy has no coherent direction — and why Israel is driving the agenda behind the scenes ✅ How Western propaganda systematically dehumanises Iran to manufacture public consent for illegal military aggression ✅ Why Iran's fully decentralised infrastructure makes it impossible for US-Zionist forces to cripple the country — and why Washington is now in panic mode ✅ Why Iranian public support for nuclear weapons has surged from 30% to over 90% since the attacks began — and what this means for regional deterrence ✅ How October 7 "unmasked" Western liberal values — and what that reveals about the so-called rules-based international order
⏱️ CHAPTERS: 00:00 - Intro: The Escalating War in West Asia 01:10 - The Impact of US Attacks on Iranian Infrastructure 03:04 - Understanding the Legality of the Aggression 05:09 - The Incoherent Strategy: Why is the US Doing This? 06:59 - Deconstructing the Western Propaganda on Iran 11:59 - The Reality of Living in Iran vs. the Media Portrayal 15:20 - How Sanctions and Warfare Build Resistance 19:54 - Is Balkanization the Ultimate Goal for the US? 23:28 - Iran's Strategic Decentralization 36:33 - The Truth About Human Development in Iran 41:32 - The "Imperialist Boomerang": How Tactics Return Home 46:00 - Conclusion: The Unintended Consequences of Trump's Policies
🎙️ ABOUT Bushra Shaikh: Bushra Rache is a geopolitical analyst specialising in Middle East politics, US foreign policy, and Western imperialism. Known for her sharp, independent analysis of resistance movements and US strategic miscalculations, she is a regular contributor to Deep Dive Perspective. 🎙️ ABOUT DR. Helyeh Doutaghi: Dr. Helyeh is an academic and geopolitical analyst based in Tehran, specialising in international law, US-Iran relations, and anti-imperialist resistance theory. Expelled from Yale Law School for her vocal support of Palestine and Iran, she brings a rare and authoritative on-the-ground perspective from inside the country.
🌊 ABOUT DEEP DIVE PERSPECTIVE: Deep Dive Perspective explains how power, politics, and global systems really work — in a clear, honest, and unfiltered way. We cover geopolitics, war analysis, Middle East news, US foreign policy, and international relations with guests and experts from around the world.
📌 Subscribe for weekly deep dives: https://www.youtube.com/@DeepDivePerspective
🔗 WATCH NEXT: ▶️ https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XB6frMBm1e4 ▶️ https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RPAj8Y0rOLA

entry.content:
Bushra Shaikh & Dr. Helyeh Doutaghi expose Trump's War on Iran as illegal aggression — why US miscalculations are now pushing Iran toward nuclear deterrence. 🔴
As Trump's threats to destroy Iranian civilian infrastructure dominate the iran war news cycle, the question of US intent has never been more urgent. Bushra and Dr. Helyeh — a geopolitical analyst and an academic based in Tehran — argue this is not regime change. It is balkanization: a deliberate strategy to fracture Iran and plunder its resources, driven by Israel and wrapped in Western propaganda designed to manufacture consent for illegal war. With the Israel-Iran war escalating and Iranian public opinion shifting dramatically toward nuclearization, this is the conversation the mainstream won't have.

In This Episode:

✅ Why Trump's attacks on gas plants, desalination facilities, and civilian energy grids constitute war crimes under international law — and what the US is actually targeting
✅ How the US war strategy has no coherent direction — and why Israel is driving the agenda behind the scenes
✅ How Western propaganda systematically dehumanises Iran to manufacture public consent for illegal military aggression
✅ Why Iran's fully decentralised infrastructure makes it impossible for US-Zionist forces to cripple the country — and why Washington is now in panic mode
✅ Why Iranian public support for nuclear weapons has surged from 30% to over 90% since the attacks began — and what this means for regional deterrence
✅ How October 7 "unmasked" Western liberal values — and what that reveals about the so-called rules-based international order


⏱️ CHAPTERS:
00:00 - Intro: The Escalating War in West Asia
01:10 - The Impact of US Attacks on Iranian Infrastructure
03:04 - Understanding the Legality of the Aggression
05:09 - The Incoherent Strategy: Why is the US Doing This?
06:59 - Deconstructing the Western Propaganda on Iran
11:59 - The Reality of Living in Iran vs. the Media Portrayal
15:20 - How Sanctions and Warfare Build Resistance
19:54 - Is Balkanization the Ultimate Goal for the US?
23:28 - Iran's Strategic Decentralization
36:33 - The Truth About Human Development in Iran
41:32 - The "Imperialist Boomerang": How Tactics Return Home
46:00 - Conclusion: The Unintended Consequences of Trump's Policies

🎙️ ABOUT Bushra Shaikh:
Bushra Rache is a geopolitical analyst specialising in Middle East politics, US foreign policy, and Western imperialism. Known for her sharp, independent analysis of resistance movements and US strategic miscalculations, she is a regular contributor to Deep Dive Perspective.
🎙️ ABOUT DR. Helyeh Doutaghi:
Dr. Helyeh is an academic and geopolitical analyst based in Tehran, specialising in international law, US-Iran relations, and anti-imperialist resistance theory. Expelled from Yale Law School for her vocal support of Palestine and Iran, she brings a rare and authoritative on-the-ground perspective from inside the country.

🌊 ABOUT DEEP DIVE PERSPECTIVE:
Deep Dive Perspective explains how power, politics, and global systems really work — in a clear, honest, and unfiltered way. We cover geopolitics, war analysis, Middle East news, US foreign policy, and international relations with guests and experts from around the world.

The war on Iran is part of a larger attempt by the US empire to reverse the decline of Western hegemony and recolonize the Global South. After invading Venezuela, Donald Trump wants to "take" Cuba, Greenland, and Gaza. Meanwhile, Israel is annexing the West Bank and colonizing southern Lebanon. Ben Norton explains the imperialist plan.

entry.title: [EAST] "War, War until Victory!": Iran resists ZioAmerican aggression w/ Sara Larijani and Taha Zeinali
entry.summary:
Sara Larijani is a PostDoc fellow in Political Geography at the University of Tehran. Her research is on British colonialism in Iran's oil frontiers.
Taha Zeinali (tahazeinalih) is a doctoral researcher in development studies at the Autonomous University of Barcelona. His research focus is imperialist hybrid war, sanctions and sovereign development in Iran.
They are both co-founders of the Center for Resistance, Sovereignty and Development Studies at the University of Tehran.

entry.title: [EAST] A Call for Revolutionary Intellectual Alignment w/Dr. Ghassan Abu Sittah
entry.summary:
This livestream collaboration with Lara Sheehi and Psychic Militancy discusses an important document "Toward a Revolutionary Charter for Comprehensive Liberation" released recently that takes an adamant and uncompromising critique of contemporary Arab intelligentsias and calls for a radical realignment against colonial knowledges and with the masses. This is a critical juncture in West Asia; the convulsions of the Gaza genocide, the war on Iran, and the reshaping of its political boundaries, economies and societies in violent colonial war and resistance demand that all social groupings and classes address themselves to revolutionary anticolonial struggle.
Lara and I speak with one of the authors of this radical manifesto, Dr. Ghassan Abu Sittah, the famous Palestinian surgeon, academic, activist, and Rector of Glasgow University in Scotland. We'll take up the questions of why "pessimism of the intellect" has not been accompanied by "optimist of the will" among the intellectual classes, the manifestations of "defeatist" and comprador thought, and how to revive the courageous role of intellectuals organically in the people's struggle as a resource for liberation. Centered on the question of Palestine but addressed to the wider Arab peoples confronting Zionism, colonialism, and imperialism, the conversation will explore the document's six key principles and concepts like rooted knowledge, intellectual sovereignty, democratizing knowledge and how ideology can be turned into a material force for liberation.
This will be a fascinating discussion! Join us.
You can read a translation of the document here: https://en.al-akhbar.com/news/toward-a-revolutionary-charter-for-comprehensive-liberation1
Follow Dr. Ghassan Abu Sittah on X: https://x.com/GhassanAbuSitt1
Subscribe to Psychic Militancy: https://www.youtube.com/@PsychicMilitancy
Support the Sameer Project's Ramadan Campaign: https://chuffed.org/project/166623-sameer-project-x-ramadan-campaign

entry.title: [EAST] The Ramadan War: Iran strikes back w/ Helyeh Doutaghi
entry.summary:
Friend of the show Bikrum Gill is joined by Helyeh Doutaghi to discuss the resistance of the Islamic Republic of Iran against the US-Zionist war of aggression. The discussion focuses on the strategic objectives pursued by Iran in its resistance, and what the larger stakes are for the region and the world-system as a whole. It considers how Iran’s resistance represents a historic advance for forces of anti-imperialism. Finally, the episode explores the basis of the specific social, historical, and theological bases of Iranian sovereignty.
Helyeh Doutaghi is scholar of international law and geopolitical economy. Her research explores the intersections of the Third World Approaches to International Law (TWAIL), encompassing postcolonial critiques of law, sanctions, and international political economy. Dr. Doutaghi’s research draws on the mechanisms, harms, and beneficiaries of the sanctions regime imposed on Iran, centering questions of value transfer and wealth drain. Additionally, she is interested in International Humanitarian Law (IHL), having written about its history, practice, and the production of knowledge (and ignorance), particularly in the context of the US military.
She was expelled from Yale Law School and the LPE project for speaking up for Palestinian liberation last year. She is currently a post-doctoral fellow at the University of Tehran, where she will focus on completing her manuscript on the Iranian sanctions regime and neoliberalism.
Bikrum Gill is a scholar of international political economy, and author of "The Political Ecology of Colonial Capitalism: Race, Nature, and Accumulation", published by Manchester University Press.
Consider supporting the show www.patreon.com/east_podcast

entry.title: [EAST] LIVE NOW - Iran Vs. The Declining Kayfabe Empire featuring Sina Rahmani & Kumars Salehi

We'll talk about this idea in relation to how the US Empire projects itself globally, and particularly in light of massive setbacks being faced by Israel & the US in their war on Iran and the Arab-Iranian region.

entry.title: [EAST] The prehistory of Al Aqsa Flood w/ Mujamma Harakat (pt.1)
entry.summary:
***This conversation with Mujamma stretched slightly over two hours and covered a lot of terrain. Part 2 will be available to patrons in the coming days, as will the video edition of the entire episode. If a few of you sign up for the Patreon, I will unlock it in a couple of weeks on the free feed.***
The first half of a two-part episode with academic and translator Mujamma Harakat about the historical trajectory of actors, social forces, and ideologies that propelled the Islamic Resistance Movement in Palestine (HAMAS) from its early history to today.
Check out his latest piece in Liberated Texts https://liberatedtexts.com/reviews/chronicling-a-unifying-resistance-figure-ghassan-duarsimad-aql-the-legend-of-jihad-and-resistance/

entry.title: [SYR] Kevork Almassian Warns: The Empire Above Jeffrey Epstein
entry.summary:
Jeffrey Epstein files, Epstein emails, elite networks, hidden power, leverage, and media narratives—in this Syriana Analysis clip, Kevork Almassian explains why Epstein may be the surface of a much bigger system.
In this segment, I break down: • Why the Epstein document drop should be read as a power-network story, not tabloid entertainment • How “leverage” works in elite politics—and why it matters more than any single scandal • How narrative management shapes what the public focuses on (and what gets ignored) • Why institutions often protect networks first—and citizens last • What this means for public trust, accountability, and geopolitics
⚠️ This is analysis and commentary. We discuss publicly circulating documents and media reporting and do not treat allegations as proven facts. The focus is on systems, incentives, and accountability.
👍 Like • 🔔 Subscribe • 💬 Comment: Do you think the Epstein story is being contained—or redirected?

entry.title: [EAST] Office Hours 23-The Treason of the Clerics w/Justin Podur
entry.summary:
The intellectuals and intelligentsias, like Noam Chomsky, have been exposed as much as any of the corporate and political elites in the Epstein revelations. What is the role of "the intellectuals"? The "Treason of the Clerics" comes from Julian Benda's bemoaning of collaborationist intellectuals, the clerics, of society who betray their sacred duty of dispassionate critique. As we know, even that is a mirage in what Gabriel Rockhill calls the Intellectual World War. Who are the intellectuals of practice, the organic and oppositional leaders, in the struggle for liberation instead? Joining me to talk about this as well as the stifling of Cuba, threats on Iran, and other theatres of US empire is Justin Podur, a professor at York University, author of several important books analyzing US empire, and host of the indispensible Anti-Empire Project and corollary Anti-Genocide Project podcasts and video. Office Hours comes at an early afternoon time, 1pmET Friday Feb 13th so don't miss it!
Support the show on Patreon if you can (and get early access to episodes)! www.patreon.com/adnanhusain Or make a one-time donation to the show and Buy Me a Coffee: https://buymeacoffee.com/adnanhusain Like, subscribe, share! Also available as audio podcast on all major platforms: https://adnanhusainshow.libsyn.com X: @adnanahusain Substack: adnanahusain.substack.com www.adnanhusain.org
🎙️ New to streaming or looking to level up? Check out StreamYard and get $10 discount! 😍 https://streamyard.com/pal/d/5595083013226496

The "Treason of the Clerics" comes from Julian Benda's bemoaning of collaborationist intellectuals, the clerics, of society who betray their sacred duty of dispassionate critique. As we know, even that is a mirage in what Gabriel Rockhill calls the Intellectual World War. Who are the intellectuals of practice, the organic and oppositional leaders, in the struggle for liberation instead? Joining me to talk about this as well as the stifling of Cuba, threats on Iran, and other theatres of US empire is Justin Podur, a professor at York University, author of several important books analyzing US empire, and host of the indispensible Anti-Empire Project and corollary Anti-Genocide Project podcasts and video. Office Hours comes at an early afternoon time, 1pmET Friday Feb 13th so don't miss it!

entry.title: [SYR] Tarik Cyril Amar: Why Iran Can’t Compromise — and Why Trump Hesitates
entry.summary:
Trump Iran talks, “compromise,” Netanyahu pressure, Iran missile deterrent, and the risk of war—in this Syriana Analysis clip, historian Tarik Cyril Amar explains why concessions rarely end pressure, and why Trump may hesitate.
In this segment, Tarik breaks down:
● Why “compromise” can become a trap that invites more demands
● The role of messaging and pressure politics in Washington
● Why Iran’s deterrence posture (especially missiles) is treated as a red-line issue
● Why a major war would be unpredictable and costly—making hesitation rational even for hardliners
● How diplomacy, threats, and media narratives can be used together as leverage
This clip is an analysis and commentary based on publicly reported developments and historical patterns. The goal is clarity, not hype.
👍 Like • 🔔 Subscribe • 💬 Comment: Do you think pressure leads to a deal—or escalation?

entry.title: [EAST] Who Paid the Pipers of Western Marxism?
entry.summary:
**Producer's note: The folks at Popular Front Canada asked me to moderate this discusssion of Gabriel Rockhill's latest book on the history of the Frankfurt School. Make sure to check out Popular Front!
Unfortunately, my recording software crashed 75 mins into the episode, so I had to use the (notably inferior) backup camera feed from that point on. **
---
We’re thrilled to host author and philosopher Gabriel Rockhill on his book tour in Toronto. One of the key intellectuals of our movement today, his latest work explores ideas that are essential to understanding the challenges and necessities in building a socialist movement—especially for those of us living in the West.
Starting with a talk from the author, followed by a discussion with a guest panel and an audience Q&A – this will be an important evening to explore the past, present, and future of socialist thought, and the principles we need to uphold in order to succeed (which the ruling class desperately wants us to abandon).
Featuring an exciting guest panel, including: Sina Rahmani (co-host) Sardana Nikolaeva Justin Podur Zeyad El-Nabolsy
Special thanks to our partners in co-organizing this event: Qiao Collective (@qiaocollective) Critical Theory Workshop (@criticaltheoryworkshop) Monthly Review (@monthlyreview) East is a Podcast (@eastpodcast) And to our additional media partners: Anti-Empire Project (@jpodur) Journal of International Solidarity (@journalofintlsolidarity)
Popular Front Canada
https://www.instagram.com/popularfrontcanada
---
ABOUT THE BOOK: “Rockhill’s book elucidates how the intellectual world war on the socialist alternative has sought to promote a “compatible left” intelligentsia while misrepresenting, maligning, and trying to destroy the revolutionary left. […] The volume concludes by bringing to the fore the guiding methodological framework: a thoroughly anticolonial and anti-imperialist Marxism dedicated to building socialism in the real world.”
More info at Monthly Review Press: https://monthlyreview.org/9781685901349/
Canada: Available at most online book sellers, including University of Toronto Bookstore: https://utpdistribution.com/9781685901349/who-paid-the-pipers-of-western-marxism/

entry.title: [BE] Holocaust Education Is for Israel
entry.summary:
Why are we so uniquely aware of the Holocaust? In this video I explain how this oversaturation is the result of a project that is, in large part, intended to make people more sympathetic to Israel. Support me on Patreon: https://patreon.com/BadEmpanada Subscribe to my Substack: https://ofpatmos.substack.com/ One-time donations: https://ko-fi.com/BadEmpanada Second Channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCjY9R3_6sxfUViabj93Vnhw Follow me on Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/badempanada.com
Sources (by order of first appearance): [1] https://www.claimscon.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/Claims-Conference-Holocaust-Survey-US-Topline-F1.14.25.pdf [2] https://www.pewresearch.org/religion/2020/01/22/what-americans-know-about-the-holocaust/ [3] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holocaust_education [4] https://www.instagram.com/p/DT-0wtbj6wr/ [5] https://x.com/HolocaustUK/status/2016167232141721666 [6] https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1177/0002716208314803 [7] https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/14623528.2024.2448061 [8] https://www.yadvashem.org/es/education/educational-materials/lesson-plans/antisemitism/israel-palestine.html [9] https://web.archive.org/web/20250327080122/https://www.yadvashem.org/holocaust/holocaust-antisemitism/faqs.html [10] https://holocaustremembrance.com/who-we-are/member-countries [11] https://holocaustremembrance.com/resources/working-definition-antisemitism [12] https://www.ushmm.org/information/press/press-releases/president-obama-appointments-to-us-holocaust-memorial-council-1 [13] https://www.cair.com/press_releases/breaking-cair-calls-for-removal-of-us-holocaust-memorial-council-member-who-claims-all-palestinians-in-gaza-are-unworthy-of-any-mercy-are-collectively-guilty-and/ [14] https://salzburg.orf.at/stories/3267915/ [15] https://www.swissinfo.ch/spa/justicia-argentina-procesa-a-diputada-de-frente-de-izquierda-por-acusar-a-israel-de-%22nazi%22/89128175 [16] The issue of the Holocaust as a unique event, Alan Rosenberg & Evelyn Silverman. [17] https://encyclopedia.ushmm.org/content/en/article/gassing-operations [18] https://encyclopedia.ushmm.org/content/en/article/auschwitz-key-dates [19] The American West and the Nazi East, Carroll P. Becker & others. [24] https://www.australianjewishnews.com/shoah-museums-in-every-state-territory/ [25] Making Visible the Australian Frontier Wars and Comparative Memorialization, Brook Andrew & others. entry.title: [SYR] Historian Calls Out Noam Chomsky After Epstein Files | Tarik Cyril Amar
entry.summary:
Epstein files, Noam Chomsky, and elite influence networks — in this Syriana Analysis clip, historian Tarik Cyril Amar explains why the Epstein context raises serious questions about credibility, gatekeeping, and how “public intellectuals” relate to power.
We discuss:
● Why the Epstein document drop changed how many viewers interpret elite “intellectual” circles
● Chomsky’s public image vs the credibility cost of elite proximity
● How institutions protect reputations—and why narrative framing matters
● What viewers should treat as documented vs what remains unproven
⚠️ This is analysis and commentary. We do not present unverified claims as established facts. The focus is on evidence, context, and public trust.

entry.title: [SYR] The REAL Issue with the Epstein Files: Power & Influence | Kevork Almassian
entry.summary:
Epstein files, power and influence networks, elite protection, media narratives, and what’s actually known — in this video, Kevork Almassian breaks down the REAL issue behind the document drop: not gossip, but how power works.
This isn’t tabloid entertainment. It’s a case study in access, reputation management, and institutional protection — how certain circles gain leverage, shape narratives, and survive scandal. We separate what’s documented from what’s alleged, and focus on incentives: who benefits from specific framings, and why some stories dominate while others disappear.
In this video:
● Why “document drops” can manage outrage while preserving deeper networks
● How influence is built through philanthropy, academia, media access, and politics
● Why unverified claims spread fast — and how to stay evidence-based
● What this means for public trust and geopolitics
⚠️ This is analysis and commentary based on publicly circulating documents and reporting. We do not present unverified allegations as established facts.
👍 Like • 🔔 Subscribe • 💬 Comment: What question do these files raise that the media won’t touch?

entry.title: [EAST] "They won't leave us alone": Organized chaos and hybrid war in Iran w/ Navid Zarrinal
entry.summary:
***Note: Navid's internet has been very unreliable for weeks now, so when I managed to get hold of him with a stable VPN, we decided to hurriedly record. In haste, both of us managed to mangle our respective recordings. I have been forced to resort to--please forgive me, cruel podcast gods---AI audio enhancer tools to see if it could be improved, which it mostly did but there are some funny spots on my side.***
Navid Zarrinal, host of the Colony Archive (@TheColonyArchive) and one of the oldest friends of The East is a Podcast, described what he witnessed during the last few weeks in Tehran.

entry.title: [EAST] "Listen to the Iranian": Sina Rahmani (The East is a Podcast)
entry.summary:
**Note: When stuff in Iran goes down, my natural inclination is to avoid doing content and stay above the fray, at least until the media hysteria dies down. Not the best way to run a media project, I know, but I just have a hard time with all the breathless coverage--BREAKING!!!--and don't really want to feed it. Even then, I am still Iranian, and if people ask me to record during these times, my cultural programming compels me to accept the invitation.
This episode of @FREDSHETSARNA with Mehmet and Erik was recorded last Friday as I was travelling for work and at the end of a busy, emotional day. The scale of what had just transpired in Iran was becoming clear to me that morning in between working on the footage I had recorded the day earlier, itself an intense and emotional experience for entirely different, non-Iran-related reasons.
All of this is to say that by the time I recorded with these guys that afternoon, I was feeling pretty loopy. You can kinda hear it at the beginning of the episode when I had the giggles and infected everyone else with them! I have no idea how I come off in this episode (too lazy to check it too, sorry), and I was reluctant to even post to this feed!! In my memory, I was exceptionally wandering in my rants. Maybe I'm wrong and it's the same Sini as usual. IDK.**
----------------------
"Listen to the Iranians" they kept saying.
Well, we give you Sina Rahmani, the indomitable host of @eastpodcast and producer of countless other anti-imperialist shows.
We talk about the events in Iran which have been one-sidedly and sensationally presented in the west. In the background the Empire and the Ethno-state have their fingers dirty.
So why is Iran such an important target for the Empire and what does it mean to be sovereign?
We discuss some of the contents of the imperial toolbox and factors leading up to the protests. Economic pressure with sanctions and currency manipulation, smuggling of Starlink units and weapons to foment violent riots and the media machinery.
We discuss the Shah and his son, the clown-prince in exile, Iranian diaspora, support for Palestine and what the destruction of the Iranian state would mean to people in the area.
While the battle may be over the war might just have begun. The peoples and states in the crosshairs of a dying empire will keep being demonised. If they resist they are authoritarian, if they open up they’ll be vulnerable. What the future holds and what the way forward to avoid a devastating war is unknown.
Early in the episode Sina reads a testimony from his friend Navid Zarinnal, who is living in Teheran. He hosts a podcast on imperialism called The Colony Archive, which we highly recommend.
Please note that we had some technical difficulties in the beginning, that led to a lot of chaos and laughter. Big thanks to Sina for being such a joyful human being even in these difficult times.
The genocide in Gaza continues. We urge you to support the Palestinians in these very dire times by donating:
- via @thesameerproject - via @lifeline4gaza

entry.title: [LOI] Agent Orange: A War Crime Without End in Vietnam
entry.summary:
For five decades, Vietnam has been struggling with the aftermath of Agent Orange. This was one of the worst war crimes ever committed by the USA, causing harm to generations of innocent Vietnamese as well as descendants of American war veterans. entry.title: [EAST] Showing up for Refaat Alareer’s Family in Gaza: A Fundraiser with Special Guests
entry.summary:
Two years after the assassination of Palestinian martyr Dr. Refaat Alareer his legacy and written work continue to make a profound impact on the movement for Palestinian liberation and self-determination globally, with a particular emphasis on the plight of Palestinian people in Gaza, where Dr. Alareer was an English professor who influenced thousands of Palestinian writers and intellectuals.
https://chuffed.org/project/163752-financial-aid-for-refaat-alareers-family
The surviving members of Palestinian martyr Dr. Refaat Alareer, supported by Refaat's brother Adel, have unfortunately had their family fundraiser mismanaged by a sponsor in the US and are out roughly $20,000 as a result. This family means support and it is politically and morally important that we show up for them. There is a new fundraiser set-up by a very responsible and committed individual which will ensure that all funds get to the family: https://chuffed.org/project/163752-fi...
An all-star line-up including Louis Allday (Liberated Texts, Ebb Books), Dr. Jared A. Ball (Black Liberation Media, IMIXWHATILIKE), Renee Johnston (Saturdays With Renee, BLMedia), Sina Rahmani (East is a Podcast), Benji Schoendorff (Resistance is Fertile), and Lara Sheehi (Psychic Militancy) wll join us! Other guests may be announced or join us live!
Come through and hear from some wonderful guests and make a contribution to Refaat's family!

entry.title: [BE] This is Zionist Propaganda - The TRUTH About Modern Hebrew
entry.summary:
Linguistics YouTuber 'Languagejones' made a video claiming to be debunking propaganda about the reconstruction of modern Hebrew. In truth, it's very generic, entirely unsourced pro-Israel propaganda. Here I respond to it, explain how modern Hebrew actually came about, and refute a bunch of the other tired old Zionist arguments that he makes throughout the video. The 'reconstruction' of modern Hebrew was a very intentional, nationalistic, colonialistic, political endeavour, and his video is based around attempting to deny this reality by talking about just about everything EXCEPT how and why the reconstruction movement actually formed. entry.title: [SYR] EU Blacklist: Jacques Baud and Nathalie Yamb Targeted for ‘Hybrid Threats’ | Syriana Analysis
entry.summary:
EU Blacklist: Jacques Baud and Nathalie Yamb Targeted for ‘Hybrid Threats’ | Syriana Analysis
EU measures against so-called “Russian hybrid threats”, information manipulation and alleged propaganda – and what this means for free speech and civil liberties in Europe – are the focus of this video on Syriana Analysis. On 15 December 2025, the Council of the EU expanded its “hybrid threats” listing regime “in view of Russia’s destabilising activities,” adding new individuals and entities. Those listed face asset freezes and travel bans, with serious consequences for their banking, contracts, mobility and reputation.
Officially, this toolbox targets foreign information manipulation and interference (FIMI), cyber activity and other hybrid actions linked to the Russian state. But the Council’s own explanations now include terms such as “propaganda,” “conspiracy theories” and “information manipulation” when it justifies adding people to the list. In this monologue I ask a simple question:
👉 When the EU freezes a person’s assets and blocks their travel because of what they say, what safeguards exist for free expression and due process? I explain how these restrictive measures work, how they affect the lives of commentators and public figures, and why using executive asset freezes against people like Jacques Baud or Nathalie Yamb raises serious concerns about proportionality, transparency and legal standards.
🧠 In this video we cover: • The new EU “hybrid threats” framework and FIMI toolbox • How “propaganda”, “conspiracy theories” and “information manipulation” appear in EU justifications • The difference between prosecuting espionage or cyber attacks and punishing speech • Why asset freezes and travel bans are not a “debate” but an exercise of power • What safeguards and review mechanisms are needed if Europe wants to remain credible as a defender of democracy
This is not about agreeing with any particular commentator. It is about asking whether administrative blacklisting is the right tool to answer controversial opinions – and what happens to European democracy if criticism can be branded a “hybrid threat” by political decision.
🔔 Subscribe to Syriana Analysis for independent coverage of Europe, Russia, hybrid warfare and global geopolitics. 👍 If you found this useful, please like, comment and share – especially with friends in the EU who care about civil liberties.
📌 Support the Channel: 🅿️ Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Syrianaanalysis ▶️ YouTube Membership: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UClvD6c1VI75QZWJjA_yiWhg/join 💳 PayPal Donations: https://www.paypal.me/kevorkalmassian ☕ Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kevorkios1l
📕 Creative Chaos: Inside the CIA's Covert War to Topple the Syrian Government: 🇺🇸 USA: https://amzn.to/4lllPXk 🇪🇺 Europe: https://amzn.to/41sub8A
📢 Disclaimer: Guest opinions featured in this video do not necessarily reflect the views of Syriana Analysis.
🎥 Syriana Analysis is an independent platform providing in-depth geopolitical insights, founded by award-winning journalist Kevork Almassian. This channel aims to offer well-researched commentary that broadens understanding of international relations, conflict zones, and media narratives.
#SyrianaAnalysis #EU #HybridThreats #FreeSpeech #Russia #InformationWarfare #CivilLiberties #Europe #Geopolitics

In this monologue I ask a simple question:

👉 When the EU freezes a person’s assets and blocks their travel because of what they say, what safeguards exist for free expression and due process?
I explain how these restrictive measures work, how they affect the lives of commentators and public figures, and why using executive asset freezes against people like Jacques Baud or Nathalie Yamb raises serious concerns about proportionality, transparency and legal standards.

🧠 In this video we cover:
• The new EU “hybrid threats” framework and FIMI toolbox
• How “propaganda”, “conspiracy theories” and “information manipulation” appear in EU justifications
• The difference between prosecuting espionage or cyber attacks and punishing speech
• Why asset freezes and travel bans are not a “debate” but an exercise of power
• What safeguards and review mechanisms are needed if Europe wants to remain credible as a defender of democracy

This is not about agreeing with any particular commentator. It is about asking whether administrative blacklisting is the right tool to answer controversial opinions – and what happens to European democracy if criticism can be branded a “hybrid threat” by political decision.

🔔 Subscribe to Syriana Analysis for independent coverage of Europe, Russia, hybrid warfare and global geopolitics.
👍 If you found this useful, please like, comment and share – especially with friends in the EU who care about civil liberties.

entry.title: [EAST] "We are simply people who love life": Voices from the Gaza holocaust
entry.summary:
**Note: As I explain in the introduction to this video, this is a very special episode that has been 2 months in the making. It consists of two halves featuring three Palestinians in Gaza sharing their stories. It has become more than evident that our racist ruling class and their media warriors are simply refusing to allow anything that humanizes Palestinians in the slightest into the mainstream. It falls to us to help break the narrative blockade on Palestinians and their liberation struggle. I hope to do more of this content in the future, so if you know people in Gaza who want to make their own episodes in this format, reach out to me, and we can work with Lina to get it translated into English and posted. Maybe if we have enough episodes, we can spin it off into its own channel**
A very special episode featuring my friends Mohammed and Mahmoud, who both share their stories of the early days of the genocide and some of their memories of the last two years.
Please consider donating to their campaigns
Mohammed's campaign https://chuffed.org/project/120676-mohammed-from-gaza
Mahmoud's campaign https://chuffed.org/project/141277-help-mahmoud-and-his-family-survive-and-stand-again
In the second half of the episode, long-time friend of the podcast Migdalia Arcila-Valenzuela interviews Lina Jehad, an Arabic teacher and translator based in Gaza, about her work and struggles during the genocide.
Hire Lina for Arabic lessons or translation work https://www.instagram.com/lina_jehad1212/

entry.title: [SYR] American Parents Need to Hear Why Their Sons Are in Syria | Syriana Analysis
entry.summary:
US troops in Syria and American soldiers deployed in the Middle East are at the heart of this video. A Syrian talks directly to the American public and asks a simple question: Why is the US military still in Syria after so many years of war? This video highlights the complex "international relations" and ongoing "conflict" in the region, offering a perspective often missing from mainstream "world news" and "us news" reports about the "Syrian civil war" and the broader "Middle East".
Many Americans have been told a straightforward story about why US forces went into Syria: • to protect the homeland, • to confront a dangerous group, • to promote stability and democracy.
This video offers a different perspective.
I explain how Syria became a central arena of US foreign policy, how large-scale programs supported armed groups on the ground, and how the conflict was shaped long before most Americans had even heard of places like al-Tanf or eastern Syria’s oil and wheat regions.
I then outline four main reasons why US troops were deployed and kept in Syria: • control over key energy fields, • presence at important transport corridors, • influence over agricultural areas that sustain millions of people, • and the ability to shape any future political settlement.
Today, with a completely new leadership structure in Damascus, US personnel are still on the ground, working alongside local partners and carrying out joint activities far from American soil.
The central question of this video is simple:
👉 If you believe the US military should primarily defend American territory and citizens, what exactly is the long-term mission of US troops in Syria today?
This is not about hostility toward the United States. It is about asking whether current policy in Syria truly serves the American nation and its families, or whether it mainly serves distant strategic and economic agendas.

entry.title: [SYR] America Turns Transactional: The Real Implications of Trump's 2025 Strategy | Syriana Analysis
entry.summary:
In this video, Kevork Almassian breaks down the 2025 U.S. National Security Strategy – a document that quietly declares the end of the unipolar moment and lays out a much colder, more transactional American foreign policy.
On paper, the new NSS looks dry. In reality, it is one of the most brutal self-critiques Washington has ever published – and at the same time, a roadmap for a fortress-empire America that defends interests, not values.
We look at how the strategy: • Admits the unipolar dream failed, and that U.S. elites hollowed out their own economy, society and middle class • Shifts from liberal hegemony to hard realism: spheres of influence, deals, balance of power • Rebrands Europe from “partner” to problem, warning of “civilizational erasure” and a continent “unrecognizable in 20 years” • Questions endless NATO expansion and talks about restoring strategic stability with Russia • Treats Europe as a declining, over-regulated protectorate, not a co-equal pole • Frees Washington to focus more on China, the Indo-Pacific and a 21st-century Monroe Doctrine • Replaces moral crusades with power politics: sanctions, trade blackmail and control of the dollar system
From a multipolar perspective, the document is both an obituary for the old system and a warning: less ideology does not mean less conflict – it just means the conflicts become more honest and more brutal.

entry.title: [BE] Requiem for Chomsky
entry.summary:
Noam Chomsky was the most prominent left-wing intellectual in the West for over 50 years. Recent revelations about his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein may complicate this legacy. In this video I take a look at Chomsky, aspects of his career, and what we can learn from it. I also investigate common claim made against him by his detractors (that he is a 'Genocide Denier' regarding Cambodia, Bosnia, and Rwanda). Support me on Patreon: https://patreon.com/BadEmpanada Follow me on Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/badempanada.com Become a member on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/BadEmpanada/join One-time donations: https://ko-fi.com/BadEmpanada Second Channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCjY9R3_6sxfUViabj93Vnhw

[10] https://chomsky.info/19770625/ [11] https://www.theguardian.com/uk/2000/mar/15/medialaw.media2 [12] https://www.theguardian.com/media/2000/feb/21/pressandpublishing.mondaymediasection6 [13] https://chomsky.info/20060425/ [14] World Orders, Old and New, Noam Chomsky. [15] https://www.monbiot.com/2012/05/21/2181/ [16] https://web.archive.org/web/20051103031628/http://books.guardian.co.uk/departments/politicsphilosophyandsociety/story/0,6000,1605276,00.html [17] https://www.theguardian.com/media/2005/nov/17/theguardian.pressandpublishing [18] https://chomsky.info/20051031/ [19] https://chomsky.info/20051113/ [20] https://www.theguardian.com/news/2006/may/25/leadersandreply.mainsection [21] https://chomsky.info/20060619/ [22] https://news.un.org/en/story/2007/02/210142 [23] https://www.elobservador.com.uy/argentina/politica/la-camara-federal-ratifica-el-procesamiento-vanina-biasi-incitacion-la-discriminacion-n6011908 [24] The Issue of Genocidal Intent and Denial of Genocide, Edina Becirevic. entry.title: [LOI] Here's How Vietnam has ELIMINATED Low Quality Housing!
entry.summary:
Vietnam has just completed repairing and replacing all low quality housing five years ahead of schedule. Here is how the Socialist Republic of Vietnam is dealing with poverty and low quality housing!

entry.title: [BE] How Graham Platner Exposes the U.S. Left
entry.summary:
Many on the U.S. left believe that killing the world's poorest people for money is fine, and that those who do it bare no responsibility whatsoever - provided they're American. This video explores this common tendency by analyzing recent controversy surrounding Democratic Party U.S. Senate candidate Graham Platner, a proud & unrepentant occupier of Iraq & Afghanistan and Blackwater mercenary. We will look at & refute the defenses of him by many American self-styled leftists, progressives, and anti-imperialists. Used as examples are Hasan Piker, Chapo Trap House, Breaking Points (Krystal Ball & Ryan Grim), and the Majority Report (Sam Seder & Emma Vigeland) - but there have been many more. entry.title: [SYR] The Truth About Middle East Geopolitics in 2025 | Syriana Analysis
entry.summary:
In this intense and deeply honest segment with Dr. Marwa Osman, Kevork Almassian of Syriana Analysis unpacks the painful truth behind the collapse of the Middle East after the destruction of Syria.
We discuss how decades of geopolitical engineering from the Zionist vision of the “New Middle East,” to U.S. meddling, Gulf intervention, sectarian manipulation, and the rise of militant proxies reshaped the region beyond recognition.
This conversation lays bare:
• Why Syria’s fall was the strategic breaking point of the region • How sectarian fragmentation and foreign-backed militias destroyed state sovereignty • How Israel, the U.S., and Gulf powers engineered the current map • Why Syria, Lebanon, Iraq, Jordan, and Palestine are now simultaneously destabilized • How economic warfare and proxy groups dismantled national resilience • Why the "resistance axis" failed to answer at the critical moment • How Gaza, Swaida, the Syrian coast, Lebanon’s collapse, and Idlib all connect • Why normalisation with Israel is now the driving force of regional politics • Why the Middle East is entering a historically dangerous phase
Follow Marwa: https://x.com/Marwa__Osman
🔔 Subscribe to stay informed: https://www.youtube.com/@SyrianaAnalysis/streams

entry.title: [LOI] Vietnam's Government has DECENTRALIZED! What does it mean for the people?
entry.summary:
Vietnam's government is now going through its most major changes since the end of the war against the USA in 1975. In a major overhaul, the government is completely eliminating the District level of administration in an effort to drastically cut bureaucracy. In this video, we explain how and why the government is making these changes and reflect on historical reasons for the old system as we make way for the new!

entry.title: [BE] Racism in Argentina: Myth vs Reality From 'Nazi Argentina' to 'Black Genocide'
entry.summary:
For some reason, people really like to talk about racism in Argentina. Aside from football, it's basically all that English speakers seem to think about regarding the country. So in this video I explore some of the common misconceptions on the topic (like the 'Nazi Argentina' and 'Black Genocide' tropes), then provide a comparative historical overview, and finally discuss what things are like today - and how they have recently been getting worse under current president Javier Milei. Support me on Patreon: https://patreon.com/BadEmpanada Follow my Instagram: https://instagram.com/BadEmpanada Become a member on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/BadEmpanada/join One-time donations: https://ko-fi.com/BadEmpanada Second Channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCjY9R3_6sxfUViabj93Vnhw
VIDEOS MENTIONED IN THIS VIDEO: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8mFeQ6gIfgg https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OaJDc85h3ME https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=R1gcipAvplY https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EeCu8pMHma0
Sources in pinned comment as the list is too long to fit into the description.

entry.title: [BE] Jewish Exceptionalism, Liberal Zionism, Jewish Supremacy - How YOU Inadvertently Support Israel
entry.summary:
There is a subtle ideology in Western society that leads many people to believe that certain types of Zionism are actually pro-Palestinian, leading well meaning people astray from truly understanding the root causes of the Palestinian problem and rendering them incapable of ever effectively fighting them. It's called Jewish exceptionalism, and in this video I dissect everything about it. In particular I address arguments from one of its most prominent 'left-wing' proponents over the last decade, Owen Jones. entry.title: [BE] The Appeal to Jewishness Fallacy, Explained & Dismantled
entry.summary: One of the bedrocks of present day pro-Israel propaganda, incredibly pervasive in Western countries in particular, is the idea that there's something exceptional about Jewish people that therefore uniquely justifies Israel & its atrocities - which is in turn weaponized to brand anyone who refuses to accept such a notion as 'Anti-Semitic'. In this video I dissect a great example of this sort of argument from Zionist YouTuber 'LonerBox' & outline how clearly invalid it is when you lay it out and think about it even slightly. Support me on Patreon: https://patreon.com/BadEmpanada Follow my Instagram: https://instagram.com/BadEmpanada Become a member on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/BadEmpanada/join One-time donations: https://ko-fi.com/BadEmpanada Second Channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCjY9R3_6sxfUViabj93Vnhw

There's recently been a boom in the Palestinian traitor industry, where Palestinians who are willing to spout pro-Israel talking points can benefit greatly from selling out their people. In this video I shed light on this practice by covering three examples: Mosab Hasan Yousef (The Son of Hamas), John Aziz, and Ahmed Fouad Al-Khatib. entry.title: [LOI] Vietnam defeated fascism. So can YOU!
entry.summary:
This video discusses the rise and fall of the fascist puppet regime which occupied and brutalized the South of Vietnam from 1954 until 1975. We learn how Vietnamese communists resisted, protested, and struggled against fascism under desperate and dangerous conditions and ultimately overthrew fascism and imperialism once and for all.

entry.title: [BE] The Truth About Israel - It's Not Just Netanyahu
entry.summary:
In this video I explain that some popular perceptions of Israel as a country where the government oppresses Palestinians against the will of the Israeli populace are extremely wrong. I explain how & why its society is so overwhelmingly supportive of their nations' crimes against the Palestinian people. I also explain how, if we applied the same standards commonly applied to Muslims to Israel, then Israel is basically Jewish IS1S, which recruits foreign fighters from all over the world just like they did. But in the case of Israel, this is totally accepted in the Western world. entry.title: [BE] The Nakba, The Partition & "The Expulsions" - Zionist Myths Refuted
entry.summary:
In this response to Ethan & Hila Klein, I go over three key topics related to 1948, the core of the Palestinian question, the nature of Zionism, & how and why they are distorted by Zionists like these two: the Nakba, the Partition, and the myth of "mass, uniform Jewish expulsion from Arab countries." I also touch on things like Arab Jewish culture (and its erasure), Israel as a European Jewish supremacist project, and how Zionists cynically use Arab Jews - and even Arab food - as little more than a tool against Palestinians. Support me on Patreon: https://patreon.com/BadEmpanada Follow my Instagram: https://instagram.com/BadEmpanada Become a member on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/BadEmpanada/join One-time donations: https://ko-fi.com/BadEmpanada Second Channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCjY9R3_6sxfUViabj93Vnhw
Sources: [1] https://www.aljazeera.com/features/2017/5/23/the-nakba-did-not-start-or-end-in-1948 [2] https://www.palquest.org/en/highlight/159/un-partition-plan-29-november-1947 [3] https://www.jstor.org/stable/3993428 [4] Israel and the Gaza Strip: The First Decade 1947-1957, Amon Golan. [5] https://www.timesofisrael.com/virgin-atlantic-removes-palestinian-from-couscous-description/ [6] The Invention of the Mizrahim, Ella Shohat. [7] 1949, The First Israelis, Tom Segev. [8] https://www.middleeasteye.net/big-story/truth-behind-israeli-propaganda-expulsion-arab-jews [9] Sephardim in Israel: Zionism from the Standpoint of Its Jewish Victims, Ella Shohat. [10] https://prrn.mcgill.ca/research/papers/shenhav.htm
00:00 Intro 02:10 The Nakba & The Partition 11:00 Collective Arab Responsibility Narrative 14:30 Arab Jews As a Tool For Zionism/Cultural Appropriation 27:30 The "Mass Uniform Expulsion" Narrative

entry.title: [LOI] Do Vietnamese Love Capitalism? Here's why you can't trust Pew Research Center
entry.summary:
Almost every day, some jerk sends me the same 10-year-old chart from Pew Research Center which they claim proves that Vietnamese love capitalism. It comes from Pew Research, and after I did some digging, I found out that it's total BS and capitalist propaganda.
So, what is Pew Research Center? Who is behind it?
In this video today, I will debunk the infamous chart from Pew Research Center claiming that 95% of Vietnamese “support the free market.”

entry.title: [LOI] What did LENIN say about DEMOCRACY? | Democratic Centralism Series
entry.summary:
What did Lenin think about democracy? How does this pertain to the modern socialist framework of democratic centralism?
Today, in part 3 of our Democratic Centralism series, we will explore the contribution that Lenin made in formulating the philosophy of democratic centralism and how it was practiced in the USSR!

entry.content:
What did Lenin think about democracy? How does this pertain to the modern socialist framework of democratic centralism?

Today, in part 3 of our Democratic Centralism series, we will explore the contribution that Lenin made in formulating the philosophy of democratic centralism and how it was practiced in the USSR!

entry.title: [LOI] A Vietnam War Movie from North Vietnam | Interview with DSA International Committee
entry.summary:
In this interview with the DSA International Committee, Luna Nguyen introduces "The Little Girl of Hanoi," a remarkable Vietnamese film shot just 6 months after the Christmas bombing of Hanoi. Luna also discusses the Vietnamese perspective of the war and how Vietnamese feel today about American Vietnam veterans and the state of the USA empire in our modern era from a Vietnamese perspective.
Editing and End Credit Song by Noam Bergman: https://www.youtube.com/hilightnotes
Watch the full interview, which includes the full unedited film here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pBsloYK6sqI
The Worldview and Philosophical Methodology of Marxism-Leninism book: https://www.banyanhouse.org/shop
